### digital — 2015-06-26T03:26:38+0300

Can't wait to see Quantlet get released. Would be awesome to bio-hack it's use here in New York City. Likely the Quantlet will operating in multiple wavelengths between the 600 and 900nm range.

As an experiment here (larger unit compared to the Quantlet), we are using multiple tunable lasers in this band, testing out what configuration works best: specific wavelengths, nm width and power strength; and then comparing to a wide wavelength source.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3799027/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3799027/)
It is well known that wavelength of approximately 630 nm represents the biostimulatory region of visible light and promotes cell proliferation, DNA synthesis, and cell adhesion.5,6) These phenomena are thought to be induced by the absorption of light by cytochrome c oxidase present in the mitochondria, which has an absorption band ranging from 600 to 1100 nm.7) Cytochrome c oxidase is considered a primary photo-acceptor in low-level laser therapy (LLLT).8) Thus, the 630-nm light used in PDT corresponds to the wavelength of light used in LLLT may influence on the photodynamic effect required for killing cancer cells.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-04T19:26:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-170770>

Well.........Ruben wont let me speak freely...........but I can point and click you to places where the answer may lie:

So how do electrons and proteins interact? Light.
The dualism to the nature of light is best demonstrated by the photoelectric effect, where a "weak" UV light produces a current flow (by releases electrons) but a strong red light does not release electrons no matter how intense the red light. Red light then acts a type of cellular capacitor for energy. Where is it stored you ask? In cell membranes and in mitochondria.

Einstein explained that light exists in a particle-like state as packets of energy (quanta) called photons. The photoelectric effect occurs because the packets of energy carried by each individual red photons are too weak to knock the electrons off the atoms no matter how many red photons you beamed onto the cathode (metal). But the individual UV photons were each strong enough to release the electron and cause a current flow. It appears that life needs a lot of UV light to work properly at low energy parts of the electromagnetic spectrum because life's job is to move electrons to and fro to alter the redox chemistry of proteins with those electrons.

In many diseases when light signaling or electron flow is off...........disease manifests. One of the things people with disease rarely get told is that they should be highly charged with UV light. This means that an equatorial existence is one of the things that may help them tremendously avoid the turn on switch of many different diseases.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-04T19:32:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-170771>

light in many diseases has another more complicated effect that no one is connecting to biology as far as I know. My members have been getting that juice for a long time in webinars and Q & A's but maybe they don't know it? The Quantum Hall effect tied to electron spin. I covered that in some webinars about topologic insulators.

Abnormal light signals cause protein misfolding disorders. Many of you have said you read every blog? Well have you assimilated that stuff? OSF 3-5 has this very cool light based mechanism built in them....many diseases have a specific mito or nuclear protein misfolding pointing the gun inside or outside the nucleus where the target, namely DNA or mitochondrial DNA are located.

For example in 1997 discovery that mHtt fragments [misfold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein_folding) proteins led to the discovery of the [nuclear inclusions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inclusion_bodies) they cause in DNA to alter its expression in huntingtons disease.

What electrifies and magnifies DNA can also cause disease, and this is something that modern science still does not get to optimize biologic performance. Light does this to DNA directly. Artificial light is a real problem when you understand the quantum Hall effect of light. And when you have the HD gene it can actually kill you later in life as the protein mis folds more as the redox drops in neurons.

A very good example of two edges of the sword are as follows: this is how the phosphorescent coating that you find inside fluorescent lights works too. These coatings reflect visible light very efficiently, hence they appear white to our eyes. But they absorb UV light and re-emit it as visible light; this is essentially what happens when we turn this type of light on. Like fluorescent lights, nucleic acid proteins also have a built in specificity. That specificity is quantized in nature. Nucleic acids only code for proteins, and it appears they had to innovate proteins capable of absorbing light from the spectrum of sunlight at frequencies 3000 nm or less, while also making sure the emission gap of the protein always released light in the UV range for signaling. In HD that is broken when bad frequencies of light alter that defective gene over decades.

Read up on John Ott and Sarasota experiment with ADHD.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-04T19:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-170778>

The late stage of severe niacin deficiency is known as pellagra. Early records of pellagra followed the widespread cultivation of corn in Europe in the 1700's. The disease is generally associated with poorer social classes whose chief dietary staple consisted of cereals like corn or sorghum. Pellagra was also common in the southern United States during the early 1900s where income was low and corn products were a major dietary staple. Interestingly, pellagra was not known in Mexico, where corn was also an important dietary staple and much of the population was also poor. **_Mexico is closer to the equator where Vitamin D is more abundant because of the presence of UV light in solar radiation compared to the USA._**
In fact, corn contains appreciable amounts of niacin, but it is present in a bound form that is not nutritionally available to humans. The traditional preparation of corn tortillas in Mexico involves soaking the corn in a lime (calcium oxide) solution, prior to cooking. Heating the corn in an [alkaline](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#alkaline) solution results in the release of bound niacin, increasing its[bioavailability](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#bioavailability).

Might Pellagra be a disease of acute deficiency of UV light?

Consider that the most common symptoms of niacin deficiency involve the _skin (vitamin D)_, the _digestive system (think yew)_, and the nervous system (altered Vitamin A cycle). The symptoms of pellagra are commonly referred to as the three D's: [dermatitis](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#dermatitis), diarrhea, and [dementia](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#dementia). A fourth D, death, occurs if pellagra is left untreated. Lacking UV light in cells might be a big deal huh? So what might happen when you start wearing clothes, stay out of the sun and say bake yourself in artificial light instead of nude sunbathing while you eat tons of GMO corn?

In the skin, a thick, scaly, darkly pigmented rash develops symmetrically in areas **_exposed to sunlight._** In fact, the word "pellagra" comes from "_pelle agra_," the Italian phrase for rough or raw skin. Symptoms related to the digestive system include inflammation of the mouth and tongue ("bright red tongue"), vomiting, constipation, abdominal pain, and ultimately, diarrhea.

Interesting huh? Remember every living cell needs to release ELF -UV radiations to signal properly..........can you do that in pellagra? Nope.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-04T19:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-170780>

The key point that you should be kept coming back too when you look at all my blogs on the eye clock is the construction of it from the fundamental thermodynamic givens that were present on Earth before the evolution of DHA 600 million yrs ago. No one has thought about that have they? I have. So how was any "eye clock" built before DHA was present?

Light and water were the cornerstones of its design. Water absorbs light best at 3000 nm to make and exclusion zone battery (EZ), but at 3000 nm it is associated with a lot of heat generation. Heat, but specifically temperature rise is bad for energy efficiency. This would have made the clock very innacurate. So 3000 nm might be ideal for water molecules, but that frequency was not optimized for cellular signaling when the clock was built. It seems cells would have the needed to step down the solar power from this high IR range, using intermediate forms of atoms, we called proteins. Evolution did this by using specific arrays or lattices of atoms to modulate the light's frequency back to 650 and 850nm of IR light. By stepping down the power and heat another benefit manifested. IR light reflects too easy, and lower frequency light like UV light, does not reflect as easy. Reflected light is a poor signaling choice. The lowest frequency of light that could make a large EZ in water was 270 nm light in the UV range. Pollack's experiments have shown that EZ water absorbs well in the 270nm range. In this way, water acts like a broadband dielectric for absorbing UV light. This 270nm frequency in water begins to absorb UV light and begins to make a very large EZ or battery in a cell. This frequency of light is not reflected and can be used for signaling between cells. All bio-photons emitted from cells have been shown to be in the UV range of light so far in experiment. The emission of UV light from a cell seems to have been predetermined by what was on Earth before cells formed. It also appears water and light's interaction were the two things that set these thermodynamic parameters in photochemistry. These physical characteristics of water and light's interaction is what set the precise and quantized evolution of proteins, in my opinion.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-19T08:02:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-179848>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170778)
>
> The late stage of severe niacin deficiency is known as pellagra. Early records of pellagra followed the widespread cultivation of corn in Europe in the 1700's. The disease is generally associated with poorer social classes whose chief dietary staple consisted of cereals like corn or sorghum. Pellagra was also common in the southern United States during the early 1900s where income was low and corn products were a major dietary staple. Interestingly, pellagra was not known in Mexico, where corn was also an important dietary staple and much of the population was also poor. **_Mexico is closer to the equator where Vitamin D is more abundant because of the presence of UV light in solar radiation compared to the USA._**
> In fact, corn contains appreciable amounts of niacin, but it is present in a bound form that is not nutritionally available to humans. The traditional preparation of corn tortillas in Mexico involves soaking the corn in a lime (calcium oxide) solution, prior to cooking. Heating the corn in an [alkaline](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#alkaline) solution results in the release of bound niacin, increasing its[bioavailability](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#bioavailability).
>
> Might Pellagra be a disease of acute deficiency of UV light?
>
> Consider that the most common symptoms of niacin deficiency involve the _skin (vitamin D)_, the _digestive system (think yew)_, and the nervous system (altered Vitamin A cycle). The symptoms of pellagra are commonly referred to as the three D's: [dermatitis](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#dermatitis), diarrhea, and [dementia](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/glossary#dementia). A fourth D, death, occurs if pellagra is left untreated. Lacking UV light in cells might be a big deal huh? So what might happen when you start wearing clothes, stay out of the sun and say bake yourself in artificial light instead of nude sunbathing while you eat tons of GMO corn?
>
> In the skin, a thick, scaly, darkly pigmented rash develops symmetrically in areas **_exposed to sunlight._** In fact, the word "pellagra" comes from "_pelle agra_," the Italian phrase for rough or raw skin. Symptoms related to the digestive system include inflammation of the mouth and tongue ("bright red tongue"), vomiting, constipation, abdominal pain, and ultimately, diarrhea.
>
> Interesting huh? Remember every living cell needs to release ELF -UV radiations to signal properly..........can you do that in pellagra? Nope.

I've heard that the term "red neck" actually comes from settlers in the southern states that did not use the lime when they grew corn. Their necks would become all red from the sun and they would be extremely moody.
I also heard something about an epidemic of pellagra in Italy which was treated with niacin. Everyone that they treated ended up also having Lyme spirochetes.(lack of UV possibly?)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-20T20:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-179986>

WANT TO WIN A QUANTLET?

We have been amazed, honored and humbled by the messages of support and interest we've received so far. Some have been very creative and others simply heart-touching. So, in the spirit of gratitude and giving back, we're going to put our money where our mouth is.

INTRODUCING THE 'I WANT MY QUANTLET' VIDEO MESSAGE CONTEST!

Here's how you can ENTER and WIN:

1\. Record a short (less than 15 seconds) video with your phone, webcam or camcorder explaining in your own words what the Quantlet does and how you intend to use it in your life.
2\. Email us the video to [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]).
3\. We will review and post it to our page for votes when deemed appropriate and after you complete a simple release document allowing us to promote your video.
4\. The video with the highest number of votes WINS and receives their Quantlet once BETA units ship early next year.

A few recommendations:

1\. Speak slowly and clearly, even if you're excited.
2\. Keep it simple, short, sweet and appropriate.
3\. Have fun!

We'll announce the winner prior to the Indiegogo campaign.

Good luck!!!!

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-23T07:23:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-187149>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=187147)
>
> I believe so but I wonder how our liver will feel about that. Hopefully the UV will power up the liver to clear our all that carnage

UV blood irradiation seems to be nothing but sunshine and smiles:

"
UVP has been shown to:

-   **Energize or enhance the biochemical and physiological defenses** of the body by the introduction of ultraviolet energy into the blood stream. UVP does not and can not kill every pathogen in your body directly. If that were the case, you would have to irradiate all of the blood in your system and every inch of your body. Rather UVBI transmits energy to the blood that empowers a strong response in the body. White blood cells, if they are out of control, begin to stabilize. UVP increases cell membrane permeability which enhances the body's ability to produce antibodies and marks pathogenic microorganisms for destruction by your immune system. The debris from dead pathogens stimulates the immune response further against even the most stubborn (antibiotic resistant) bacteria or viruses.

-   Rapidly **detoxify and relieve toxemia. UVP also helps the liver** clear itself of fats.

-   **Increase venous blood oxygen in patients with depressed blood oxygen values. In fact, one of the effects noted by s**ome is a "flushing" in the face and possibly a slight rise in temperature. Oxygen in the blood has a powerful effect, helping to eradicate not only pathogens, but it also creates an environment in which yeasts, fungi, and cancer cells cannot exist. It is generally believed that cancer requires an anaerobic (oxygen free) environment to grow."

[http://www.drkaslow.com/html/uvbi.html](http://www.drkaslow.com/html/uvbi.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-02T19:36:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.15839/post-187958>

We are down to 14 hours left on the beta units and they are now officially flying off the shelves. Are you in yet? Are you ready to rewind your clock to capture more light? [http://ow.ly/XQVFQ](http://ow.ly/XQVFQ)

This maybe the last time these devices are offered this cheap. I hope people dig into the science and see it. As far as I can see, the Quantlet team has surrounded themselves with well-known, world-class experts on the Quantlet's subject matter. These aren't people kind Facebook claiming to know better. These are world-class scientists and engineers! It is highly improbable that these opinion leaders would put anything but the best science and engineering behind the products and companies they support/advise. In addition, the Quantlet team is pretty clear on how they plan to demonstrate their results. They will have around 500 beta units out in the field with longitudinal tracking of labs to be done by a third party to be performed as part of these beta testers' experience. While it's not a clinical trial per se, it is bold and tells me enough about what they believe this product can do.

# Thread: Jens Optimization Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenRN — 2015-06-27T05:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169698>

Hello all, I am Jennifer and I am here to use the Epi-Paleo approach to sort out some puzzling health challenges that my doctors have not been able to. I am 45, married without kids, a registered nurse and student. I live a high stress lifestyle in an urban oasis of nnEMF which I now know has played a role in my situation.

So heres my story.....

Ten years ago I moved to Los Angeles and within three months I gained 40 lbs, developed moderate hypertension (average 145/95), pre diabetes (102 fasting) & hyperthyroidism (TSH 0.2) and starting re-growing uterine fibroids that had been removed 15 years prior ( they are now the size of 16 wk pregnancy).

Before all this I lived in a low nnEMF cold environment with no health problems for 12 years. Well, actually I got very sick when I moved there with stomach flu but that was it.

I am sick and tired of no doctor being able to explain why this is happening to me or how to stop it- I expect better and believe I have found it here. Its even hard for me to believe I've spent ten whole years of my life feeling half dead when I've been such an active and healthy person.

So after lurking around here I have made the following recent changes in preparation for a Leptin Reset and healthier lifestyle.

1.  Whole house Schumann generator during daylight hours started 2 weeks ago
2.  North oriented sleeping pad every night, started a month ago
3.  Blue Blockers after dark several times a week, started a month ago
4.  Quit a very stressful position a few months ago. I have a more manageable one now
5.  Eating wild caught sardines within ten minutes of rising out in the backyard on the grass, barefoot for three weeks now. However there are power lines back there and a smart meter
6.  Cooking with olive oil only and a spoonful of coconut oil occasionally for the last year. I utterly hate the taste.
7.  Face dunking 2-3 times a week is the best I can do at CT at the moment. I hate being cold!
8.  Reverse Osmosis water, approx. 3 cups a day (huge improvement from 0-1 cup tap water). I cook with it as well and have been doing so for a month.

Thats the good, now here is the bad

1.  I still sleep with my cell phone on and within reach. I am paranoid work might call with a medical emergency
2.  Energy is a huge struggle for me. I fall asleep on the couch in front of the TV several times a week. Yes its on......
3.  I'm not sure why but I'm scared to turn off the electricity to the house at night. I keep thinking what if an earth quake happens or WWIII breaks out.....
4.  I can't CT yet because it makes me really cold. (!!)

**Symptoms:**

-   Constant fatigue. Easy to sleep but only for a few hours.
-   Average 5 hours of sleep
-   Mild short term memory loss
-   Only can lose weight on Hcg. Mostly subcutaneous fat on thighs, back and abdomen.
-   Cellulite on inner thighs
-   Bulging eyes occasionally
-   80% loss of body hair
-   Joint pain in the fingers
-   eczema occasionally on chest and neck only
-   I crave carbs and have hypoglycemic attacks if I can’t get any. Not to mention the severe hunger pangs that start at 5 pm. This is a daily struggle and the best I’ve done so far is low carb tortillas. I am averaging 80-100 grams a day down from 200 or so.

Diet:

**Morning Supplements:** Magnesium malate & glycinate 200mg total, Magnesium transdermal, vitamin D3 100k, 2% Active iodine tincture 4 mg, K4 1500 mcg, K7 400mcg, Vit A 100,000 iu, selenomethionine 600 mcg and a generic prenatal.

**Breakfast:** Fish, eggs fried in coconut oil with tomatoes and string beans. Occasionally steel cut oatmeal, cooked not microwaved

**Lunch:** Home made salad with crab meat/sardines/ uncured bacon and olive oil. Occasionally cottage cheese or cheese and quinoa.

**Dinner:** steak, meatballs, chicken and quinoa with veggies and lemon juice.

Snack: I know I am not supposed to but if I feel I’m going to die of starvation I will have a few low carb tortillas with bacon and or cream cheese or pork rinds (Pork Clouds brand is divine).

**Family History:**

Grandfather (Paternal) - Deceased at 92, slight hypertension. Life long tobacco chewer

Grandmother (Paternal) - Deceased at 80, no known diseases

Grandmother (Maternal) - Deceased at 74 from bed sores, probably due to untreated diabetes

Grandfather (Maternal) - Deceased at 72, stroke

Father - Healthy, age 76. An extremely angry man, probably has cardiac issues

Mother - Age 73, Hypothyroidism after lifelong hyperthyroidism, recent history of DVTs and arthritis.

Sister 1- age 38, chronic pain, anorexia and MS.

Sister 2- age 37, uterine fibroids, hypertension and obesity.

Sister 3- Deceased at age 21, due to thyroid storm.

Medications:

I am on metformin, propranolol, and now amplodipine. I have been on methimazole and T3 for the thyroid but stopped on my own. MD refused cycloset but I will keep trying. I have routine labs in about a week.

Exercise: Yoga videos 1-2 times a week, treadmill walks 1 mile or less once or twice a week and FIR sauna 110 F for 30 minutes, three to four times a week. I have done all this for the last 5 or 6 years.

I feel 20% better on these improvements so far. I start the Leptin Reset on July 1st. I will be posting here to keep motivated and accountable . I am open to any feedback, suggestions and thoughts. I will share results and what I’m learning as openly as I can so please feel free to ask! Thank you in advance!

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-27T08:23:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169718>

Hi Jen and welcome! Great journal start!

One thing that jumped out at me is that with your high nnEMF exposure and low water intake, dehydration and calcium efflux are likely to be an issue. I have access to an FIR but wouldn't use it without an intake of at least 4 litres of water a day (my context amount, yours may be different). I ask myself am I helping to make a good exclusion zone in a well hydrated body (the benefits outweighing any emf exposure from the FIR), or is what I am doing just heating and causing more inflammation in already dehydrated cells? Might be worth focusing on water intake ......?

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-27T08:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169721>

Hi Jens,

I think you still have too much carbs in for Leptin reset. Quinoa has 63,9 per 100 grams. I would take that out (also ditch oatmeal). It is still a grain. If carbs are out, after a while you don't need them so badly. The veggies are enough. Eat more fat and protein during break fast and lunch until stuffed (verge of over eating). Maybe it is better to wait for autumn to do Leptin reset. Because during summer we are meant to eat more carbs (from fruit and vegs, not grains). Have you read everything about Leptin in the blogs? What kind of fish are you eating?

I would also ditch the synthetic vit D and replace with natural sunlight (between 8 and 11 AM and 4 and 7 PM). You need lots of it because you have darker skin. Find away to get sun tan time in your schedule. Also after waking up, look at the sun first before anything else, to let your brain know the day has started. Natural sun light will help you with eczema. (but you have to cut back tv and other artificial light too)

Yes indeed what Sue says, drink more water... a lot more.

If you can't do full body ct, do spot ct. Of course face dunking, but for instance if your fingers are sore, put them in ice water for a couple of minutes and try longer every time.

Try to have dinner at 5 PM instead of snacking if hunger pangs are a problem for you then. Don't ever eat dinner later then 4 hours before bed.

And keep with it! Read all you can here and ask questions so you can also fix your environment in the long run. Persistence and patience is key.

### caroline — 2015-06-27T10:13:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169726>

Hi Jen and welcome ....

Why blueblockers only a couple of times a week????

Your day needs to look like this ....

upon rising .... go outside with little clothes on

Have a BAB within 30 min of rising ....at least 50g of protein and lots of fat

no snacking and leave four hours between meals and before bed

spring or R/O water

lots of outdoor time....grounding

### Lahelada — 2015-06-27T13:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169732>

Good morning Jen, welcome!
Nice start to a journal and good advice that you have been given so far . I would like to address the point 1- 4 you mentioned.They all seem to be related to fear somewhat. So wean yourself off in stages.

1\. The cell phone ? Being an old fogey at 50 I remember hardwired phones . Could you get an extension into the bedroom , an informal one a long extension cord really with the most basic phone you can find.No displays,nothing. If you can only give one telephone number at night forward the call to your homephone.That way you can keep the cellphone in the furthest place from you in the house.

2\. Reading by red light is a much better way to fall asleep.If it must be the TV,blue blockers and put it on sleep timer.

3\. For an EQ you need emergency lamps anyway. If it were a bad one you would likely lose electricity anyway. WW3 you will get fair warning of. Cable news will live off the potential,the probabilities,the potential aftermath for weeks .  When you hear rumblings of that you may put the breaker in your bedroom back on but only then ..

4\. Yup . So ? I know I am being harsh but hopefully also funny . CT is not easy for most people . Face dunking in cold,really cold water is a very good step in the right direction.
Another thought having visited LA for all of 3 days - The ocean ...daily visits with full immersion in the sea would be great,walking along the beach barefoot would be good too.

### JenRN — 2015-06-29T17:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-169880>

Sue thank you for the reply. I think thats a really good point about dehydration. I have always hated drinking water but thankfully I also hate coffee, soft drinks, milk etc. LOL. After my CT scan last month showed arterial calcified plaque, I came here and learned about dehydration and calcium efflux. This has really scared me into buying RO water 5 gal container and starting the magnesium. I’m probably getting more than the 4 cups I said and should keep count. This week I pick up 10 gals and will continue upping it weekly. I keep a pitcher in the fridge and another on the counter and some bottle in the back seat because I forget so easily-its got to be in view.

Anyway I do wonder about the sauna and EMF/dehydration. I got an used one off CL and have used it for 5 years. The previous owner claimed it was low emf in her sales pitch but that was above my head at the time. I got it to detox solvents from chem class out of my fat but in the last year it became a sleep aide. Some of my best deepest sleep has been in there.

However at least for now I will leave it alone. Not only do I need to measure emf myself but its a mote point when I’m this dehydrated. A question for you- can you sense when you are hydrated? Do you have a subjective sense of well being or such? I ask because I can not tell besides clear urine if I am- I have no thirst mechanism and can’t tell the difference.

### JenRN — 2015-06-29T17:39:03+0300

Lahelada,

I spent a day musing over over your suggestion and I’ve decided to look into a emf protection sleeve instead since I use my phone for an alarm as well.

I will pick up some red light bulbs this week . I was hoping to not use them and just wear blue blockers but I think skin absorption plays a role too. The husband will be delighted with the red district theme .....

You are absolutely right about emergency measures and the news...Fox news channel will have the preview and battle field fashion interviews for ww3 going 24/7!

I’m actually correctly prepared for a major disaster ( been through the Northridge quake and wont be caught unprepared ever again!) Its just a mind set I need to adapt of being “off grid”. I live in a decent area but I’ve brought into the whole house automation thing- security cameras inside and out, remote every thing so I can control my robot house from any where. I hate giving up this “convenience” but I will start with the bedroom only on Wednesday. I might need extra magnesium for the anxiety but I will do it.

As for the beach..I’m twenty minutes away technically but add an extra 40 for traffic and 20 for parking. I’m not sure if you got to visit Venice or Santa Monica-but thats close to me and have a few too many syringes in the sand to go barefoot. I do walk barefoot on concrete though a lot in the city where ever I can. I wish the beach could be daily ritual but its a weekend thing mostly. I have to get my butt in the tub and not complain.

I would love any suggestions on making CT easier or any thing else you think of. And thank you!

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-30T07:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-170024>

> [JenRN said:](/goto/post?id=169880)
>
> A question for you- can you sense when you are hydrated? Do you have a subjective sense of well being or such? I ask because I can not tell besides clear urine if I am- I have no thirst mechanism and can’t tell the difference.

Jen I found that because I had a bad thirst mechanism I just had to drink a set amount each day, whether I was thirsty or not. Treated it like medicine, regular doses throughout the day. I eventually started to get the thirst mechanism back. I now drink when I get up, and I usually CT in the morning and drink a litre then, so have usually drunk 2 litres by 9 a.m. I drink a lot late afternoon/early evening. It's almost as if I go to the watering hole twice a day for my main intake . I drink more as i'm preparing meals, and then have smaller amounts as and when the rest of the time, when I'm thirsty. Sometimes I get caught out and don't have water with me, but its pretty rare, and its acute, not chronic ..... At the moment I can't sense too well when I'm properly hydrated, but I can sense when I'm not.

With the sauna, (and even sunbathing) I now think of it like the tides, only use it if the tide is in...... if the tide is out, I'm baking the beach even if it "feels" nice at the time...

### shah78 — 2015-07-02T18:53:59+0300

### JenRN — 2015-07-02T23:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimization-journal.15893/post-170413>

You guys are a riot! Thank you for your responses and humor, its uplifting! I have just a few minutes before I am back to work but really wanted to check in.
First I will say the Sun did survive without help from my electrohouse. However, what a very strange night for me. I was asleep by 9:30ish in an unplugged house. My husband came home around midnight and switched the power back so he could watch TV in the living room. I have no memory of this but he says I got up, went to water my garden and was talking to the plants out loud before I went back to bed.

Now what I do recall is _dreaming_ about "heart talking " to my grape vines which aren't doing so good and placing my hands on them to heal them.

This is something I have done in real life but not since moving here. Its a very subtle energy exchange and I haven't been centered or healthy enough to sense it. Plus plants here generally feel dead and empty.

So for me to be talking to them or dreaming about them them is rather nice, maybe a preview to recovery....

However, I woke in pain all over again and in a terrible mood. I think its because I got lulled into a nice sleep and this changed when the power went on. Anyway thats my best guess. And I was thirsty, not hungry. I had beef meatballs fried in duck fat with onions and 2 glasses cold water for breakfast.

I have hope I don't get ravenous at five. I can't eat before then. I have coconut oil packets and pork rinds in the car if I do.
Hopefully I can get home in a few hours but I may have to do a night shift. If so I'm in blue light hell again........

I will check in later. Enjoy the sunshine!

ps TV stays. It would be the end of the world here if there couldn't be. One broke in the living room and hubby got a new bigger one delivered 3 hours later. It was truly an emergency situation the day that happened. However we're down to two from five and I got rid of the one in the bedroom years ago.
If I had it my way we would only have one for playing my workout videos and Extant watch parties......which hardly happens .....

Lunch red meat and 6 gram carb tortilla
Dinner at 6:30 grilled lamb kabob, lettuce , cucumber and tomatoes- at a restaurant ( forgot my packed dinner at home on the counter)
no hunger but lots of anxiety and insecurity, easily frustrated all day while I'm out.
Home at 8 sat in driveway and out of nowhere hungry.
Go inside and immediately lose the hunger pain.

Working late on computer tonight without blue blocker which I lost out today. Feeling irritable plus all electricity on.
Sitting with ice pack on back is helping .

Going to bed in next thirty minutes, feeling very disoriented-will turn off wifi but not electricity since hubby still needs it for next few hours.

Honestly, I think I'm less hungry possibly from all the red meat but feeling irritable due to no fish .i feel calmer eating seafood.

# Thread: child suffering from stuttering/stammering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sami — 2015-06-27T18:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/post-169738>

Hi Dr Jack,
I have a friend whose 4 yr old child stutters or stammers, is this due neurological disorder that governs the speech or something else, what could be the remedy for such disorders. kindly advice
warm regards
Sami

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-27T22:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/post-169739>

AFAIK, Jack's away on the Kruse Cruise at the moment. Maybe he'll see this when he comes back.

I don't know whether stammering can be down to a neurological disorder, but I do know that for some people Alexander Technique can be helpful, which suggests that in at any rate some cases the problem is down to unhelpful muscular tension. That would also be consistent with its coming and going with mood, nervousness and so on, which does seem to be true for some people.

There's an interesting note on that here:

> The Alexander Technique teacher did not attempt to work on my speech problem directly, as the technique deals with the use of the whole person. Using manual guidance and verbal instructions he helped me to become aware of the long held muscle tension throughout my body. With his skilled hands and patient instructions, he encouraged me to let go some of this tension, so I could move with more freedom and less effort.
>
> As the lessons continued he would point out how quick and automatic my reactions to any stimulus had become. This was particularly noticeable to him when I attempted to speak. With his help I gradually became aware that when I attempted to talk I would revert to a habitual pattern of tightening in the chest, legs, feet etc., my lips tongue and jaw were tense and held, my breathing shallow and restricted. I would try and force out the words using great effort and strain in my whole body. The teacher pointed out to me that if I could stop this habitual pattern of interference throughout my body and allow the correct use of the tongue lips and vocal organs my fluency would improve. ...

[http://www.stammering.org/speaking-out/article/alexander-technique](http://www.stammering.org/speaking-out/article/alexander-technique)

The Alexander people say - with reference to most things - that if you get out of your own way, "the right thing does itself".

Also see this:

[http://www.sarahchatwin.com/life-the-alexander-technique/not-just-the-kings-speech/](http://www.sarahchatwin.com/life-the-alexander-technique/not-just-the-kings-speech/)

That makes the point well that from this way of looking at it the fix is not to approach a specific problem with a specific solution - exercises for the jaw or something - but to look at overall patterns of tension. They're looking at the person as a whole system. What tends to happen - with most of us actually - is that when we go to do something, before we even do it, we engage habitual patterns of tension. It's a way of "getting set" that we tend to do automatically without even being aware that we're doing it. If these patterns are particularly dysfunctional you can get problems like RSI and frozen shoulders when it's an activity that you do a lot. (Or you can get something like stammering.) There are a heck of a lot of musicians, for example, who have to stop playing, because they can't do it without severe pain. This is one reason why Alexander Technique is taught at some schools of music like the Royal College of Music:

[http://www.rcm.ac.uk/life/studentservices/alexandertechnique/](http://www.rcm.ac.uk/life/studentservices/alexandertechnique/)

If this were my child I'd take him or her to an Alexander Teacher and get them to have a look and assess what's going on with him or her.

If your friend's in the U.S. and interested in looking into that the place to locate one is through AmSAT

[http://www.amsatonline.org/teachers](http://www.amsatonline.org/teachers)

I hope that might be of some interest and help. As I say, unfortunately Jack is away at present.

### Sami — 2015-06-28T02:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/post-169753>

Hi Gagnrad,
thanks for the leads, my friend is from Bangalore , india .
I was wondering if this condition is related to EMF being more in the environment. I read one of the blogs \[[https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-7-intro-to-your-gut-microbiome](https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-7-intro-to-your-gut-microbiome)\]from jack kruse where one of the reader mentioned that green beans may cause this condition.
Cheers
Sami

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-28T11:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/post-169762>

> [Sami said:](/goto/post?id=169753)
>
> Hi Gagnrad,
> thanks for the leads, my friend is from Bangalore , india .

Hi! I'm in the UK myself. I thought you might be U.S.-based. Alexander Technique was started over here in the UK (by an Australian) in late Victorian times but now has a fair penetration in the U.S. But I don't know if it's much used in India. I guess it's a kind of "mind-body" technique and India already has it's own systems like that - Hatha Yoga of course - so perhaps not. Actually, I wonder what Yoga people would say about a problem like that - much the same, I'd guess. It's a kind of mal-coordination, and someone who's very attuned to the body and movement can probably see it going on in real-time - and even feel some of the tensions in the other person with his or her hands.

> I was wondering if this condition is related to EMF being more in the environment. I read one of the blogs \[[https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-7-intro-to-your-gut-microbiome](https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-7-intro-to-your-gut-microbiome)\]from jack kruse where one of the reader mentioned that green beans may cause this condition.

As regards causes I don't know. As I say, I think it's unhelpful patterns of tensions that are the problem and those tend to be system-wide not isolated in any particular part of the body. It's a bit like coins in a stack: if you displace one, all the others move to compensate. Those patterns can be undone, but it's not always easy, although I understand it tends to be very much easier with children. But what _causes_ those patterns in the first place is another matter. It might be, for example, a traumatic incident - physical or perhaps even emotional. That's clearly so in some cases: an example would be a limp that stays with someone after his broken leg has healed. There's a brilliant account of that from his own experience by Oliver Sacks, a professor of neurology at the NYU School of Medicine:

[http://www.amazon.com/Leg-Stand-On-Oliver-Sacks/dp/0330507621](http://www.amazon.com/Leg-Stand-On-Oliver-Sacks/dp/0330507621)

In many cases, though, you may never know where and when a problem originated. Fortunately, you don't need to.

I think what might _affect_ these things is yet another matter again. Almost anything, I'd suppose. I mean I guess we should have left behind the deterministic view - the era in which many people supposed that almost any kind of medical problem was innate and determined by genetics. So if we're going to take the environment and how it affects us seriously again, then I suppose many different factors might put someone under strain, or ease it, and make existing problems either better or worse.

But I'm not a doctor and that's just an amateur attempt at an answer.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-28T16:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-suffering-from-stuttering-stammering.15917/post-169772>

Sami,
welcome . Removing green beans and other goitrogenics may help but the issue is that these foods should not be a problem and would not be if the environment was right for the child. I will paraphrase Dr Kruse when he says that no human being should be considered innately impaired . Get your friend to look at circadian rhythm for example that the child goes to bed just after dark,does not watch TV before bed, gets up at first light . He should consider changing the apartment lighting to yellow lighting as this will help with sleep.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-29T20:34:09+0300

### Bill1 — 2015-06-29T05:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-research-is-flawed.15939/post-169829>

**Recent finding discover about half of all research is flawed.**

Dr. Marcia Angell, physician and longtime editor in chief of the _New England Medical Journal_, feels that objective research has taken a turn for the worse:
_“It is simply no longer possible to believe much of the clinical research that is published, or to rely on the judgment of trusted physicians or authoritative medical guidelines. I take no pleasure in this conclusion, which I reached slowly and reluctantly over my two decades as an editor of the New England Journal of Medicine"._

[http://wakeup-world.com/2015/06/28/...half-research-based-literature-simply-untrue/](http://wakeup-world.com/2015/06/28/leading-scientists-believe-half-research-based-literature-simply-untrue/)

# Thread: St. Helena Bay Skeleton from Southern Africa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/st-helena-bay-skeleton-from-southern-africa.15944/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-29T07:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/st-helena-bay-skeleton-from-southern-africa.15944/post-169840>

> What can DNA from the skeleton of a man who lived 2,330 years ago in the southernmost tip of Africa tell us about ourselves as humans? A great deal when his DNA profile is one of the ‘earliest diverged’ – oldest in genetic terms – found to-date in a region where modern humans are believed to have originated roughly 200,000 years ago. ...

I thought people would enjoy this bit:

> The complete 1.5 metre tall skeleton was examined by Professor Alan Morris, from the University of Cape Town. A biological anthropologist, Morris showed that the man was a ‘marine forager’. **A bony growth in his ear canal, known as ‘surfer’s ear’, suggested that he spent some time diving for food in the cold coastal waters, while shells carbon-dated to the same period, and found near his grave, confirmed his seafood diet.** Osteoarthritis and tooth wear placed him in his fifties.

[http://www.garvan.org.au/news-event...m-southern-africa-throws-light-on-our-origins](http://www.garvan.org.au/news-events/news/ancient-human-genome-from-southern-africa-throws-light-on-our-origins)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-01T02:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/st-helena-bay-skeleton-from-southern-africa.15944/post-170138>

Marine = brain. Simple.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-29T08:55:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs-would-not-let-his-own-kids-use-ipads.15951/post-169848>

> New York Times reporter Nick Bilton said he once assumingly asked Jobs, “So your kids must love the iPad?”
>
> Jobs responded:
>
> “They haven’t used it. We limit how much technology our kids use at home.”

This is not unusual in Silicon Valley the article goes on to suggest:

> “My kids accuse me and my wife of being fascists and overly concerned about tech, and they say that none of their friends have the same rules \[says Chris Anderson, CEO of 3D Robotics\]… That’s because we have seen the dangers of technology firsthand. I’ve seen it in myself, I don’t want to see that happen to my kids.”

The article actually claims:

> in Silicon Valley, there is actually a trend of tech execs and engineers \[shielding\] their kids from technology. They even send their kids to non-tech schools like the Waldorf School in Los Altos, where computers aren’t found anywhere because they only focus on hands-on learning

Waldorf Schools is the U.S. name for Steiner Schools.

[http://nextshark.com/why-steve-jobs-didnt-let-his-kids-use-ipads-and-why-you-shouldnt-either/](http://nextshark.com/why-steve-jobs-didnt-let-his-kids-use-ipads-and-why-you-shouldnt-either/)

Very interesting. Makes you wonder if the senior management in the processed food industry breakfasts off bacon and eggs. I'd not be surprised.

Would you be surprised to hear of pesticide tycoons eating organic food - or even perhaps biodynamic food (Rudolf Steiner connection again). I don't think I would be.

And do pharmaceutical company executives avoid their own drugs - or at least the ones they must know are useless and even harmful, such as statins. I don't know, but, again, I'd not be surprised. Would anyone else?

On a similar note, did you know Jeff Koons has a classical style house filled with _real_ art?

And [Richard Rodgers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Rogers) - who has done so much to make city landscapes so unpleasant for the ordinary person in Britain - lives in a Georgian house in a lovely English village where every step towards "development" is fiercely resisted.

I'm not saying there aren't uses for tech (or pharmaceuticals, or many of the rest of these things) but the public oughtn't to buy what people are selling so easily and use them so thoughtlessly. It makes me cringe when I see people hand toddlers mobile phones to play with.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-29T18:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs-would-not-let-his-own-kids-use-ipads.15951/post-169883>

This topic came up in the cruise with Rob's wife, Julie, a physician........she was surprised.

### Shijin13 — 2015-06-29T18:28:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs-would-not-let-his-own-kids-use-ipads.15951/post-169888>

we're the only parents in our group of friends who don't let our kids play on our phones/tablets. 5years ago - we didn't know any better. n ow we do... so we made the change for the better... kids don't need technology to learn to read, or to engage their brain. there's a marvelous world outside they can explore to learn from.

### thomas — 2015-06-29T22:18:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs-would-not-let-his-own-kids-use-ipads.15951/post-169961>

To sell to the masses you must give them what they want. Which is basically cheap crap that kill them. That's kinda why I don't see Jack Kruse 's site Catering to more than a small group of people (hopefully that can change, with a global change in conscioussness maybe..). But the smart people who think for themselves like execs make the right decisions both in terms of money(aka selling the right thing to the right and biggest markets) and personal choices(like the right health choices).
That's a contradiction that's important to understand.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-29T23:09:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs-would-not-let-his-own-kids-use-ipads.15951/post-169963>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=169961)
>
> To sell to the masses you must give them what they want. Which is basically cheap crap that kill them. That's kinda why I don't see Jack Kruse 's site Catering to more than a small group of people (hopefully that can change, with a global change in conscioussness maybe..). But the smart people who think for themselves like execs make the right decisions both in terms of money(aka selling the right thing to the right and biggest markets) and personal choices(like the right health choices).
> That's a contradiction that's important to understand.

Actually, it may surprise you to know I don't agree with that - though I think it sounds persuasive.

I'll explain what I was trying to do first off. I could have just posted the Silicon Valley story and left it at that - and that might have a been a better choice. It would have focused in more on the effect technology has on children, which is an important topic (and one I did link back to again at the end). But I just found it interesting to make a lot of linkages. I didn't do that to arouse outrage. Nor did I do it to suggest that the public is foolish. (It can be gullible, but isn't always and I think we agree there.)

I was more trying to show a pattern and to suggest people need to get more sceptical about what they're told. And, FWIW, I think I do see some movement.

I don't, of course, know that all these different groups don't swallow their own Koolaid. I doubt Silicon Valley people see technology as Koolaid - just as something that younger children should not spend too much time with. As the guy quoted there said: "we have seen the dangers of technology firsthand. _I’ve seen it in myself_". And actually I wouldn't be surprised if Big Pharma and Big Agra do believe at least some of their own propaganda. I'm sure they know they often tell lies, but I doubt they realise _quite_ how useless and harmful what they do often is. (My guess is they lie to themselves: see my sig on that.)

I have, however, seen the story of a scientist approaching a breakfast-cereal managing director to tell him urgently that the extrusion process for making their product deforms proteins in the cereal grains and makes them toxic. See this WAPF article on that topic:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/dirty-secrets-of-the-food-processing-industry/](http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/dirty-secrets-of-the-food-processing-industry/)

Apparently, the man cackled in a senile way and said something like: "This kind of stuff is all right for throwing at weddings, but if people will put it in their mouths …" I doubt he was eating it himself. What do you think?

The stories about Jeff Koons and Richard Rogers come from this fascinating lecture on Beauty and Desecration:

Both men know how to make money. But _they_ certainly _don't_ buy their own koolaid.

These are all interesting cases to think about.

Now to get to the nub of the matter. You say:

> the smart people who think for themselves like execs make the right decisions both in terms of money(aka selling the right thing to the right and biggest markets) and personal choices(like the right health choices).

This is where I have to part company with you. In the first part you're talking about someone wanting to, and knowing how to, make money - and only that. In the second part you're talking about people who make the "right" personal choices - and, I suppose, it's their not using their own products that you have in mind.

Well, you know this is where I have a problem. In both cases this is "looking out for number one". You don't have to. People can and do take part in commerce with wider and more admirable aims. And Steve Jobs actually would be an example here: he did - [unlike Michael Dell, for example](http://news.cnet.com/Dell-Apple-should-close-shop/2100-1001_3-203937.html) - take pride in what he was doing and was fanatical about the quality of the products. Moreover, there are plenty of conscientious employers who do care about their employees.

If one's aim in life is simply to make a lot of money, however much damage one does to other people, it's a pretty sad way to go on. I don't think it's "smart" at all. Or perhaps it is: perhaps "smart" is the right word - but it's not intelligent in a deeper sense. It's a failure to grasp what human life is about and how all our lives are wound together:

> No man is an Iland, intire of itselfe; every man
> is a peece of the Continent, a part of the maine;
> if a Clod bee washed away by the Sea, Europe
> is the lesse, as well as if a Promontorie were, as
> well as if a Manor of thy friends or of thine
> owne were; any mans death diminishes me,
> because I am involved in Mankinde;
> And therefore never send to know for whom
> the bell tolls; It tolls for thee.
>
> \--John Donne

In fact, the same goes not just for other people but for the whole of creation. And even if people can't feel anything for other people or for the creatures and land around them, they'll find it comes back on them eventually. This is why Paul Ehrlch says with respect with what we're doing to the environment “We are sawing off the limb that we are sitting on.”


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Thyroid Help

### Sami — 2015-06-29T10:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-help.15957/post-169855>

Hi Dr Jack,
My wife has been diagnosed for hypothyroidism for the past 7 years, the doctor whom we consult here in Bangalore is an endocrinologist and had prescribed 125mcg of thyronorm \[thyroxine\] tablets.
The initial tests had revealed TSH greater than 100, however antibodies were ruled out.
Recently I purchased your book EPI-Paleo RX, and understood that hypothyroidism may be reversed using cold thermo genesis. hence we started CT for 2 months and stopped the tablets. for some reason my wife couldn't continue on CT fully for the last 2 months , only used cold showers \[10 minutes\] for the past 2 months.
we noticed that she was gaining weight \[5ft 2in , 71 kgs\] and she had unexplained muscle pain and fatigue.
yesterday we did a test and the result was TSH=>100mcIU/mL, T4=3.92mcg/dL,T3=1.13ng/mL
kindly advice. Note when she did CT with full body immersed in cold water she lost 4 kilos in 3 weeks.
what other tests do we need to do? RT3 is not available in Bangalore.
Regards
Sami

### Spy — 2015-06-29T16:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-pretoria-south-africa.15965/post-169873>

Hi Everyone,

I live in Pretoria (South Africa) and have stumbled upon Jack's site via my research into Paleo.

I have start reading and implementing some of the protocols:

-   Not going to work before the sunrise anymore , respecting the lights cycles . Avoiding Blue light ...only try to watch some TV on Saturday's afternoon before sunset.
-   i am eating a lot more of fish and trying to eat oysters at least 3 / 4 times week. Sardines work quite well too. I try to eat my last meal 2/3 hours before i go to bed.
-   In general i sleep between 6- 8 hours per night but on avg 7.
-   CT is coming a along but i am not there yet hopefully soon i will be able to follow the protocol correctly to reap all the benefits . I always feel great and sleep well after a session and my inflammation also seems to be a lot less.
-   I currently stay just outside the city in metropolitan where i am exposed to EMF and all the things that are not good for me, My wife and i purchased a piece of ground bordering onto a nature reserve and although other houses are being built on the sides of ours at least no houses can be built behind because of the nature reserve so hopefully this will also contribute towards better health.

On weekends i like cycling on my MTB , always feels goods after spending some time in nature.

I have ankylosing spondylitis but after reading some of the blogs i have new hope , i am very fortuned i have no fusing in any part of my vertebra or on my SI joints which is normally the 1st area's that are effected with this disease . i am planning to get a Full Blood work done but it seems my CRP and ESR is way off because if the AS.

Thank you
Jaco

# Thread: Help me understand Magnetico vs. Earthing vs. PEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-magnetico-vs-earthing-vs-pemf.15967/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SpiritGuide — 2015-06-29T16:43:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-magnetico-vs-earthing-vs-pemf.15967/post-169877>

Hello good morning. This may sound like a very basic question to many of you, and if it's a dead horse topic please forgive me for being a newb.

I could use some help in figuring out what to do with regards to "recharging the battery."

About 2-3 years ago I read the book "Earthing, the most important health discovery ever" by Clint Ober... and I also read "Zapped, why your cell phone shouldn't be your alarm clock and 1268 ways to outsmart the hazards of electronic pollution" by Ann Louise Gittlemen.

Almost immediately after reading these books we purchased an Earthing sheet (half sheet) which we recently upgraded to the full King sheet and have been using Earthing ever since.

More recently I heard about PEMF to energize the cells using the natural magnetic frequencies of the Earth, but at an average of $3,000-$5,000 per unit the entry level price of a PEMF machine makes implementing this as a bio-hack cost-prohibitive for me without first knowing how effective it would be.

After listening to several of Jack Kruse's interviews on Youtube recently I'm very interested in the Magnetico, but again with an entry level price higher then Earthing, but lower then PEMF, I'd like to just find out more about it before investing $1,000 in a King-sized device.

So on the surface it seems to me as though the Magnetico is more of a PEMF-type device but I'm not quite sure.

Can anyone give me an honest run-down of Magnetico vs. PEMF vs. Earthing to help me make this decision?

I just have a couple of initial questions:

\- Is PEMF the same as a Magnetico?

\- Can I (or should I) use my Earthing Sheet in conjunction with the Magnetico?

Please if you're up to date on all of these technologies can you help me understand this better? Ultimately I just want to know if I should buy the Magnetico, and if so do I use it in conjunction with the Earthing or in place of it, and would this replace the need to get a PEMF device altogether?

Thanks!

### Lahelada — 2015-06-29T19:53:03+0300

### Zene Kamili — 2016-07-01T08:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-magnetico-vs-earthing-vs-pemf.15967/post-197658>

> [Limpei said:](/goto/post?id=195637)
>
> Hi all, question:
> I read the magnetico is essentially 10 gauss to 20 gauss magnets embedded in a sleep pad.
>
> Isn't it possible to DIY this by purchasing the corresponding magnets and sewing it into a sleep pad?

I was thinking this same question myself. About 10 months ago, I bought about 30- 12 to 15,000 gauss magnets and put them under my mattress evenly. I did this for about 2 or 3 month to test out the effects. I used a compass to find the negative sides of the magnets so that they can face up to project the magnetic field through me.

I began this in the late fall, (if that matters) in Korea where I live and it gets super cold. I live on the 3rd floor of a 4 story building that is in a rural area (if that matters as well).

My results:
I was doing CT concurrently with this (basically wearing shorts and minimal clothing all the time and going without the heat in my place). I was on a high fat diet full of seafood as per the Jack's recommendations.
My sleep was normal at first and I could feel the magnets sooth me as I slept at night. But, after about 6 weeks, I began to notice my energy feeling drained. After 2 months, I noticed my achillies tendon getting extremely sore and tender for the first time ever in such a manner. My feet and heel began to peel and have a burning sensation. I thought that it was the CT somehow and assumed from the CT blog's and Magnetico info that it was a toxin dump or something (5 months of constant ice baths in the late summer and fall and cold winter embracing of the winter-no ice baths) I had also been waking up with what felt like slight dehydrating headaches that my girlfriend mentioned noticing too when she came over to stay.

Long story short, I had to abandon the magnets from under my mattress and notice that even then that I was sensitive to them being anywhere near my bed. My feet and everything healed up immediately after discontinuing this.

I am super anxious to know what went wrong here. I figured that I need to learn more about magnetism and the practical use of magnets for healing first before I go back to try to hack this again. Does anyone know any reference on the pitfalls and proper ways to do this safely? I even wondered about the EMF being attract to my bed because of this and the spring mattress some kind of way or from living on the 3rd floor of my building.

More Context:
rural South Korea/Mid 30s/fit to muscular/goal to bring down blood sugar under 90 or even close to it/goal to increase mental functioning and a lethargic mind and mood/ at the time had abandoned exercise to try increase my charge (3 months)/ butter-seafood-leafy veggies-sweet potato- base diet/ first time ever trying CT (5 months then)

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-29T20:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-169929>

I think we may have done this before, but a long time ago. Anyway, here's one to kick off. Anyone else interested in posting any?

This is a fairly simple one, "The Buckle" from Walter de la Mare's _Songs of Childhood_. I don't think de la Mare is a great poet, but I do think that he has a substantial talent and that he does this kind of thing really well. There's a breath of enchantment in this, and perhaps that's something we should look for more in an age when too many people wish to disenchant the world—and wish children to be off to school at an early age where, it's hoped they will be "stretched". (A teacher said to me once that whenever she heard that expression she used to think of a child on a rack.)

The first verse isn't really anything child would do, but I think it establishes a kind of careless and joyful mood. And by the time you reach the last verse you have a sense of seeing the child and almost entering her world. Delightful—at least to an unserious person like me.

**THE BUCKLE**

I had a silver buckle,
I sewed it on my shoe,
And 'neath a sprig of mistletoe
I danced the evening through!

I had a bunch of cowslips,
I hid 'em in a grot,
In case the elves should come by night
And me remember not.

I had a yellow riband,
I tied it in my hair,
That, walking in the garden,
The birds might see it there.

I had a secret laughter,
I laughed it near the wall:
Only the ivy and the wind
May tell of it at all

[http://www.gutenberg.org/files/23545/23545-h/23545-h.htm](http://www.gutenberg.org/files/23545/23545-h/23545-h.htm)

### b612 — 2015-06-30T22:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170108>

**
I Am Very Bothered**

I am very bothered when I think
of the bad things I have done in my life.
Not least that time in the chemistry lab
when I held a pair of scissors by the blades
and played the handles
in the naked lilac flame of the Bunsen burner;
then called your name, and handed them over.

O the unrivalled stench of branded skin
as you slipped your thumb and middle finger in,
then couldn't shake off the two burning rings. Marked,
the doctor said, for eternity.

Don't believe me, please, if I say
that was just my butterfingered way, at thirteen,
of asking you if you would marry me.

Simon Armitage

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-30T22:29:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170111>

Here's an Ezra Pound translation form the Chinese:

**Lament of the Frontier Guard**

By the North Gate, the wind blows full of sand,
Lonely from the beginning of time until now!
Trees fall, the grass goes yellow with autumn.
I climb the towers and towers
to watch out the barbarous land:
Desolate castle, the sky, the wide desert.
There is no wall left to this village.
Bones white with a thousand frosts,
High heaps, covered with trees and grass;
Who brought this to pass?
Who has brought the flaming imperial anger?
Who has brought the army with drums and with kettle-drums?
Barbarous kings.
A gracious spring, turned to blood-ravenous autumn,
A turmoil of wars-men, spread over the middle kingdom,
Three hundred and sixty thousand,
And sorrow, sorrow like rain.
Sorrow to go, and sorrow, sorrow returning,
Desolate, desolate fields,
And no children of warfare upon them,
No longer the men for offence and defence.
Ah, how shall you know the dreary sorrow at the North Gate,
With Rihoku’s name forgotten,
And we guardsmen fed to the tigers.

### b612 — 2015-07-01T00:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170124>

I can't say I love this one. But I kinda like it. Would like to make my own version

“since feeling is first,” e.e. cummings

since feeling is first
who pays any attention
to the syntax of things
will never wholly kiss you;
wholly to be a fool
while Spring is in the world

my blood approves,
and kisses are a better fate
than wisdom
lady i swear by all flowers. Don’t cry
—the best gesture of my brain is less than
your eyelids’ flutter which says

we are for each other: then
laugh, leaning back in my arms
for life’s not a paragraph

And death i think is no parenthesis

### JoeBranca — 2015-07-01T02:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170136>

I used to scribble a bunch. This is one of the more coherent ones, from almost 18 years ago in the basement of the university library (wasn't into studying for the math final).

"Detail on Map of the World"

What lies behind the intent
With acts of common occurence
Such as the flash meeting of the eyes
of strangers passing on the street?

Is it an instinct to affirm humanity
Or a superficially courteous duty
Or something of a deeper well:

Like a longing to intercept beauty
Or silent defense of our safety
Or is a faint hope through the eyes
Though at a distance, to recognize?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-01T02:38:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170137>

**Magical Sunset**
As the day ends to rest
The sunset does its best
Setting on fire the lively waves
Coloring orange the nature he saves
its pure ancient glorious perfection.

The great ocean will receive
The burning sun who's going to leave.
Slowly comes the night
Devouring that magic light:
we are still suspended in a great delight.

### notsoperfick — 2015-07-01T09:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170207>

Always a favourite and Walter De La Mare again:

**The Listeners**
By [Walter de La Mare](http://www.poetryfoundation.org/bio/walter-de-la-mare)
‘Is there anybody there?’ said the Traveller,
Knocking on the moonlit door;
And his horse in the silence champed the grasses
Of the forest’s ferny floor:
And a bird flew up out of the turret,
Above the Traveller’s head:
And he smote upon the door again a second time;
‘Is there anybody there?’ he said.
But no one descended to the Traveller;
No head from the leaf-fringed sill
Leaned over and looked into his grey eyes,
Where he stood perplexed and still.
But only a host of phantom listeners
That dwelt in the lone house then
Stood listening in the quiet of the moonlight
To that voice from the world of men:
Stood thronging the faint moonbeams on the dark stair,
That goes down to the empty hall,
Hearkening in an air stirred and shaken
By the lonely Traveller’s call.
And he felt in his heart their strangeness,
Their stillness answering his cry,
While his horse moved, cropping the dark turf,
’Neath the starred and leafy sky;
For he suddenly smote on the door, even
Louder, and lifted his head:—
‘Tell them I came, and no one answered,
That I kept my word,’ he said.
Never the least stir made the listeners,
Though every word he spake
Fell echoing through the shadowiness of the still house
From the one man left awake:
Ay, they heard his foot upon the stirrup,
And the sound of iron on stone,
And how the silence surged softly backward,
When the plunging hoofs were gone.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-01T20:16:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170239>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=170124)
>
> since feeling is first
> who pays any attention
> to the syntax of things

Clever. And, whether true or not, written by someone - don't know much about him other than his dislike of capitals - who would likely would understand syntax well.

It's interesting to compare the Rossetti. That's quite syntactically complex. I'll bet you many - most? - people would hear:

> Better by far you should forget \[me\] and smile
> Than that you should remember and be sad.

But she actually says:

> For **if** the darkness and corruption leave
> A vestige of the thoughts that once I had, better, etc. ...

She's not quite ready to be forgotten, I think.

Anyway, a careful reading already showed it was forgetfulness "for a while" that she had in mind:

> … if you should forget me **for a while**

### b612 — 2015-07-02T12:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170341>

**Fog**
BY [CARL SANDBURG](http://www.poetryfoundation.org/bio/carl-sandburg)

The fog comes
on little cat feet.

It sits looking
over harbor and city
on silent haunches
and then moves on.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-02T20:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170393>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=170340)
>
> **The Love song of J. Alfred Prufrock**
> \`
> Have known the evenings, mornings, afternoons,
> I have measured out my life with coffee spoons;

From memory … let's see if I can … the concluding stanzas, where the bumbling Polonius shuffles offstage somehow and the mood changes quite powerfully

… I have seen the mermaids singing each to each
I do not think that they will sing to me.

I have seen them riding seaward on the waves
Combing the white hair of the waves blown back
Where the wind blows the water white and black

We have lingered in the chambers of the sea
By sea-girls wreathed with seaweed red and brown
Till human voices wake us and we drown

But almost any Eliot. Here the "hyacinth girl" speaks:

You gave me hyacinths first a year ago
They called me the hyacinth girl
Yet when we came back, late, from the hyacinth garden
Your \[ … \] and your hair wet
I could not speak and my eyes failed me
I was neither living nor dead and I knew nothing
Looking into the heart of light the silence

Then the quote from Tristan and Isolde.

_Oed und leer das meer_

I lost it in there somewhere. I forget what goes where I put \[ … \]

So - there's a game. Which poem does that fragment above come from?

### b612 — 2015-07-05T10:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170871>

The beginning... I know it by heart:

Dylan Thomas

Under Milk Wood

\[Silence\]

FIRST VOICE \[very softly\]

To begin at the beginning:

It is Spring, moonless night in the small town, starless and bible-black, the cobblestreets silent and the hunched, courters'-and- rabbits' wood limping invisible down to the sloeblack, slow, black, crowblack, fishingboat-bobbing sea. The houses are blind as moles (though moles see fine to-night in the snouting, velvet dingles) or blind as Captain Cat there in the muffled middle by the pump and the town clock, the shops in mourning, the Welfare Hall in widows' weeds. And all the people of the lulled and dumbfound town are sleeping now.

Hush, the babies are sleeping, the farmers, the fishers, the tradesmen and pensioners, cobbler, schoolteacher, postman and publican, the undertaker and the fancy woman, drunkard, dressmaker, preacher, policeman, the webfoot cocklewomen and the tidy wives. Young girls lie bedded soft or glide in their dreams, with rings and trousseaux, bridesmaided by glow-worms down the aisles of the organplaying wood. The boys are dreaming wicked or of the bucking ranches of the night and the jollyrogered sea. And the anthracite statues of the horses sleep in the fields, and the cows in the byres, and the dogs in the wet-nosed yards; and the cats nap in the slant corners or lope sly, streaking and needling, on the one cloud of the roofs.

You can hear the dew falling, and the hushed town breathing.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-05T11:58:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170872>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=170870)
>
> T.S. Eliot “The Waste Land.”
> Beautiful.

What slipped my mind came to me later: "your _arms full_ and your hair wet …" I suppose he's carrying the flowers.

Yes, the passage is beautiful indeed. Like several other parts of the poem it seems to be there partly to provide an ironic commentary on the seduction of the typist, who "smoothes her hair with automatic hand, And puts a record on the gramophone". The phrase makes one think of the motorised arm of the player moving, and emphasises that she, and her emotions, aren't engaged. Unlike the girl coming back from the garden, "looking into the heart of light the silence", she doesn't know what it's all about.

How could anyone who hadn't had an experience like that know that there could be something more? For me this is one of the things poetry does superlatively well. Because form and content are inseparable and form matters - or, actually, better, because it speaks the language of the heart - it can suggest such things.

But the incurably prosaic are always with us. _"Par ma foi, il y a plus de quarante ans que je dis de la prose, sans que j’en susse rien ; et je vous suis le plus obligé du monde, de m’avoir appris cela."_

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-05T12:01:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-170873>

Here's a poem by Alistair McLean I stumbled upon once. I've never seen it anywhere in any collection. It seems quite extraordinary to me. It's an imagined conversation between one of the "gangrel folk" - wandering tinkers in Scotland - and a wealthy man on a Hebridean island.

Said the thriving man to the tinker’s wife,
“Spell me the secret of your happy life.

“I could not bide it, I was never made
To couch in bracken with the birch for shade.

“And hills I love not, their ironic smile
Seems scornful of the things I hold worth while.

“Nor am I ravished of the woodland note
That breaks in tumult from the merle’s apt throat.

“Yet you love them all. But why? What’s the art,
That turns rose-petal into quiet of heart?”

“Ah, easy to answer,” she softly said,
“I’m richer than you—that’s your riddle read.

“Have you heard fir-music? Or the whisp’ring tales
That the tide brings in when the white moon pales?

“Or glimpsed the slim grace of the tall June grass
Bowing demurely to the winds that pass?

“Or strayed through a glen where wild violet grew
And thought, “Well, Heaven will be nothing new?”

“So I envy none—if I’m poor, I’m free
To the feast love spreads for the like o’ me.”
