### Gagnrad — 2015-09-27T17:57:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-177982>

> [notsoperfick said:](/goto/post?id=177937)
>
> Cambridge professor with a bent for Latin, I have to admire that, Latin and I never gelled.

Yeah, but I think one has to bear in mind that that is what education in England used to be like. Think of the lessons shown in _Stalky & Co._ Education in the public schools and the grammar schools was based around the classics, particularly Latin. It was Arnold's innovation at Rugby to introduce the study of _modern_ languages! I know of grammar schools that as late as the 1930s had the A stream learning Latin and the B stream put to science subjects.

Interesting thing here. Latin is now a dead language, but did you ever wonder who killed it? My understanding is that it was the humanists. (I'm using that word in its original sense to mean people interested in humane learning, not in its modern politicised sense. Think of people like Erasmus and Sir Thomas More.) So the recovery of the classics - approx 16th century - meant getting the linguistic tools to read Greek texts. It also meant people struggling towards what they regarded as a "purer" Latin. That meant Latin of the "Golden Age". Unfortunately, they'd failed to realise that the language had changed, because people had continued to adapt it to fit new uses. My understanding is that, for example, the medieval philosophers had developed it in quite useful and powerful ways. Among other things, it's said, they made some great advances in logic that were later lost … and we then had to wait for people like Frege and Russell to rediscover the same, only in a new idiom. So the scholars, while making some important advances, also froze the language and killed it.

Back to Housman … I think Kipling could have written something like "When I was one-and-twenty …" almost as if it were a compositional exercise and taken a turn around the garden before Housman had finished sucking his pen.

I do quite like Housman's "Epitaph on an Army of Mercenaries". This is about the BEF stopping the Germans on Marne, isn't it? It was a professional army, of course - hence "mercenaries". He rose to the occasion there.

But again think of Kipling. Such facility with language. Again and again and again he does it. These are very moving:

[http://www.kiplingsociety.co.uk/poems\_epitaphs.htm](http://www.kiplingsociety.co.uk/poems_epitaphs.htm)

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-28T21:14:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178041>

How about a sonnet from Sir Philip Sidney?

Come Sleep! O Sleep, the certain knot of peace,
The baiting-place of wit, the balm of woe,
The poor man's wealth, the prisoner's release,
Th' indifferent judge between the high and low.
With shield of proof shield me from out the prease
Of those fierce darts despair at me doth throw:
O make in me those civil wars to cease;
I will good tribute pay, if thou do so.
Take thou of me smooth pillows, sweetest bed,
A chamber deaf to noise and blind to light,
A rosy garland and a weary head:
And if these things, as being thine by right,
Move not thy heavy grace, thou shalt in me,
Livelier than elsewhere, Stella's image see.

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-30T09:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178227>

> [notsoperfick said:](/goto/post?id=178178)
>
> I don't know Sidney, my education in the classics is sadly lacking. I have just looked him up though and another of his. Quite hard work as some of the language is a little obscure now, mmm, perhaps I don't mean obscure, archaic maybe.

The Arcadia is supposed to be vey good. I have got a copy but never round to reading it:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Countess-Pembrokes-Arcadia-published-Classics/dp/B00E32OKW0/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Countess-Pembrokes-Arcadia-published-Classics/dp/B00E32OKW0/)

Another very romantic figure - soldier and poet from the golden age of English. He died fighting the Spanish in the the Netherlands. Charlotte Yonge brings him in briefly as a character in _The Chaplet of Pearls_, which is a fairly good historical novel.

> Socialism killed Latin, it's so obviously elitist that it had to go

LOL. Still, it is a kind of interesting thing to think that it was a dead language already when it was killed. All through the Middle Ages Latin was still a living language. And this was why it was able to be the _lingua franca_ for Western Europe.

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-30T09:28:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178228>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=178185)
>
> please forgive me. Its the first poem i've everwritten.

I didn't understand it at first - about three quarters of that is you larking around before you start, isn't ? I like "those 48 kilos of me". That's a clever twist.

I was thinking someone could probably work up a good satire in verse on paleo forums if they thought long enough about it. Because paleo forums do have their ridiculous side, don't they? I often used to think that reading some of the posts at MDA.

I mean this. Most of the poetry in the world is about either the emotion of erotic love or religious emotion. There's a reason for that. It's because these experiences shake people up, challenge their ordinary ways of seeing, and seem to point towards the transcendent. People contemplating their own small selves aren't really that interesting:

_Dayadhvam:_ I have heard the key
Turn in the door once and turn once only
We think of the key, each in his prison
Thinking of the key, each confirms a prison ...

The near-obsession with one's own physical health that you will find on some paleo boards would be sad if it weren't ridiculous. (Plato ridicules this kind of thing in the _Republic_, which shows it was already going on in Ancient Greece.) Where it's people seeking answers for health problems it's one thing (and one hopes, anyway, people doing that have other interests in life). But all this nonsense about tweaking and "perfecting" diets and exercise regimes just because people have got nothing else to fill their time and their minds with. There are people who actually worry about their abdominal muscles being able to be seen through the fat layer. In fact, there are even some women who do that - and some who have gone into eating-disorder behaviour because of it, because the fat layer _should_ be proportionately rather thicker on women.

Another reason I like the WAPF is because Sally Fallon did get interested in diet mostly because she was interested in diets for pregnancy and for growing children. So there is a wider context.

But there's certainly room for some satire directed at the paleo forums.

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-30T15:42:45+0300

### b612 — 2015-10-01T21:19:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178400>

> You are the cold that you feel

You forgot ".........................................."

I've been dancing all the afternoon. Rock'n'roll kind of songs.

I found this one. Just imagine Jack with a pink purple Jack singing

_Yo no soy marinero

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-08T15:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178859>

How about some nonsense verse?

I have an idea that nonsense verse might be something peculiarly English - though I can think of one American example I might post later - and if so perhaps there's some deep cultural root to it. But I don't know. There is a book on this subject by a history academic, but I haven't read it:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Origins-English-Nonsense-Noel-Malcolm/dp/0002558270](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Origins-English-Nonsense-Noel-Malcolm/dp/0002558270)

Lewis Carroll seems a good place to start. (Maybe with him there's some connection with his interest in mathematics and logical games.)

"The Walrus and the Carpenter" comes to mind - and as a bonus for this place oysters come into the poem:

"I weep for you," the Walrus said: "I deeply sympathize."
With sobs and tears he sorted out
Those of the largest size,
Holding his pocket-handkerchief Before his streaming eyes.

But let's begin at the beginning:

The sun was shining on the sea,
Shining with all his might:
He did his very best to make
The billows smooth and bright--
And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. ...

[http://www.jabberwocky.com/carroll/walrus.html](http://www.jabberwocky.com/carroll/walrus.html)

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-08T19:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-178874>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=178859)
>
> ... I can think of one American example I might post later ...

"A Pavane for the Nursery" by William Jay Smith:

[https://fromtroublesofthisworld.wor...-pavane-for-the-nursery-by-william-jay-smith/](https://fromtroublesofthisworld.wordpress.com/2012/02/22/a-pavane-for-the-nursery-by-william-jay-smith/)

Emma Matthews the Australian coloratura soprano sings a setting of this, put to music by an Australian composer, on her album _Emma Matthews in Monte Carlo_.

They may have a hole in the O₃ layer, but at least they've got a good soprano Down Under ...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-25T14:27:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-180415>

Light sculpts our proteins to give us the life we get. Light truly is one of the greatest mysteries in the universe. Life has its enigmatic events, that can create a mystery, or slowly evolve our misery. It all depends on the perspective we use to see, and is ultimately determined by what kind of light enters our retina to build our frontal lobes. This is why we must approach our life's scientifically and not poetically. We can enjoy the prose of life but we cannot allow it to dominate our attention.

### Gagnrad — 2015-11-04T09:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favourite-poems.15968/post-181118>

The humanities have their uses. I'm not sure I could ever bring you to understand this. They are certainly more than entertainment, and would have died out before now in favour of TV and video games if that were all they were. But their purpose and meaning is not perhaps that easy to understand. I have linked at least one important lecture by a world-leading philosopher on this before. However, this is all a disputed area anyway. Here, at any rate, is _a_ view on the matter for those who are interested:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Culture-Counts-Feeling-Besieged-Encounters/dp/1594031940/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Culture-Counts-Feeling-Besieged-Encounters/dp/1594031940/)

I think there are some good poems in this thread, some of them posted by me. My regret is that I did not leave anything better here or elsewhere on this site. As the Dakota say:

[https://twitter.com/NativAmProverbs/status/661698672523157504](https://twitter.com/NativAmProverbs/status/661698672523157504)

# Thread: Really tough family situation hindering health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T05:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170012>

disclaimer: if this is too personal and breaks some kind of rule, please let me know and I will take it down right away.

Hi everyone,

This is something I've been dancing around for far too long. It is an issue that is probably the greatest hindrance on my health, far beyond any blue light or nn-EMF issue in my environment. It really brings me down too often and I just can't handle it anymore.

I'm 15 (almost 16) and live 20 minutes outside of Philadelphia. I attend a public high school. I am one of four kids in my family, three triplets (2 girls and then myself- all C-section) and a little brother who is 12.

My parents were married for around 6 years but separated in 2002 for reasons I'm unsure of, but strongly assume have to do with the stress of raising four kids and therefore miscommunications, although I can't speak to this with any certainty. I have memories of wanting to defend my mom during those times, though they're very vague.

As I grew older, I only really have any memories of my mom being horrible stressed all the time. She had to raise us almost full time (we had a nanny for a while when I was younger) as well as try to make a living, as well as try to have money to retire with. As time went on, I remember these stresses never ending and being omnipresent, myself especially picking up on and being affected by them.

Fast forward to middle school, nothing has changed in these respects, only worsened. I have grown and am enjoying being grown up. I run track, and am a "healthy" vegetarian. I have a great group of friends, and life is good. I feel as though nothing is wrong and all is right. However, there is a big gaping hole, almost a numbness, that I almost immediately ignore and never consider about those times. It is like I have blocked out memories. Trying to return to them, I remember feeling helpless and never knowing what to do in the face of lots of what felt like extreme verbal fighting at home, as well as my mom often vocalizing (or at least not censoring) her massive stresses.

### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T05:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170013>

Eventually struck 9th grade, and my health falls apart. Chronic migraines, constant, never-ending gas, nasty breath, anxiety and depression, GERD, all force me to challenge my beliefs.

Paleo diet, GAPS diet, binge ice cream eating/extreme paleo/GAPS dieting for many months. 10th grade, extreme insecurity about appearance, breath, gas, etc., because I believe it is my fault. Slowly enter epi-paleo. I find a paleo practitioner. We find a parasite in my gut. Antibiotic + gut rebuilding diet + supplements. We find high cortisol. Specific supplements, and I begin to dive down the Kruse rabit hole. Slowly enter cold adapting. Slowly end carb binges and allergenic food (autoimmune protocol foods) binging. Ketosis + seafood. Eventually, enter EMF and blue light reduction. Enter sleeping in basement, then outside. Enter CT. Enter specific supplement regimens to help specific issues. Currently, I am following the leptin rx, CT, leaky gut rx, and am looking to do EPCOTx. You can see my specifc interventions in the most recent journal post of my journal- "15 year old who could use some advice" [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/page-10](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/page-10)

Enter YES abroad, enter Bosnia and Herzegovina. I looked originally as a way to get out of a shitty situation with my insecurity at shool. I came to see it as a phenomenal decision regardless. I leave in 6 weeks.

Come the end of 10th grade, many of my issues have significantly resolved. I really dive back in socially. I'm doing quite well, although my confidence could improve (related to this issue). I generally feel happy, when I'm not at home near my mom.

My mom is 50. She is beautiful, kind, and strong. She has always cared about health, though conventional views ruined her. She has vitaligo (paleo diet slowed spread) and hashimoto's. She found a nodule on her thyroid during a catscan and found a cancer. Whole thyroid removed, taking synthroid. I'm convinced she has an eating disorder- she eats nothing and isn't hungry but then becomes a ravenous animal.

Here's how it ties to me. I find myself anxious, then miserable, then suicidal, then depressed all too often, The process happens entirely a few times a month, but is often resolved early.

I have been working with a phenomenal therapist that my dad is kind enough to pay a lot for. He has really helped. He has identified a compassionate child, "attunement", and a sponge that became saturated with my health finding. I am curious of Dr. K's views on traditional psychology.

Tbe reason I have posted this is because I am the edge of insanity right now. I know that I dont want to hurt myself because I have a whole life to live, but sometimes it's appealing. What happened today was: I wake up feeling great after a great weekend at abroad orientation in DC. I have to get bloodwork so my mom drives me. I am sensing stress the entire trip. This gets me off to a weird start. Later, she is talking to my sister in the kitchen, but it felt like an argument to me and triggered me and I got upset and asked them to stop fighting. Then my mom yelled at me to not tell her how to talk. That action pushes me off a clif. I hit rock bottom. I start slamming things feeling helpless. I walk outside and slam the door. My mom follows me out in a hostile manner and takes my food to get me to talk. I'm scared and dont. I walk away barefoot with nothing to a park and go sleep in the woods there for a few hours, crying the whole way there and while there for a while. I come home, hide from my friends who are looking for me, and then call my dad in tears. I continue to lay distraught, as if the world is ending- despite how priviliged I know I am. Eventually my (other) amazing sister orchestrated me talking to my mom. She probably feels distraught too. We just made up, and I feel like life is worth living again. I'm out of rock bottom.

This has been my mental struggle for a long time and I've ignored it. I would appreciate any experience, insight, or anything. I hope to shed light on whether this has more to do with emf/fake light or more to do with the circumstantial stress and my thoughts, and where should I focus my attention to adress this issue?

As most of you are experienced adults, I would really love to hear anythingn you have to say. My goal is to fix my health therefore fix this major weekly stressor, and to have a more solid relationship with my mom and hopefully to convince my mom to overcome her health issues.

Thanks,

\- Matt

### Penny — 2015-06-30T08:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170032>

Matty, you can't change anyone's behavior, just your response to it - telling someone how what they're doing is making you feel might be a better way to go instead of telling them what to do - adults hate being told what to do Matty - but we feel so self-righteous telling our children what to do - the hypocrisy of it all

In my family, we had long periods of boredom coupled with brief moments of intense excitement - then 45 minutes everyone was supposed to "get over it" - I remember sitting there in shock thinking "Jesus, do you people even realize what you just said to each other?"

Your mom has a pattern that she got from her mom who got it from hers - until someone recognizes the pattern and feels the need to change it, you can do nothing except try to avoid the fall out -

You are also entering your last growth spurt - my youngest kid just hit 6'4" - truly, to say your hormones are screwed up at the moment and that you are raw is the understatement of the year - my advice to teenagers is : just try to get through it - your hormones will level off, your brain will fully mylinate and the way you look at the world will be totally different and way more logical than what you have going on at the moment - this is why we have special laws for children -

Try to cut your mom some slack; who knows what she is feeling at the moment or what her stress load is or even what her hormones are like -

As a pragmatic thing, if I were you, I would learn "The Relaxation Response" - when you feel stress coming on, you actually go into a meditative state - pretty soon you are doing that automagically and you won't be so raw - you can google it -

happy thoughts Matty - you only have 6 more weeks, hang in there - it is going to be a marvelous experience

### caroline — 2015-06-30T08:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170036>

Thinking of you Matty ...and sending you a big hug .....

### b612 — 2015-06-30T11:23:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170048>

Hey Matty.
It's a little bit hard for me to write in English because it's not my native language. I would like to say a lot of things because I also had a lot of troubles when I was your age.
Please don't take it wrong... you are a teenager. Every little thing hurts more than it should.
Make a profit of these years. The world is magic now. Write poems or short stories. Create songs and paint. Express your pain and joy.
Don't grow numb to the world's pain.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-30T13:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170051>

Hey Matty,

cool that you can be so open about this issue. Reading it , taking away the mental anguish,it sounds like an electron steal situation acercabated by your surroundings other anxieties,nothing more brought on by a stressful early childhood. It would be good if you remembered what the doc said : "Noone should be considered innately impaired = nothing is hopeless" In short what you have in teh doc's approach is the most rounded view why we are the way we are and how we could be . This was just a day ,nothing more .
Did you ever go to the beach as you were musing in another post ? Do it ,now ! It s something you will not get in Bosnia but you will get other marvellous things . Leave your school experince behind . The climate you know is a very US centric one . Looking from the outside I cannot believe the ridiculous peer and parental pressure kids get put under. It is not all roses in other places but I think a little easier still. Consider yourself strong and you are strong. Search anything new that gives you pleasure and does you no harm. Presume that there s a new person coming out of you ,that he will land with your passport in Bosnia in six weeks time . The challenges will be exciting and shape you if you let them . Report them here so we can live the adventure with you.

Thanks so much. I will definitely look into the relaxation response. I feel as though I might have been intuitively trying this, but not going all the way with it. I guess my hormones are all crazy. I'll keep doing everything for optimal and responding to these situations well.

### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T15:00:00+0300

### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T15:02:54+0300

You're english sounds good- this reminds me of how I will feel in Bosnia . Thanks a lot. I will do everything to really enjoy these good times when I am free. I will definitely always try to do something I love. I'll try my best not to take anything the wrong way.

### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T15:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170064>

Report them here so we can live the adventure with you.

Thanks a lot, Lahelda! I will keep doing my best and trying to give my mom an easier time and remember that I can't necessarily change anything besides how I respond. There is nothing like Dr. K's views on health and happiness.

At my orientation, I met (for a second time) the kid I will be going to Banja Luka with. He is just turning 16 (like me), but 6 foot 4 inches and a very handsome kid. Guess where he lives? Carmel, California- a block from the beach. Guess what he does- surfs, all day every day. No other person has been able to scientifically explain this phenomenon that is no coincidence. My best looking friends are from the backwoods of montana, the jersey shore, or spent many summers there at least. It's really fascinating.

I did end up going to the beach, it was amazing. I will do that more.

\- Matt

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-30T15:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170070>

This is a transgenerational epigenetic story and you need a lot of DHA and I am glad you're seeking help. Don't stop your quest for improvement. The world needs you.........

### Matty_M — 2015-06-30T17:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170091>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170070)
>
> This is a transgenerational epigenetic story and you need a lot of DHA and I am glad you're seeking help. Don't stop your quest for improvement. The world needs you.........

Just went to the fish market and bought 10 lbs of good candian farmed atlantic salmon and 6 of wild pacific king- eating almost a half pound raw twice daily with lime juice! much thanks to @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) for the inspiration. In Bosnia there's less emf but I'll make a point to buy lots of canned sardines, which the coordinator said there is no shortage of! Thanks for the input Dr. K!

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-30T20:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170100>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=170013)
>
> I have been working with a phenomenal therapist that my dad is kind enough to pay a lot for. He has really helped. He has identified a compassionate child, "attunement", and a sponge that became saturated with my health finding. I am curious of Dr. K's views on traditional psychology.

Well, he did comment, but not on that. I know he doesn't like the level of hostility that's sometimes levelled by people in paleo at the medical profession - understandably, since while some paleo "practitioners" are very ready to throw stones, there are a lot of questions begging to be asked about their own level of understanding and qualifications for dispensing advice.

But not everyone in the "psychological" area is a qualified medic.

You say "traditional psychology" there but the word you used first was "therapist". I don't know about the U.S. but in the UK a psychiatrist and a clinical psychologist wouldn't be the same thing, although they would work together, and the first might refer you to the second. A psychoanalyst would be something else again - and IMO (for what that's worth) would be using a broken model. A psychotherapist - or just "therapist" - could mean pretty much anything over here. Again, I don't know what the situation in the U.S. is.

If you were willing to do so, it might be revealing if you were to say a little more. You say he's helped: how has he helped?

Also … What happens in a session? Does he ask you about the past a lot? Is there anything you're given to do between visits? Have you been asked to change your thinking about certain situations? If so, have you been given any strategies for doing that? Anything else that's been shown to you and that you've been asked to do - protocols for "relaxing", for example?

Don't go into it, if you'd prefer not, of course. But it might help to get a handle on what you're telling us.

### Matty_M — 2015-07-01T21:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170246>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=170100)
>
> Well, he did comment, but not on that. I know he doesn't like the level of hostility that's sometimes levelled by people in paleo at the medical profession - understandably, since while some paleo "practitioners" are very ready to throw stones, there are a lot of questions begging to be asked about their own level of understanding and qualifications for dispensing advice.
>
> But not everyone in the "psychological" area is a qualified medic.
>
> You say "traditional psychology" there but the word you used first was "therapist". I don't know about the U.S. but in the UK a psychiatrist and a clinical psychologist wouldn't be the same thing, although they would work together, and the first might refer you to the second. A psychoanalyst would be something else again - and IMO (for what that's worth) would be using a broken model. A psychotherapist - or just "therapist" - could mean pretty much anything over here. Again, I don't know what the situation in the U.S. is.
>
> If you were willing to do so, it might be revealing if you were to say a little more. You say he's helped: how has he helped?
>
> Also … What happens in a session? Does he ask you about the past a lot? Is there anything you're given to do between visits? Have you been asked to change your thinking about certain situations? If so, have you been given any strategies for doing that? Anything else that's been shown to you and that you've been asked to do - protocols for "relaxing", for example?
>
> Don't go into it, if you'd prefer not, of course. But it might help to get a handle on what you're telling us.

I see what you mean. So when I go it is just me and I sit down and were in opposite chairs and just kind of start talking and he leads me where to go mentally and I guess the thoughts start coming out. He generally seems to believe that I have become like a saturated sponge and have to face past experiences in order to overcome these present scenarios.

We have worked back in my memories to find that a lot seems to stem from feeling scared and uncertain when I was younger because of my parents divorce. It was very traumatic and there was always lots of talk about living with our dad when we all wanted to stay at the same house and financial stuff and fighting. It seems like my therapist believes these past experience have stuck with me and have become a blow to my confidence and made me much more tense and less able to handle stressful situations with my mom (they always stress me out for no reason).

I do not believe he prescribes medication.

He has told me things to do such as really make an effort to talk to some new people or girls, where I would previously have had little or no confidence in these regards. I was very social in 8th grade but after my health story began it overwhelmed me and brought out these old emotions, like a saturated sponge. It definitely really has helped a lot to adress these things I hid from myself.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-01T22:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170250>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=170246)
>
> I see what you mean. So when I go it is just me and I sit down and were in opposite chairs and just kind of start talking and he leads me where to go mentally and I guess the thoughts start coming out. He generally seems to believe that I have become like a saturated sponge and have to face past experiences in order to overcome these present scenarios.

Yeah, that is interesting. So it doesn't sound like CBT or anything like that.

I've reservations about this kind of thing. There is some interesting material around on the what's said to be the ineffectiveness of the "talking cure". There is a brilliant French friend and colleague of Devra Davis, now sadly deceased - taken out by a cancer that was probably related to cellphone use (in the days before even she knew that was a problem) - who has some mordant and significant commentary on that:

[http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Without-Freud-Prozac-approaches/dp/1447211464/](http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Without-Freud-Prozac-approaches/dp/1447211464/)

I'd be wary of an expression like "saturated sponge". It's just words, but the way of seeing you've been offered in these words can come to dominate your thinking … and just maybe that's a kind of magic of words and it shouldn't.

I don't want to go too far. Maybe it could be helpful. But it's probably unwise to expect too much from that approach, and there are cautions with it, too. For example, bringing stuff back can make it become "live" again in a sense, and if those are problematic things and unless you're helped - somehow - to come into a new and better relation with them is that a good thing?

> We have worked back in my memories to find that a lot seems to stem from feeling scared and uncertain when I was younger because of my parents divorce. It was very traumatic and there was always lots of talk about living with our dad when we all wanted to stay at the same house and financial stuff and fighting. It seems like my therapist believes these past experience have stuck with me and have become a blow to my confidence and made me much more tense and less able to handle stressful situations with my mom (they always stress me out for no reason).

How much do you want to look back, though? And might it be more helpful to think more about moving on?

> I do not believe he prescribes medication.

Then does he choose not, or can he not? That's the question. But that might be a good thing. Pharmaceuticals are something of a can of worms. I really don't believe that they always are what we've been taking them to be - and people far, far clever than I are saying so pretty loudly these days.

> He has told me things to do such as really make an effort to talk to some new people or girls, where I would previously have had little or no confidence in these regards.

So he has more than one card in his hand. Interesting. IMO, that's good advice. I'd take it. I really would. Yes, it's not easy to talk to new people - and specially girls - but do it anyway. The pain lessens the more you do it.

Off the wall suggestion, but you might look into neurofeedback. Very, very interesting stuff. Not saying you have any really bad problems, but there's some success with that with people really intransigent problems like veterans with post-combat stress and alcoholics and drug-addicts, so it can likely do something for the rest of is. It might at least be kind of interesting to have an initial session and look at the mind-maps from it. I did, although I didn't think it worth the cost to pursue it. Maybe that doesn't interest you at all, but if it does say so and I'll look you out some links to read up on it.

### prAna303 — 2015-07-02T10:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-tough-family-situation-hindering-health.15985/post-170334>

> I have been working with a phenomenal therapist that my dad is kind enough to pay a lot for. He has really helped. He has identified a compassionate child, "attunement", and a sponge that became saturated with my health finding. I am curious of Dr. K's views on traditional psychology.

My take on this...

Everything that is living in somekind of way can become your therapist, why?

\- Living things share particles that makes the whole.

\- Take one and add one, you get something far more than two.

\- Put us in a working situation, natural one and you start to resonate.

This is why, digging in the dirt to make wonderful art with flowers do it for us, petting that dog, talking to a living human that is open and not taking himself to serious do it, list goes on...

When you face something living you start to understand yourself, today this is distorted with technology.

# Thread: IHT-Intermittent hypoxic training
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iht-intermittent-hypoxic-training.16010/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilly antic — 2015-06-30T16:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iht-intermittent-hypoxic-training.16010/post-170086>

somebody tried to convinced me the other day about the beneficial effects on mitochondria with IHT-
intermittent hypoxic training.
I was and remained sceptic as I think it cannot control increasing ubiquitination levels!

As an example they mentioned of all animals the whale, emphasizing the longevity of this mammal by using intermittent hypoxia.
But as we know, the rules and conditions of life in water are different than on land and air ( think the Ama....), so that the whale example might not be the convincing point of the theory.

Any ideas on the topic?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-01T02:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iht-intermittent-hypoxic-training.16010/post-170133>

It can help you when your redox is good. Ben Greenfield uses it well. If your redox is bad it is then a bad idea.

### lilly antic — 2015-07-01T10:06:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iht-intermittent-hypoxic-training.16010/post-170208>

Thanks Jack,

I thought so, too. Just was not sure .

### RyanMSauce — 2015-07-01T00:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-170123>

Hey everyone I am very excited about this. Blue blocking glasses are great around the house at night but if you want to go out and socialize past sundown its a little weird looking like you are about to do some wood work.

I thought that yea it would be cool to have amber tinted contact lenses and lo and behold they make them!

[http://www.mariettaoptometry.com/sport](http://www.mariettaoptometry.com/sport)

I think even if you don't have sight problems you can get them without vision correction.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-02T02:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-180939>

contacts? I see you don't read blogs carefully. Bad idea

### RyanMSauce — 2015-11-11T21:28:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-181688>

These would only be to wear at night after the sun goes down if you are going out. It would be the same thing as the blue blocking glasses except you don't look as weird wearing orange glasses at night.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-11T21:31:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-181692>

Again......you missed the point and the science and when you invoke vanity..............see ya.

### lilly antic — 2015-11-11T21:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-181697>

> [RyanMSauce said:](/goto/post?id=181688)
>
> These would only be to wear at night after the sun goes down if you are going out. It would be the same thing as the blue blocking glasses except you don't look as weird wearing orange glasses at night.

There are no blood vessels at the outer surface of the eye, so you depend entirely on the oxygen from the air directly.
If you put contacts on, you block that oxygen supply and create (pseudo)hypoxia. This is definitely nothing you want to have.
So better look geeky

### RyanMSauce — 2015-11-11T23:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-181710>

Is lasik a good option? I have really bad vision. -8.00 contact prescription. I have glasses that I try to wear half of the day but I get dizzy from them. I try and get as much light into my eye in the first half of the day as possible. I know contacts are horrible before I knew better I was wearing them 24/7 sometimes.

### JoeBranca — 2015-11-17T00:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-a-bad-ass-blue-light-mitigation-hack.16014/post-182118>

ah the high cost of past cluelessness... shooting myself in the foot for the quick fix, getting lasik 14 years ago. and then encouraging the wife to do the same two years ago : /

# Thread: Another newbie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie.16048/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2015-07-01T12:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie.16048/post-170210>

Hi Guys

Just wanted to introduce myself from the UK and hopefully find out some more information to help my husband's current situation.

I will try and keep this as short as possible and to the point, since 2013 my husband has had really low testosterone levels – we have seen many specialists who were not interested in finding out why this is happening and would rather just put him on HRT for the rest of his life. However we are also wanting to start a family, we had all of the other health checks and we found my husband has hypogonadism and azoospermia (zero sperm count).

We instantly changed his lifestyle he does not drink or smoke. We have changed his diet to meat and fat breakfast and a seafood lunch (mainly oysters) we are also trying extremely hard to limit EMFs but the type of work he does it is pretty hard but we have put many things in place to limit them as much as we can. We are outside a lot getting plenty of sun in the mornings & evenings and does CT every day for 2 hours+.

Over the last few years he has been feeling a lot better than he has but we are still having issues maintaining a decent Testosterone level it has fluctuated from 2-10 (UK ranges 8.6-29)

He was advised to try the HCG injection in 2014 but that was an absolute waste of time – it actually made him feel even worse, his Testosterone spiked for one month whilst on the HCG to 10 (UK ranges 8.6-29) He was also advised that he could be a hyper converter where his Testosterone is just turning into estrogen hence the reason the HCG did not work?

Since April 2015 we pushed the specialist to try Arimidex & Clomid as these are supposed to block estrogen production and increase Testosterone, here are the results so for…

Month 1 - May

Testosterone: 17.3 (ranges 8.6-29)

Oestradiol: 140 (ranges 28-156)

Month 2 - June

Testosterone: 16.5 ranges 8.6-29)

Oestradiol: 160 (ranges 28-156)

My thinking would be these drugs should be reducing the estrogen not increasing and I am little concerned that this is not a healthy level - even though the testosterone is at his highest – I don’t really like the look of is estrogen levels. What would be a good estrogen level of a man in his mid 30s?

I am after any advice on how we can find out what is causing this, how to improve his T and lower his E and hopefully produce sperm so we can start a family.

Thanks for reading and look forward to any feedback

### fitness@home — 2015-07-01T13:10:41+0300

I am going to tag @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) so he doesn't miss seeing your questions.

Hi All
I wanted to give you an update on our current situation, we have been following JK's protocols for the last year and back in July my husband's Testosterone levels were on the up and he started producing sperm again from having zero which was absolutely amazing (thank you so much for JK's protocols) there was a small amount which were of good quality to be able to try IVF treatment in September and but unfortunately we were not successful on this occasion. I am now thinking that we needed to give more time on doing the right things before we tried IVF but we also found out whilst doing this that I have borderline Polycystic ovaries and my AMH ovarian reserve is 99 which says I do not ovulate all the time? We are currently going through all the blogs but would appreciate a little help.

We recently did a Vit D test and our results were okay Husband: 96 & Myself: 93 but as we are in the UK we do struggle with the weather

What I would like to know is how we can improve this even further using JK's methods?

We are in the UK - can someone advise about where to get the black lights from please and also can anyone help us with regards to the reptile lights in the UK and what we need to purchase?

Would a magentico make much of a difference?

Should my husband increase his CT?

Look forward to your replies.

### Deverie_Night Owl — 2015-10-22T05:45:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie.16048/post-180128>

What type of work does your husband do? It will be very important to mitigate EMF exposure and reduce blue light. More EMF definitely calls for more CT.

A magnetico is very helpful if you can afford one. What is your living arrangement? (apartment, house, big city, etc.)

For bulbs, I don't know about US vs. UK, but can at least tell you the buzz for what some of us are using. Natural sunlight is always best, but these can be used when that's not an option:

This is a decent bulb some of us are using when we have to be inside on the computer (also shutting off fluorescent overhead lights helps):
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00101JIFG?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o02\_s01](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00101JIFG?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s01)

### Scompy — 2015-10-22T09:31:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie.16048/post-180131>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=180082)
>
> Thanks Lahelada for the links, I will have a read of those!
>
> Can anyone else help with my questions please.
>
> Thanks

Welcome...! For the husband, I'd recommend a lot more seafood, more water, better water, blue-blocking in the evening, grounding to the earth frequently and back-reflecting EMF. Bad behaviors in bad environments is a double problem. Is your husband a gamer? For instance, I was shocked to find out how much magnetic EMF was coming off of my game console's controller--WHEN IT RUMBLES--while having the controller on the lap effectively frying the family jewels, right? There's quite a bit of RF-EMF that comes out of the game consoles themselves too. It could be something stupidly silly-like these examples. It's hard to know the type of EMF and how much exposure you might be getting unless you have the right meters to check.

Personally, I have to mitigate my workspace for the RF-EMF I'm being bombarded with. Cellphones from many nearby employees, plus other Wifi sources including the fire alarm systems, are continuallycausing RF-EMF in the 0.5 to 3 milliWatts/Square meter range. I really wish everyone knew to put their devices in airplane mode so they don't ping cell towers every 5-10 seconds. Now I'm typically wearing an RF-reflecting cap and copper+silver threaded short sleeve undershirts to back-reflect more RF. I also get up out of my workspace frequently, drink more water and even ground frequenly over the day by sitting down at a nearby park in the morning sun taking an earlier lunch. It's little behavioral changes for personal mitigation that takes little to no effort.

### Deverie_Night Owl — 2015-10-26T22:51:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie.16048/post-180514>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=180265)
>
> Thanks very much for the reply and help!!!
>
> He works on a computer. Can i ask is the UV light bulb the black light that everyone talks about?
>
> And should the UV and infrared be used together when it is dark?
>
> Apologies but also do you have to keep a certain distance from the lights?
>
> Many, many thanks!!!

The Solar-Glo light has both IR and UV and also heats up. People will use this one when on the computer to get some good light when we can't be outside. Beware of distance, anything that heats up will also heat tissues and will cause some benefit loss (it's the opposite of CT - causes tissues to swell instead of shrink). I make sure I am more than a foot from the bulb and I only use it for an hour here and there throughout the day.

The Feit UV bulb or something similar is what people are using at night. Some people use mercury vapor UV bulb, but it is more expensive. I find that I have only the UV bulb on when I am journaling or reading in my bed at night. Some people will sleep with it on because it is not supposed to affect sleep, but Jack suggests shutting it off when going to sleep. I also look directly into this bulb in the morning if I have to get up before the sun rises.

I personally haven't used the IR only light since it has higher cost and I haven't purchased it yet. I try to just get the morning sun! Other people use that type of IR light on their navels or have an intranasal light. I don't know if they use it at night, I've seen most people use IR in the morning.

I don't think there is a minimum distance for bulbs that don't heat up. LED bulbs should not get hot. Power decreases with distance, so I wouldn't be too far from them. The IR light people put directly on their naval or inside nose. UV bulb I am usually a foot away from so it can shine on all my naked skin and eyes.

Does your husband wear contact lenses? If so, he should stop wearing them immediately.

As always, sunlight is best, but these bulbs sure are helpful for those of us who live further north and have already lost lots of sun (I'm in Wisconsin).

# Thread: Jack Ware Journal, 38 years old, Ohio, 6' 5" 220
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackware — 2015-07-01T17:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-170229>

Born in Mississippi. My family has a history of heart disease on both sides, and now one case of Alzheimer's. I played basketball in high school and have always been tall and lean with a high metabolism, and tan well.

Only major health issues have been a broken ulna and radius in 2009, due to a fall, countless sprained ankles and fingers over the years from playing sports, and over the last year I've developed right knee pain (after training for a half marathon) and over the last two months I believe I have developed a lower left leg stress fracture, just below the knee. Interesting how all my issues have been joint and bone related.

I have spent a lot of time on the computer since 1995, at home and for jobs, and indoor work has kept me out of the sun for a lot of that time. I can definitely see that my time getting sun and being grounded decreased as I aged. In my youth in Mississippi I played outside all the time, everyday.

I've been aware of Dr. Kruse for about two years and have tried to incorporate a lot of what he teaches, and I've done a lot of reading on the topics he discusses. I've always loved seafood, so that has been the easy part. The hardest part has been hacking the environment; figuring out ways to get grounded more often, cut blue light exposure, drinking more water, limit sitting time, eating the right things when at work and traveling, and finding opportunities to be cold with a wife who doesn't like the cold.

I just ditched my cell phone when my contract ended in May and I'm really happy about that one. Major environmental shift is going to occur next July when my wife and I move to Hawaii. We bought 3 acres on the Big Island, in a sparse subdivision, after a trip there last December and we're going to build our own off-the-grid home with farm and water catchment system. I'm so looking forward to rising with the sun, spending time at the beach and in the water, hiking, hunting, fishing, all other the other available island lifestyle choices.

After the homestead is built and we're harvesting plenty of food I want to buy a sailboat and eventually take annual sailing trips all around the world, visiting all the great coastal locations and gorging on the seafood available in those areas. I plan to finance all my adventures through writing and music. I'm working on my first projects now, stealing time both at home and work to get them done. Look forward to meeting and talking with everyone.

### jackware — 2015-07-02T22:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-170405>

First night of sleeping with no covers. Woke up several times cold but was able to get right back to sleep. Got up earlier than normal and went outside for some sunshine. Bacon, eggs, and salmon for breakfast.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-02T22:29:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-170408>

moving will be huge help............

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-02T22:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-170409>

People who walked for 90 minutes in nature showed decreased activity in a region of the brain associated with depression. We need to reconnect with nature and disconnect with our self-constructed, blue-lit, microwaved world.

[http://news.stanford.edu/news/2015/june/hiking-mental-health-063015.html](http://news.stanford.edu/news/2015/june/hiking-mental-health-063015.html)

### jackware — 2015-07-03T02:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-170443>

Yes, sir! Got a shucking knife on order. Got my eye on those 49 cent oysters at the local grocery store.

### jackware — 2015-07-08T04:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-171314>

CT is getting easier. Just doing 30 minutes a day in my bathtub, coldest water I can get out of the tap, with some frozen bottles of water added. Also getting used to sleeping nude or only with a loose pair of shorts. Other than the cold/cool temperatures getting easier, I am now remembering my dreams. I had gone the last couple years with very few nights of remembering what I dreamt. It's been every night lately, and Harrison Ford made an appearance. I don't know the science of why I'm remembering now but I'm sure it's just because things are working like they are supposed to.

### jackware — 2015-07-31T00:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-journal-38-years-old-ohio-6-5-220.16050/post-173569>

Fried oysters for lunch and sockeye salmon right now for dinner. Have not done a cold tub in weeks but I have cold showered each and every day. Lost 4 lbs. so far.

# Thread: Psyche's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-07-01T20:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-170240>

**INTRO**

48 y/o female, 5'4

The perfect storm of peri-menopause and running excessive carb-fueled distances while eating an overall poor diet has (shockingly) destroyed my hormonal balance and left me literally feeling like I have a dead battery.

I started bleeding (all the time) in 2013 and it scared me. I don't go to doctors, I've never been sick, etc. My doctor said I have uterine fibroids, she put me on bc pills, prozac. Bleeding stopped, I felt somewhat better- for a while. Worsening PMS, and just feeling extremely crappy and unlike myself most of the time. It finally just came to a head, and I said "enough".

I began looking for ways to improve my situation. I stopped eating all processed/ packaged foods and adopted a higher fat diet. As an ultrarunner, I adopted a training method developed by Phil Maffetone based on HR training that is designed to force you into aerobic training to improve mitochondrial function. I was shocked to find I had to WALK to stay within my aerobic HR range. This was a wake-up call that my mitochondria were trashed. Also a bad sign- I never felt good after walking or running.

The fact that mitochondria are HUGE kept me paying attention to everything I could do to improve mitochondrial function.

Ironically (now it's ironic, not then) I got an iPhone for the first time, and since I drive an hour to work, I started listening to podcasts. I eventually heard an interview with Dr. Kruse. The information about Leptin made total sense to me, and I immediately understood the need to first deal with my leptin issue before doing anything else.

**June 1 – 2015**

I started the Leptin Reset and CT. No exercise at all. Stopped taking all meds, which may not be smart, but I say go big or go home. So far, no ill effects, and I just had a "normal" period.

It’s been four weeks, and it’s hard to believe how good I feel. I may have regained leptin sensitivity at the brain level(?) by now but certainly not at the liver and muscle level.

Next- labs. Expense is an issue. In a few weeks I should at least be able to afford the proxy labs (HS-CRP, Vitamin D, DHEA).

On a final note, I'm addicted to CT. Someone who runs 100-300 mile races doesn't do anything small. CT is my new drug, and I need to find me a commercial grade ice maker.

This is an amazing ride- I feel like the health benefits I’m gaining are actually a side benefit to the education I’m receiving.

### Psyche — 2015-07-02T23:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-170432>

I've been reading a lot of other journals here. It's amazing how such disparate paths lead to the same place. For me, I feel like I wrecked my own health out of ignorance, and it wasn't until I got sick and tired of being sick and tired (and hormonal) that I said "enough" and decided to take responsibility for my health. That one action has changed my life.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-03T00:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-170435>

Estrogen = a higher anion gap = The anion gap is what MD's use to diagnose a metabolic acidosis. It is a measure of too many protons or too few electrons of most likely a combo effect of both. Calcium efflux = an increase in the anion gap. Increase anion gap = low NAD+ = Pseudohypoxia = low O2 = Low NAD+/NADH ratio = NAD+ drops in people with blue light exposure = excessive nnEMF in your environment for any reason = elevated ubiquitin rates = low levels of electrons = electron density in tissues is a function of the DHA concentrations = low EZ size in cell water = dehydration = higher positive charges (protons) in proteins making them less hydrophilic = low intracellular pH = low redox potential = cell and mitochondrial swelling (cyto c release) = lowered magnetic and electric fields in mitochondria = low ATP levels = a lot of carbs and protein electrons on ECT = altered serotonin and dopamine levels in the frontal lobes = NT release tied to calcium efflux = calcium controls voltage gated channels, NMDA, and glutamate excitotoxicity= low DC electric current = low tissue DHA = altered perceptions of reality and depression/anxiety

[http://highered.mheducation.com/sit.../chapter25/animation\_\_how\_the\_nad\_\_works.html](http://highered.mheducation.com/sites/0072507470/student_view0/chapter25/animation__how_the_nad__works.html)

Which, now that I think of it, I don't really even qualify as any more- after all, I actively study dialectric chemistry, semiconduction, physicis, cell biology, etc! And I mean DAILY))!!!

I have a lot of catching up to do. I read Jack's blogs every single day, and most days I listen to at least one of the podcasts...I actually printed the entire BG series and put it in a notebook and read it on vacation last month.

Lately, I notice I can connect the dots a lot more...this morning I looked at my dog's huge eye boogers and knew she had them because of low DC current. Yes, I seem to be seeing the world from a different perspective...

### Psyche — 2015-08-26T16:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-175790>

Hi, Caroline
Thank you...I'm grateful for the forum here. I'm from Western NC- work in Asheville, NC and live in Rutherfordton, NC (50 miles east of AVL).

### Psyche — 2016-01-05T17:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-185474>

**January 5, 2016
**
Looking back

I ended up with a bad hormonal imbalance last year (April 2015) that really affected me personally. I NEVER want to feel that bad again. I imagine I'm like a lot of people, who only search for answers when confronted with a serious problem. Yet, I also know that "serious problems" are serious opportunities, with tremendous power to change and shape you. So I'm not unhappy to be where I am.

My view on running (for myself) has changed in the last 6 months- I see running as an activity I can do only IF I can “hormonally” afford it. Because I believe altered hormone panels over a period of YEARS will more than likely produce a disease in me. I have enough little warning signs that all add up to tell me I need to pull back and regain a very solid level of health. This is “my” opinion as applied to myself only. I’m not going to judge or condemn anyone for doing something differently. I’m simply updating my knowledge, and I’ve made a change based on what I now believe, and based on my own situation and history. I still feel a ton of love for the ultra running community.

I started reading Phil Mafetone's work in April and listening to his ideas on podcasts, etc. This somehow led me to Ben Greenfield, and eventually to a Jack Kruse podcast. Everything Mafetone was saying, Jack Kruse said as well, but with massive science to back it. That is extremely important to me. At one time (in the late 80's and early 90's) I really tried to grasp the science of sport and nutrition, and made huge efforts to understand how we work and how to optimize everything (for sport but also for life). I gave up and was thoroughly disgusted with the lack of knowledge available, and the sheer amount of BS you have to wade through, and you couldn't trust ANY of it. Yet, my desire to know and learn didn't go away, I just shelved it. I feel like if you're human, it almost has to be of interest to you by definition, but then I talk to other people, and I see 90% of people (even athletes) are really NOT interested in this amazing thing we call LIFE. If they are, then it seems it almost always ends up that you get in to disagreements about ideas ("dogma"). Dogma is unavoidable, and it's so important to know this. The key and challenge is to learn things with an open mind and challenge everything, always.

I decided I'm not going for a quick fix in optimizing myself, but taking this opportunity to learn the things I've always wanted to know, but couldn't find the the information. The science Jack has is astounding to the point of being unfathomable. I mean, I am literally mind blown and couldn't turn back if I wanted to at this point. I came to fix my hormones, but stay because the science of Einstein is at the heart of all of our problems, and there is hope for everyone using what Einstein discovered. I've set off on a journey that will change my life for the better, and maybe the lives of people I love, too. This is an AMAZING journey, and I am grateful for the generous amount of time and caring that Jack freely gives in helping us all improve ourselves.

### Psyche — 2016-01-13T17:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-186182>

**January 13, 2016** \- Spring water, UV, DHA and CT update

**Located a natural spring water source -** Last week I found a natural spring near my house where I can fill up on water. I've started drinking water from it exclusively. It's delicious. It really tastes better than any water I've ever tasted. It may not be true, but it seems like it stays colder than water "should". I can't imagine what mechanism could be behind this, if it's true. Best part- it's FREE!!! Strike that- the BEST part is that spring water helps light transduction, especially UV light transduction. Every time I drink this delicious water I think about my eye clock being powered up to generate ocular melatonin. I actually _feel_ healthier....

**Tanning bed 3 x's a week -** I began supplementing UV by going to a tanning bed place three times a week. This is new, and so far it seems to really agree with me. I can only go early in the morning or at 6PM, so I'm choosing morning. I hydrate before, do some spot CT and face dunking before, use magnesium oil on my skin in hopes of absorbing more UV. Then, when I drive to work, I keep the windows down and get some inefficient CT.

**CT -** The transition to winter has been HARD. I stopped wanting to do CT and could not figure out why. In looking back, I see I finished the Leptin reset in September and my BAB went to the wayside. What I think happened, is that my BAB was helping me mitigate EMF effects from work. I would not crave sugar/ carbs in the bad environment. When the BAB stopped, my cravings came back. Partially, I think it was because I wasn't taking in enough calories, period. What I'm doing now is eating 2 meals a day, with most of it in the morning, and the second meal is more of a snack. It seems to be helping me, but I have no idea if it's "optimal" or not. At least I feel like I'm getting enough calories and this is helping me mitigate the sugar cravings from EMF at work. I'm also emphasizing **DHA** intake every day- I have SOME seafood each day, preferably raw oysters. I haven't seen too much desire to CT, but I'm back to face dunking with good results, and will try a session in the tub before the weekend. Ideally, I want to CT on days I go to the tanning bed.

### Psyche — 2016-01-15T19:17:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-186384>

**January 15, 2016** \- Update (massive)

I have experienced a huge shift this week. I've felt friendlier and more confident. The quality of my interactions with people is totally different. My internal reality has changed and my external reality has responded in kind. The best example of this happened just a few minutes ago with my boss at work. Meetings with him are always a source of anxiety for me. I over-prepare, trying to cover my bases for anything that might come up, etc. It's draining and stressful. Today, I was totally different. I walked in to the conference room and sat down with him, and I was clearly different. We started to discuss the topic we were meeting about, but because we were both in the moment, the conversation went all over the place, and it was fantastic! We began talking about big pharma. We had a wonderful conversation about how all of life comes down to the essentials- water, light and magnetism, and how we as a species have altered these to our detriment. Turns out my boss is a very thoughtful person.

I am blown away at this experience, and all the little ones like it I've had this week. Is it possible that changing to spring water and the concentrated UV light 3 times this weeks made this big of a difference? Perhaps the DHEA cream has raised my levels, also. Who knows for sure because I'm doing several things at once, but I don't really care. I feel amazing.

One more story to share- Ever since Ubi 24, I've had an overwhelming fascination with the eye. I cannot learn enough about it, and the more I learn the more enamored I become. It's like I never knew what an eye was before. It's like you have something so common, and then one day you find out it's made of diamonds or something. Any one of the little amazing facts about the eye just blow my mind over and over again. I think I mentioned earlier in my journal that if all I ever gained from this site was knowing the eye is both a camera and a clock, I feel like I would be the winner.

Anyway...yesterday I watched this little 3 minute video about optics from MinutePhysics. I shared it with my son and boyfriend, because it's just a great demonstration of physics at work. It's simple: look through a pinhole to focus the lens. I felt like it was a piece of information that helps me share how interesting I think this stuff is, but it's not threatening their beliefs and stuff.

**How To See Without Glasses**

Their response was telling (dopamine alert). My son actually thought it was cool (but immediately made the link, _with great dismay_, that I might wear pinhole glasses _in addition_ to my lovely nighttime Uvex glasses...!). My boyfriend has yet to look at the video. And I totally get _why_. Having this interaction with each of them has been so interesting. I now believe you can tell a person's dopamine level by talking to them.

Here's where it gets good. I spent a great deal of time this week reading about the eye, listening to podcasts about the eye (circadian clocks, optics, etc.), watching videos about the eye. (I try to read, watch, and listen to things because I've learned it increases how much information I retain. I'll do a deep dive, then sit back and let it all sink in...I allow the information to go where it needs to. Sometimes this results in great insight ("insight"! now there's a word!!), sometimes not.

Last night, I was turning all this information about the eye (and light, and dopamine) over in my mind (mind's eye?), and it occurred to me....**we don't actually see from the eye**. We think we do, but we don't. We see from the brain. The eye simply allows our _brain_ to have an interaction with light. Only a small amount of the light going in to the eye is used for vision. What about the rest of it? That light must be what informs all the cells of our body about our reality. It's all so fucking cool, I can't stand it. The clock part is the key. It's made of diamonds, baby.

### Psyche — 2016-01-15T19:26:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psyches-journal.16051/post-186385>

Another thought I'm pondering: "We are designed to respond." Nothing about our physiology enables us to initiate anything. Think about the ramifications of this. It means we don't chase light, it chases us.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-07-03T04:35:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-factor-x-tie-to-ubiquitination.16055/post-170455>

As usual, one of the core concepts to understand from this blog is how coupled cycles behave.

Let's refer specially to the case of CO2, global Temperature, global Nitrogen flux (storage and release), and their net effect on global Systemic Ubiquitination rates. We must understand that any perturbation in the cycle leads to feedback somewhere else in the cycle.

This is important to bear in mind, or else you may get confused by claims like "CO2 rises in response to Temperature", or "Temperature rises due to higher CO2", or "CO2 levels drive ubiquitination rates". The answer to each of those questions is Yes, Yes, and Yes  -- **all inputs drive all outputs in a coupled cycle**.

Of course, all of the drivers in the coupled cycle are just methods of encoding / representing the Electromagnetic force. It bears repeating that **life is designed to work with low-power / low frequency electromagnetic radiation**.

Implication: Any increase in EMF power, from any source whatsoever, will increase progression of biological time (high Ubiquitination). If you cannot deal with this fast-forwarding of the biological clock, you die.

I think we're talking about different contexts.

CO2 in the atmosphere => greenhouse effect, and dominance of CO2 consumers like plants (which happen to require Nitrogen)
CO2 in human blood serum => control of where Protons go (eg: Bohr Effect -- [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bohr\_effect](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bohr_effect))
CO2 in the oceans => acidification (dominance of proton flows)

We need to define the context in which CO2 is acting in to determine how it behaves.

> [[email redacted] said:](/goto/post?id=170478)
>
> Is 'coupled cycles' a sort of biological equivalent of 'equilibrium'?. So couple cycles are a kind of equilibrium 'far from equilibrium'? So some kind of equilibrium or homeo-stastis is good................what we have now more and more is uncoupled cycles that is lacking negative feed back loops.................so a build up of CO2 caused the atmosphere to heat, which liberates more CO2 from the permafrost and the stores in the bottom of the oceans which causes more warming which liberates even more CO2 in fires etc etc. Isn't that what they mean by a 'tipping point' a point at which all systems fail and nothing can be done. The uncoupled cycle just has to play itself out and eventually after it is played out a new and different 'equilibrium' re-asserts itself......................but we may be gone by then.

Global CO2 regulation in particular is not process with fixed equilibrium. The equilibrium conditions are dependent on the Electromagnetic environment that the planet finds itself in.

Note:
\- EMF and CO2 are always coupled.
\- CO2 and O2 are not always coupled.
\- We depend on O2 for survival => "failure" wrt our species means "CO2 behaviour leads to reduction in O2"

This is not the same as the notion of tight coupling between timing information between say the SCN clock and the Pancreas and Liver clock, in order to produce the correct amount of insulin. That is a process whereby a fixed range of homeostasis is the goal.

With CO2 levels, there is no such thing as a fixed level of equilibrium. **You always get the CO2 levels that your EMF environment dictates.** What happens to life as a result is a secondary side effect.

.....

Go back and read one paragraph of Brain Gut 1 and tell me the story of ubiquitination was not given long ago: "You might find this fact more amazing. Only 3% of human DNA contains the instructions for building cells and tissues in humans. 97% of our DNA is is not active in building a thing normally. Ponder that fact for a minute. Then ask yourself why would evolution collect and carry that much DNA if it were really junk? The reason she does this is simple. This ‘junk DNA’ is how evolution works so rapidly to adapt to changes in the environment. Ever since the K-T event, if you match up DNA genomes from these animals there is a simultaneous corresponding increase amount of junk DNA in their genomes. Humans have the most of all the mammals since K-T event. This is just another fact that supports my idea that evolution decided to speed up DNA expression to all the animals who survived this event. Junk DNA is how it accomplished the task in the primate and homo trees of life to an even greater degree. The more junk DNA you have the better able you are to adapt to a changing environment. It also allows evolutionary speed to increase as time elaspses. At first, scientists called this DNA, ‘junk DNA’ when I was medical school. Since then it has been upgraded to non coding DNA, and now it is called [retrotransposons](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retrotransposon). They now are realizing that this “junk” is really important to all mammalian evolution. In fact, it is the key to understanding how we came from Great Apes so fast."

**My "eagle’s eye" has focused on the subatomic core of the crisis:** the **Planetary Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)**. I have been identifying this problem on this very website for a long time as this thread shows. This argument was made with some of the old members who are no longer here now. If a battle is worth waging then the individual must decide to continue the fight in new ways to make a larger impact. The rapid spike in CO2 can not be the result of just a climate event, but it obviously related to the biophysical change inside of Earth causing a **massive dielectric transition** that is altering the very "hardware" of living matter on the crust (land, air, and sea)

The story of the crater 65 million years ago was an abrupt "loading" of the atmosphere that broke the planet’s **Isotopic Lagrangian**. Today, we are repeating that event, but this time, the "blackout" is not just from dust, it is a **biophysical blackout** caused by the collapsing dielectric constant of the water table by a weakening dyanmo.

**1\. The Carbon Enrichment Paradox**

Living matter is **100 times more concentrated in carbon** (18%) than the Earth’s crust (0.19%) because life is a **topological filter**.
**The 6 Pathways:** Organisms use 6 primary carbon fixation pathways (like the Calvin cycle and reverse TCA) to pull inorganic carbon into organic biomass.
**The Dielectric Engine:** These pathways are not just chemical; they are **electromagnetic**. They rely on a high **dielectric constant (K)** of water to maintain the proper "gate" for electron flow.
**The Current Crisis:** As CO2 rises at an unprecedented rate, it creates a "bioplasma" that acts as a dielectric noise. This "thickens" the water table, making it harder for the 6 pathways to fractionate carbon effectively.

**2\. Ubiquitin: The "Band Gap" Monitor**
I’ve linked the efficiency of the semiconductor fab plant in us to **Ubiquitin marking**, and the biophysics bear this out. Ubiquitin is a 76-amino acid protein that serves as the cell’s primary **quality control manager for the state of your semiconductors.

Dielectric Sensing:** Ubiquitin itself has a **dielectric spectrum** that is sensitive to its hydration shell. No one in centralized medicine seems to know this.

**The Semiconductor Shift:** Biological proteins act as **organic semiconductors**. Ubiquitin marking is the cell's attempt to "optimize the band gap", the energy required for electrons to move.

**The "KT-Crater" Effect:** In a high-carbon, high-D+ environment (like the post-impact winter or today’s nnEMF cities), the band gap widens. Ubiquitin begins "marking" proteins for destruction because they can no longer maintain coherent electronic flow in the "polluted" water table. This becomes a costly energy issue that facilitates ossification of the based chain (DNA/RNA) because it allows for more atomic mass in d+ to be added to CH3 groups to become -CH2D+,-CD+2H or -CD+3

**3\. The Dynamo Coupling & The Blank Sheet**

The 2025 _Science Advances_ paper confirms that **Earth's oxygen levels and geomagnetic dipole** are a single, coupled system.

**The Weakening Dynamo:** As the dipole weakens, the "Magnetic Torque" that helps fractionate deuterium and keep the oxygen "vortex" spinning collapses.

**The CO2 Spike as Evidence:** The 800,000-year rise in CO2 compressed into 55 years is the physical sign that the **Planetary Dynamo** is no longer providing the "torque" needed to clear the metabolic exhaust.

**The Future:** If we don't become **"atomically attuned,"** the future is indeed a blank sheet, a return to the "Pre-Cambrian" state where the dielectric noise is too high for complex, photonic life to exist.

**Summary:** We are in a **Topological Race**. The rise in CO2 is dropping the planetary dielectric constant, forcing **Ubiquitin** to go into overdrive as it tries to fix a "short-circuiting" biological computer.
