with all the other diseases you seem to be happy to give a really specific answer but with HD I get some cryptic words that i am left to try and figure out...i know that light folds proteins...and I know that ubiquniation pathways clear the mutant protein...but i still don't know if i am aiming to to not turn on the HD gene at all or is it a balance between not turning it on at all or clearing mutant protein via autophagy...glutatmate is a big deal and i have read todays linkin blog....i have suffered this because i know i have not been able to tolerate carbs for 15yrs....i have been carrying about 15kgs despite eating low carb keto diet....high iron demonstrates that i am not getting enough sun...i have had so much stress in my life over the last few months....my mother died...my son has been out of control...smashing up my house...i have had to have him arrested by police...i can see that the stress i have been suffering has resulted in night sweats...and affects on my motor function...my speech has suffered....my balance...will i recover these functions or are they lost? i have tried to get sun but i am not getting enough....

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-29T02:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-202052>

HD is a genetic diease with a relative basis. It does not cause problems until something later cause the genes to misbehave. That stimulus is stress.......in the lever call excessive ELF-UV light release. HD is a disease that kills early. This means it has a time variant.
Since time is relative in life and in HD, it means it can pass more slowly depending upon "your state of motion". You state of motion is Rosetta code for less light release from your cells under duress. It sounds like your entire life is duress hence why the shit is hitting the fan. That motion is directly linked to your tunneling speeds on your inner mitochondrial membrane. Tunneling speeds are a function of geometry in mitochondrial respiratory proteins. The closer the tunneling speeds are to the speed limit of light at night the longer you will live because as one approaches the speed of light, time begins to pass more slowly. This is what time dilation is. Time dilation is how we avoid disease. The reverse is also true. The further tunnelling speeds are from the speed of light the sooner you will die. Tunneling speeds in mitochondrial are fundamentally tied to the distance in angstroms between your respiratory proteins. This is controlled by melatonin cycle, your mitochondrial DNA, and your % heteroplasmy. STRESS DESTROYS THEM ALL.

This makes living things are a system of computation that uses light frequencies at night and electrons during the day to do its accounting work to see if you are time dilating or time contracting. The more you contract time the more likely HD become epigenetic expressed. The relative speed differences is what creates time or subtracts time from your telomeres to ruin ubiquitin and cause HD to manifest from your genome. We use light at night to signal, because light is incapable of interfering with other light rays being sent to and fro, because light does not have to obey the Pauli exclusion principle. Retained light in cells, therefore, creates the chance to build a quantum computer in cells and tissues that gain the ability to make trillions of connections that simply pass through one another going from place to place carrying information and energy with no loss of energy or information. Loss of light does the opposite and makes it more likely the HD is expressed. How is the light transformed or transmuted into things we can use like matter?

Light can only be confined by electric and magnetic fields in media like stars and living tissues. What affect controls this? In cells this electric and magnetic ability is called the Faraday effect, and it the key to understanding how living things can hit "warp speed" during night time. If we can retain lights speed in our tissues by altering the space and time in and around cells, we can gain time back via time dilation. The Faraday effect gives cells this ability because it [generates massive magnetic fields.](http://phys.org/news/2016-08-method-superstrong-magnetic-fields.html)

All these things connect to you connection to light water and magnetism and a LACK of stress.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-29T03:01:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-202053>

I think HD can be escaped.........but living as nature wants you too. Not every with HD dies of it. Huntington's Disease is not fatal in itself. People with HD have a shorter life expectancy and die of other life-threatening complications related to this disease because they all have poor mitochondria and do not know how to mitogate their environmental risks. You have the opportunity too but you keep becoming held captaive by a gene repeat. Pneumonia and heart disease are the two leading causes of death for people with HD not HD itself. When will you realize this?

### Richelle Jones — 2017-03-04T08:33:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-209596>

So we arrived at latitude 24 degrees (Agnes Water Queensland population 2000) about 8 weeks ago. Moving was an incredibly stressful experience....But now we are here...we have beautiful ocean views from our house and spending 2-4hrs a day outside on the beach and in the water every day...stand up paddle boarding etc. Maximum UV is 14 during summer. Lots of local seafood available. Unfortunately the house we are renting was drenched in glyphosate just before we moved in....in fact the local council is very liberal with the application of glyphosate. My 12 year old cat has had a rapid degeneration of his control over his back legs since arriving here. I was hoping to be feeling more relaxed by now....My teenage son is still making life difficult. After listening to the March 2017 webinar I re-read JK info in my journal with greater comprehension. I love doing that after a new webinar/blog...I always see something I didn't see before. I have mito haplotype H and blood group A neg...my husband (aged 56) has mito haplotype U6 and blood group O....since arriving here his hearing seems worse and his cognitive function not as good (his mother died at 72 of AD diagnosed in early 50s)...we are preparing to build a low emf/toxin house.

### JanSz — 2017-03-18T19:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-210607>

> [Richelle Jones said:](/goto/post?id=210528)
>
> and does mitochondrial halpotype effect HD? especially if the gene is inherited from the father rather than the mother?

My opinion, may be heartless, but that is how I talk with my friend who have advanced prostate cancer.
More or less,
haplotype and genetic details count for scientist or investigator, but not for someone who have it.
You cannot change your mother or father.
But you can test a lots of stuff.
Important is to test stuff that you know how to modify.
The more and more often you test, the more it helps you (hopefully).
What you cannot change you can test only if it cost you nothing.
Be mindful of buying stuff on advice of somebody who profits from selling this stuff to you.
Try not to fall into fad or beliefs.
................

### Richelle Jones — 2017-04-06T16:32:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-211893>

So when I lived at latitude 36 in canberra i lived in a house with solar panels. smart meter, near big power lines, mobile phone tower (500m) ....i knew my house was under geomagnetic stress due to lichen growing on our roof tiles and rocks in our garden...I got barefoot grounding in our garden drinking morning coffee 1hr or so......walking dogs with a bit of barefoot walking ......i retired from police work in 2013 but continued to live a very stressful life painting the house and organising our move from canberra....since 2013 I have been conscious of grounding & sunlight ....walking barefoot when i could and getting as much sun everyday (probably about 2 hrs total).....since moving to latitude 24 and population 2000 ... i have been getting 1 hr at barefoot at sunrise at the beach...then at least 2hrs more at the beach in AM sunlight in kinikiswimwear then 1 hour in afternoon sunlight from 3.30-430pm..... i feel a great urge to just go lie on the sand at the beach...not running around exercising...just laying on the sand...the black sand. my mito haplo is H. I have not had any blood tests done yet but i really feel the difference in me...i have lost weight just by lying at the beach. a few kgs in a few months. Diet same as in canberra except now i can eat fruit for first time in 15 yrs and still lose weight. Magnetico same since 2013. These are the effects of increased UV and grounding in me.

### Richelle Jones — 2017-04-23T08:24:25+0300

It is usually around 8. I expect to get a statin prescription as well...I have been printing off info (Seneff, Kruse, Wallace) to give him but I am keen not to piss him off cause i live in a small town and there aren't many doctors. Richelle love your result  I too have this urge when at the beach, to just lay down in the sand and the sun...... and then swim a little once in a while to cool off.. I can go on doing that a whole day and I never get bored of it!  And it makes me lose weight too  Ok I am not overweight but the more I do stuff like that the better I like how my body looks and feels . It is/feels like being a kid again

That is why I love the most to go to the beach alone because otherwise I always have to leave earlier because of the other person getting bored or "getting enough sun"
I think my batteries must be pretty large... they cant get enough!

### Richelle Jones — 2017-05-25T13:51:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-214643>

So for the last few months i have been living in sunlight paradise. sound of the ocean from my house..... I see views of sunrise and sunset on the beach.....hours and hours everyday on the beach and whilst I have felt some gains i am also still swamped with family stress....two to three years of chronic family stress have really taken their toll on me....teenagers are feral, mother died, father and brother are really sick with HD.....my HD is affecting my speech mostly....a few twitches in my arms and toes and some walking into corners of walls.....but I feel it most in my ability to roll with the punches and my speech...it is stilted and i am conscious of how i am impeded in my speech fluency....i hope i can get this back if i can get some peace from family crap but stress has been relentless and i am afraid i might have lost some things for good....i have recently had an episode of feeling like i am choking on my food.. like my throat muscles are just not working.....i am consciious of my vagal tone and do CT and diving in ocean and cold showers and chanting and yoga and meditation and breathing but it is so scary when you swallow and you can feel your throat muscles not working and your food is just stuck in your throat...this has happened a couple of times over the last ten years... ,most;y during times of stress....most HD people die of pneumonia or suicide so i know what to expect. any suggestions?

### Richelle Jones — 2017-06-03T13:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-215344>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170690)
>
> So how do electrons and proteins interact? Light.
>
> The dualism to the nature of light is best demonstrated by the photoelectric effect, where a "weak" UV light produces a current flow (by releases electrons) but a strong red light does not release electrons no matter how intense the red light. Red light then acts a type of cellular capacitor for energy. Where is it stored you ask? In cell membranes and in mitochondria.
>
> Einstein explained that light exists in a particle-like state as packets of energy (quanta) called photons. The photoelectric effect occurs because the packets of energy carried by each individual red photons are too weak to knock the electrons off the atoms no matter how many red photons you beamed onto the cathode (metal). But the individual UV photons were each strong enough to release the electron and cause a current flow. It appears that life needs a lot of UV light to work properly at low energy parts of the electromagnetic spectrum because life's job is to move electrons to and fro to alter the redox chemistry of proteins with those electrons.
>
> In HD when light signaling or electron flow is off...........HD manifests. One of the things people with HD rarely get told is that they should be highly charged with UV light. This means that an equatorial existence is one of the things that may help them tremendously avoid the turn on switch of this disease.

After listening to the June 2017 webinar yesterday (02 June 2017) I re-read Time 7 : The Photoelectric effect today...with so much more understanding and appreciation (in the morning laying on the sand at the beach) Compared to when i first read this in January 2016....I have so much more understanding ... I am really getting it now...wish we had moved to a higher latitude...but we will holiday there often. I am glad I moved from where i was and i can definitely confirm that more UV and IR AM sunlight has had a massive impact on improving my health this year. Also more grounding has had a big effect on me.

### Richelle Jones — 2017-09-10T09:03:15+0300

### Richelle Jones — 2017-09-10T12:14:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-223276>

Also I have been using the qunatlet and I have found the the performance 60 has the most profound effects on me...increased stregnth and flexibility at yoga...more sweating...a feeling of wellness...i have been getting 2-4 hrs of morning sunlight at the beach grounded...my tan is quite golden which indicates lots of eumelanin consistent with hy H mito haplotype and green eyes...i am looking forward to increased UV absorption and vitamin D this summer....I am comparatively very well compared to my younger sibling and father who have HD and do not do what I do...I am having cold showers this winter and swimming in the ocean....but its not that cold here...

s

### Richelle Jones — 2017-09-10T12:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-223277>

My speech fluency is improved....family stress is less now and i feel like my sunlgith exposure is countering these negative effects i was previulsy experienceing ...i feel like i have gained some ground against what i lost earlier in this year. my handwriting has improved. my memory and learning is better... I really feel like i have gained some ground compared to what i lost earlier in the year when i was really stressed from family and moving issues....i think the quantlet combined with natural sunlight has had this effect

### Richelle Jones — 2017-10-16T14:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-225969>

Huntingtons disease...HD .....I just want anyone who is looking for HD answers to find me....here....sunlight has made a huge difference to me....sleep, mood, health and wellbeing....none of my family will listen to me but maybe there are people out there in the world who have the HD gene and they are looking for answers and I just might be able to help them....only if they are willing to do what it takes....you will probably have to change a lot of things....this is no different to anyone else who is trying to reverse a disease....i can say for sure that higher latitude sunlight (in australia) has definitely helped me.... I have a mHtt CAG of 42....I am 50...I have no obvious symptoms of HD.....I have symptoms that I notice (since about 5 yrs ago....some jumps in arm nerves and speech changes ) but nothing that could be diagnosed by any neurologist....since 2014 I eat lots of seafood (2 or 4 times a week), I eat carbs in summer and keto in winter...I drink non fluoridated water since 2014, I have a magnetico sleep pad since 2015, I have been using quantlet since august this year....these things work. they make me feel better. I do not feel like I have degenerated since 2014....stress of any kind is detrimental and i have now reached a place where stress is a lot less for me.....by design....i have left work and i have moved to a place where life is better....i still have family responsibilities and family stress but i am trying to minimise these things.....this required a journey thru lots of stress to get here but its worth the effort....please come see me at my Huntingtons fb [https://www.facebook.com/groups/734481773352328/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/734481773352328/)
People diagnosed with HD are offered NO HOPE and NO TREATMENT and this is incorrect. HD has a variable age of onset and disease progression because HD is very much influenced by epigenetic factors that mainstream medicine ignores. Gene carriers do not have time to wait for mainstream medicine to learn what JK knows about health and life. When I was diagnosed with HD I went to uni and got myself a degree in human biology so I could help myself and my family and anyone else who wants to listen about how we can help ourselves optimise our lives. We have no time to wait for mainstream medicine to catch up. JK has the only answers I have found and I am willing and able to decipher this info as much as I can for me an anyone else who is willing to listen.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-18T15:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-226142>

Injuries don't happen isolated from one another. The redox potential in the body is what links them; this is why "injury prone label" sticks to athletes with higher heteroplasmy rates in tissues. The same concept is operation in nuclear diseases like HD due to the nucleotide repeats. Understanding how to keep them quiet is related to the pentose phosphate pathway and its deep connection to protons.

DDW is $30/L so I won't be drinking that but I do have desalination RO water where I live so that's good news. ### Richelle Jones — 2017-11-22T04:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-228612>

I started menopause this year....age 50...I haven't had a period for about 3mths...I started having hot flashes after I first used the Quantlet in Sept...at first i thought the Quantlet was causing them...then I realised it must be menopause....I haven't used the Quantelet for about a week and I didn't have hot flash for a few days and then today I used Quantelet Performance 60 and afterwards I have had hot flash again. Could the quantlet have anything to do with hot flashes?

### taiyang — 2017-11-22T12:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-228616>

Richelle, i just started menopause a couple of weeks ago (or else i have a change of life baby-jk). yesterday i did everything right- lots of water- 35 minutes of grounding in am sun in cold weather after a breakfast of seafood, then limited my blue light exposure- and i had insomnia at 1:30 am!!!!!! the only possibilities i see to remedy are: i need to take my b complex supp (stopped taking them) because i need b6 to make serotonin (which is needed to make melatonin), i confused my body by wearing blue blockers in the early am before the sun was up and i was sitting on the train, and/or i ate too late at night. so annoying!!!!!!!! we just need to keep doing trial and error. i will definitely get hot flashes from fasted hard exercise even done in the morning, so i just don't do that anymore. if the quantlet is giving you hot flashes consistently, see if you can return it or sell it.

my haplotype is U4a- the present descendants live in Russia where my mother was born. I lived in tropical/sub tropical regions of Taiwan and my body which had always been chubby thinned out fast. i was doing a lot of walking and tried to cover myself from the tropical sun. i ate lots of rice. the only time in taiwan where i started to get chubby is when i started to have one candy bar a day. when i stopped that, i got thin. when i came back to the US my mom said i was so thin i looked like i had been in a concentration camp. it wasn't super healthy think cuz i had also lost muscle. back in the US it took 6 months for me to go back to my pudgy self.

### Richelle Jones — 2018-06-09T12:31:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-243052>

I want to give you all an update from latitude 24 and how much sunlight has affected me and my husband since moving here. We have been sun baking for 4 years since retiring from work. For the first two years we sunbaked in canberra at latitude nearly 36 winter and sun baking summer and winter and although we saw improvements from just walking barefoot at sunrise to 7am and then sun baking naked in the backyard till 11am -12pm since moving to latitude 24 we have seen huge improvements. now we have UVB year round...in winter it is not lower than 5...so we still feel the difference between the strength of summer and winter light the ability make vitamin D year round is a massive improvement on mental and physical health. My husband who is 57 and whose mother died at 71 from AD and his father died at 51 from prostate cancer...and he and his 4 siblings all suffer from Raynauds disease....my husband has reversed his raynauds to the point he can now surf in 15Degree celsius water and only get a tiny amount of raynauds in one little finger...this is a huge reversal of his RD that all his other siblings suffer badly. He would previously have had frozen fingers and toes.... he has had epi paleo diet for 5 yrs (his siblings don't believe anything i say) that halted progressions of RD but sunlight at latitude 24 has reversed and improved his health beyond anything. We have almost the same sunlight exposure but his vitamin D was 159 and mine was only 80...so that shows that light is not the only factor....individual ability to convert sunlight to vitamin D is a big variable.... i think my HD has stabilised and certainly has not gotten worse. I have watched my father and younger brother (both HD gene carriers 41 and 49 ....who do not listen to me) deteriorate over that time....sunlight has been a massive game changer...but i spend 4-5 + hours a day sun baking almost naked...mostly in the AM sunlight..we have great sleep and wake naturally about an hour before sunrise. we wear blue blockers and are asleep by about 1-2 hrs after sunset. we eat our big meal at lunchtime. we eat loads of seafood. we drink italian spring water but not DDW...which is the next thing I plan to add to our regime. Humans evolved to be naked in the sunlight from sunrise to sunset...we are probably getting a lot more regular sun than most people can achieve but i don't feel thats even near what we actually need. I would love to get a whole day and night of sunlight and darkness and sleeping on the earth to really experience what we are really supposed to live each day. I have been using a magnetico for about 5 years...We live in a small town of onl;y 2000 people so we don't have a lot on non native emfs to deal with.

and in the context of me... i was ketogenic from 1998 to 2014 and this reversed my food allergies and thyroid disease (Graves disease) but since then we have added in seasonal carbs...which have made a big difference especially since moving to latitude 24 but we never eat after dark...i just want you all to get that sunlight is super important...we are getting 4-5 + hrs a day of sunlight for the last 2years...that is a shit load more sunlight than most people who are woking can possibly hope to get...and i still feel i need more...i think i need all day in sunlight to really feel best and i haven't really done this yet.

### Richelle Jones — 2018-06-09T13:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-243055>

and we are building a house that has shielded wiring (MC) that is costing an extra $65000.... i don't know if its just remote queensland but everything costs more here...i had hoped to build a completely zero nasty chemical house but it costs so much when you are remote to do anything different...all we could really do was have excellent wiring and lighting and lots of open windows for natural sunlgight...we are subtropical and in a cyclone area.

great update Richelle. Are you building your house so you can shower outside? cook outside? That is what I aspire too - I hate being inside cooking so much!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-21T00:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richelles-journal.16129/post-251432>

Biological and clinical manifestations of juvenile Huntington's disease: a retrospective analysis is now out there. The difference in young vs adult onset is likely tied to heteroplasmy rate in my opinion. [https://www.thelancet.com/journals/laneur/article/PIIS1474-4422(18)30294-1/fulltext?rss=yes](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/laneur/article/PIIS1474-4422\(18\)30294-1/fulltext?rss=yes)

# Thread: Is a high iron level bad?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-05T01:14:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170846>

I just got my hair element test back today and my naturopath was concerned about a very high iron level. I remember Jack talking about a high iron level being very bad somewhere but I can't remember where that information was.
Anybody know anything about this?

### SeaHorse — 2015-07-05T03:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170854>

Use the search function and look up transition metals, hemochromatosis, early autism blogs, what happens when stars die, and high ferritin and high iron levels...any of these will take you to lots of threads....you're right high iron isn't a good deal....join the club. Giving blood is one way to lower it in a mechanical way, but basically it's a sign of poor health and we just have to start heading in the other direction away from concentrating metals in our system.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T03:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170856>

and giving away RED CELL mass is not smart either.............if your paying attn.

Iron levels outside of RBC mass is a problem.

RBC mass or Hb or HCT is not the same thing.

### Jude — 2015-07-05T03:15:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170857>

> and giving away RED CELL mass is not smart either.............if your paying attn.
>
> Iron levels outside of RBC mass is a problem.
>
> RBC mass or Hb or HCT is not the same thing.

So, as redox improves iron levels should drop?

### SeaHorse — 2015-07-05T03:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170858>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170856)
>
> and giving away RED CELL mass is not smart either.............if your paying attn.
>
> Iron levels outside of RBC mass is a problem.
>
> RBC mass or Hb or HCT is not the same thing.

Got it. That's why I said a mechanical way....one suggested by most doctors. Doesn't deal with the issue. Good timing about RBC stuff that you've been writing about.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T03:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170859>

not should they do.......ferritin is a function of too many protons and not enough electrons.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-05T05:32:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-high-iron-level-bad.16170/post-170865>

Thanks everyone. Now I have some references on where to look.

On the report it didn't say it was a concern but it alerted both me and my doctor to look into it further. Here is what the report stated:

"It is generally agreed that the hair iron level is not reflective of body stores, whether or not the distribution pattern for essential elements is normal or abnormal. Here, in the face of an abnormal distribution pattern, a high iron level does not carry any special significance."

My ferritin level last year was 155 micrograms/L (155 ng/ml.) I just googled "iron reference ranges" and I came up with:

Male : 12- 300 ng/ml

Female: 12 - 150 ng/ml

(reference - [http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003490.htm](http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003490.htm))

So it looks like it may be okay. I'm going to check my HS CRP and vitamin D3 level next week, plus my iron level again.

# Thread: When the Student is Ready the Teacher will Appear
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-student-is-ready-the-teacher-will-appear.16171/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimizedprimate — 2015-07-05T01:45:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-student-is-ready-the-teacher-will-appear.16171/post-170848>

Hi Everyone,

My name is Luis Arauz. I live in Chicago, IL with my wife Kim and daughter Isabel (5). My wife is Kimeka on the forums [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/kimeka.13731/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/kimeka.13731/)

I've been a huge science geek since I was a kid, my education took me on the path of physics, genetics, quantum physics, electronics, robotics computers, network security & computer hacking. I also became very interested in business/marketing optimization. I'm a natural learner and constantly fall down rabbit holes of research. Other than wanting to increase brain function, I never really ever thought too much about biology and chemistry.

Then in 2009 that all changed, Kim had not been a picture of health for as long as Ive known her (neither was I for that matter), but this was different, she was now dealing with (weight gain, hashimoto's, high BP, heart failure, low energy, sleep apnea, heart burn and vomiting white fluid at night to name a few symptoms.) she ended up in the hospital a few times and even had to deliver our daughter through c-section.

One thing I noticed quickly is that none of the doctors were interested in her medical history outside of any medications she was taking and her blood pressure and she wasn't getting much better.

So I decided to apply my background in science, research and computers to see if I could get to the root of what was going on.

I quickly started learning about how backwards our modern life, nutritional and medical recommendations really are. I got deep into things like Paleo, Crossfit, Bio-hacking, self-quantification. We followed many of the Paleo and biohacking "super stars" in the blog-o-sphere.

Kim definitely got better, but as many of you can imagine she started to plateau and even go backwards a bit, as her supplement regiment got higher and higher. So, we dug deeper into things like AIP, Ketosis, 23&me, ASI tests, leaky gut, I even got certified as a Functional Diagnostic Nutritionist.

But everything was either contradicting each other, was really expensive to maintain or could not give satisfactory explanations and some as I started going deeper into the science were just as bad as the woo woo stuff out there (The Paleo diet isn't even the Paleo diet). So many pieces of the puzzle were still missing.

So as I started thinking laterally, and diving into mitochondria, epigenetics, methylation, etc I discovered Jack through various podcasts and the doors blew off the hinges, all my knowledge and interest in physics came rushing back into my new passion for biology and human optimization.

Finally someone was making all of the connections in quantum physics, physics, chemistry, electronics and biology that I knew were there, but didn't fully grasp. Now I'm able to really get to the root of how we are all put together, powered, and programmed. and I hope to be able to help spread that message to others. Thank you for that Jack.

And I also now need to reconcile with some of the stuff out there that might not be was woo woo as I though it was, they may have been right but for the wrong reasons. Again thanks for that too Jack.

Now I am doing my best to help Kim navigate through all of this, so she can decide how to best optimize her personal environment in order to express her best phenotype.

Personally, I don't have any major health issues, all the more reason why I want to build up my resources, vital reserves and structure in order optimize my performance, and reach levels others think is impossible.

I'm also working on building my business ([www.optimizedprimate.com](http://www.optimizedprimate.com)) as a high performance and optimization coach for executives and entrepreneurs, but I want to do it the right way, and not have people dependant on supplements to get well (maybe if they want to further enhance performance, once they are structurally sound.) optimal health and wellness is everyone's birthright, but some people want to play at an even higher level.

I'm looking forward to getting to know all of you and helping you in anyway I can on your road to optimal and beyond.
I'm grateful to be part of this galaxy of starfish.

Be Your Optimal Selves
Luis Arauz

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T02:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-student-is-ready-the-teacher-will-appear.16171/post-170851>

welcome. Over the past ten years, it’s become clear to me that biology doesn’t know what we are yet. And now the truth is like a ray of light constantly making me squint my eyes.
The secret is not developing supreme confidence so much as it is recognizing self doubt , and never letting it dictate your actions or behavior. Getting it done, isn't the same thing as getting it right, and I am about getting it right.

### optimizedprimate — 2015-07-05T02:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-student-is-ready-the-teacher-will-appear.16171/post-170852>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170851)
>
> welcome. Over the past ten years, it’s become clear to me that biology doesn’t know what we are yet. And now the truth is like a ray of light constantly making me squint my eyes.
> The secret is not developing supreme confidence so much as it is recognizing self doubt , and never letting it dictate your actions or behavior. Getting it done, isn't the same thing as getting it right, and I am about getting it right.

Thanks Jack, I've always gotten in trouble for wanting to get the science right. So bring it on.

# Thread: Why is a seasonal approach the key in a reversal?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-a-seasonal-approach-the-key-in-a-reversal.16172/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T02:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-a-seasonal-approach-the-key-in-a-reversal.16172/post-170853>

Why are summer time high fat diets not a great thing to do chronically across all seasons? High fat diets force the use of mitochondria because they are oxidative powerhouses. But what if your mitochondria functionally suck? If that is the case you must first optimize the things a mitochondria uses to recycle. That is O2 and UV light collection to maintain NAD+ levels at cytochrome 1. What does that mean if you have real bad mito-nuclear coaptation and a poor redox? What does it mean for good coaptation? High carbohydrate diets provide more energy by fermentation, without causing humans actually needing to use their badly functioning mitochondria. Bacteria tend to like this situation. Where are bacteria in you? How to do bacteria and your mitonuclear coapatation work together? Here is another clue: those with mitochondrial diseases who have "adopted, used, and advocated" long term ketogenic diets have been known to collapse into coma because their damaged mitochondria can't provide enough energy needed for a conscious state. They actually cannot function neurologically without help from fermentation. Environmentally broken Paleo advocates use and advocate carbs for that reason. Environmentally broken LCHF folks just get sicker as time evolves because they cannot replace DHA fast enough to offset their environment they allow their mitochondria to face. Some might begin to advocate temporary nicotine use when they are completely stalled and spinning their wheels.........Why might nicotine work? What other cofactors would make nicotine work even better? Nicotine works best with good O2 consumption and strong natural sunlight. Most nicotine users smoke so they never see the optimized effect of nicotine because of co-morbid lung disease from smoking. Most people who are sick rarely go out in the sun because their doctors or their beliefs keep them ignorant of basic physics. So how might nicotine work when O2 and UV light are optimized? Nicotine slightly increases glucose levels to give a small superoxide pulse to make new mitochondria and dig yourself out of the hole called mitochondria senescence. You cannot get the superoxide pulse without the O2 or the UV exposure. Did you know that DHA in your RBC's in skin arterioles 7-10 AM allows you to absorb even more UV light? If you're anemic, hypoxic, or diabetic, your fighting an uphill battle but you can reverse it if you get the details correct. Few people have that discipline. Some of us do. That is why we share what we have learned. All people as they age, they acquire this same kind of mito-nuclear coaptation dysfunction.......the major difference in an older person is the timescale of the decline is slower and results in diseases of aging instead of the neolithic disease today's modern humans get 20-40 years old. My PSA today. This ties up a lot of ends on this forum. The physics is now past us to describe this complex process.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T03:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-a-seasonal-approach-the-key-in-a-reversal.16172/post-170855>

SEASONAL change is built into the great blueprint.........FOR a reason. You just got the reason above^^^^. It is called Mitophagy.

### Valerie — 2015-07-05T04:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-a-seasonal-approach-the-key-in-a-reversal.16172/post-170862>

# Thread: Josh T's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josh-ts-optimal-journal.16173/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh Thomas — 2015-07-05T14:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josh-ts-optimal-journal.16173/post-170882>

Well I've I finally progressed from the meet and greet to starting a journal. I am sure this will be an exciting new chapter in my journey to optimal.

As a bit of background info I was born in Melbourne, Australia by natural birth but was 6 weeks premature and spent my first weeks of life in a humidity crib in Hospital. My mother was 40yrs old and father 37. I was severily underweight but my mother would express milk and travel daily by train to ensure I received it.
Through childhood I received the I often got viruses and would receive the various antibiotics prescribed and was overweight until my late teens. As I stated in the meet and greet section I was diognosed with Rheumatoid arthritis and although it turned my world upside down, this kickstarted my passion to learn what true health really meant. Within two years I was off my prescription meds but my weight had ballooned and was never far from the pain returning. Had chronic leaky gut and worked on it with a naturopath and subequently lost 12kgs.
A move to Queensland and further tweaking improved my health and I dropped more weight but I still felt I was flying blind with occasional pain still in my spine and feet.
Over the last 3-4 years I have gone from vegetarian to vegan to raw vegan to paleo and since discovering Jack last year now epi-paleo. Each time feeling better than I had before.
I got some Labs done neally 6 months ago and although a little outdated, they give a fairly good snapshot. At the time I had ok energy levels except mid afternoon where I would completely tank, ok sleep, 90kg with abdominal fat and over exercised (crossfit). Generally felt ordinary and ate a paleo diet with more carbs than I do now.

Cortisol sum 23 ( 23-42)
DHEA 13.64 (2-10)
Cortisol/DHEA ratio 1.7 (5.6)
Estradial 12.1 ( 1-3 )
Progesterone 91.2 (5-100)
Melatonon 31.2 (12-35)
Testosterone 40.6 ( 40-130)
Lipid peroxide 6.30 (1-7.50)
Urinary bile acid sulfates 7.90 (1-12)

Basically adrenal fatigue.
upside down C/D ratio
High E
Reasonable Melatonon
Terrible T
High oxidation
Sluggish Liver
At least thats how I have read it.

Since these labs and following advice from this site my sleep has improved slightly , my energy has increased but still carry abdominal weight. Libido has improved. Taking Jacks advice has been huge but I've got plently of holes in my game.
Looking forward to getting more extensive labs soon.

**Current enviroment**
40 years old, 5"10, 90kg
Good libido ( but apparantly could be better...lol)
sleep is ok but could be better. 7-8hrs interrupted by toddlers.
Live in a house 800m to the ocean in a semi builtup suburb on the Gold Coast
Drink alot of water but not always unfluoridated.
Turn power box off at night
Diet is Epi paleo but love coffee, dark chocolate and red wine and in context probably not good.
Use 650nm intranasel red laser upon waking
Mainly walk and ocean swim now with occasional sprint sessions and bodyweight exercises/natural movement.
Use ice vest and swim in my 17 degree C pool most days
Eat seafood/oysters most days
use blueblockers if watching TV at night or driving at night
Currently taking no Supps except nicotine for flying.
Make broths each week for gut healing.

My big problem is that I fly for a living and although have implimented alot regarding air travel from this group, know that I need to leave the airlines. Sleep in hotels 2 nights a week. Fly 6hrs east- west, then 6hrs west-east Redeye once a week. Hydration, nicotine and grounding help but I am losing a ton of electrons. Think I need Ubiquinol as well.
Last year I finished studies as Intergrative Health practitioner so am working hard at transitioning by the end of this year.
Am really looking forward to moving ahead and am so grateful for this group and your imput. I've got alot of work to do am am excited for the future.

> Sorry Jack what is H/H?

From [http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx) :

> 12\. **Your hemoglobin and hematocrit, called an H/H level**, can tell you if you have a lowered redox potential (you do if you are anemic). If you have surgery for any reason at all, you can actually ask and receive a copy of your anesthetic record. Check to see what the trend of your pulse ox was on “room” air before surgery and what it was on controlled O2 air by the anesthesia machine when you were under the control of the anesthetic. I did this when I had my knee surgery. You can learn more about the science of HRV on wikipedia (I’ve read this page and agree with it with respct to HRV). Chronic hypoxia does not affect heart rate that much, but it has a dramatic negative effect on HRV. This is why obstructive sleep apnea and COPD are so bad for health.

# Thread: Daugher with EPP Erythropoietic Protoporphyria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### calishaye — 2015-07-05T17:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-170893>

My daughter was diagnosed with EPP when she was very young (she is now 18). EPP causes the enzyme porphyrin to build up in the body as the liver does not flush it out as it should. People with EPP are very photosensitive and cannot be out in the sun, and some cannot even tolerate indoor lighting. My daughter luckily has a very mild form of it and can tolerate small amounts of sun when the UV rays are low. They say it is genetic but no one in either side of the family has it or has shown symptoms of it.

When exposed to too much light, the capillaries can burst and cause swelling and immense pain.

Her vit D level was 50 when we had it checked a few weeks ago. She does not supplement with Vit D right now, but is eating foods with Vit D in it and taking fermented cod liver oil. Her liver function tested good too.

I'm wondering what it is about this disease that causes the body to allow this enzyme to build up. The sun is a necessary component to our existence and for the body to allow a situation where it cannot tolerate something that is primordial doesn't make sense to me. I'm wondering what the link is between the sun and porphyrin. Is it because they don't enough heme? light is primordial so where is the breakdown in this case? Is it not enough DHA, circadian clock being off? Miscommunication somewhere? Or maybe a mutation that cannot be changed.

Anyone else out there with EPP or have family members with EPP?

Here is a link to the EPP Foundation website:

[http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP](http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP)

Jill

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T22:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-170950>

de-photosensitization may help her or specific color therapy might. Has she ever tried red light on her skin? Blue's and Purple's would make her bad........but I bet red would be interesting.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T22:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-170951>

Most new evidence has suggested that the RBC cell membrane is a primary site of the photohemolytic effect of long ultraviolet light in EPP red cells. This tells me DHA and its competitors are having a tug of war in her CM and DHA is not winning because of excessive blue light exposure.............In EPP,
before photohemolysis occurs there is a decline in cell membrane sulfhydryl groups (Tensegrity 7 alert) and a loss in activity of the cell membrane enzyme acetylcholinesterase. This enzyme is where the PUFA action is to me. Why? The rate of decline in enzyme activity is accelerated by the addition of linoleic acid, an unsaturated fatty acid, but not by palmitic acid, a saturated fatty acid. Tells me she needs to limit omega 6's, try red light, and eat a very saturated DHA laden diet as a hack. Linoleic acid is an 18 carbon chain PUFA and only has one cis double bond.........tells me that this disease does not let DHA in the CM over linoleic acid because of the the effect of certain light frequencies.........I bet red light might put DHA in her RBC's and if so she should tolerate sun and indoor light better.

This is like having an internal third degree burn............EPP.

And ironically 3rd degree burns have shown amazing ability to be healed from topical blue light exposure which is why I am wondering if red light might really help her.........that is a hack you could do with a flash light or simple amber bulb.

### calishaye — 2015-07-06T00:54:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-171018>

We have not tried red light therapy but it's something I'm sure she will be open to trying. She was diagnosed when she was just a baby before any major blue light exposure. Heat always seemed to make it worse. She can be out in the sun in winter, maybe because the UV rays are low then, but even taking a long hot shower (I keep telling her that's no good) will cause the red marks on her arms (capillaries bursting??). Wouldn't this mean not much DHA? If so, what you stated about DHA would be true. I'll check out the Tensegrity 7 blog post.

She's always had irrational fears and phobias. Very sensitive to many things. She can hear the buzzing from high tension wires and doesn't like being around many people as the energies are too much for her.

She dislikes fish very much but I am trying to introduce it to her in ways that she will at least eat it. She is supplementing with fermented cod liver oil/butter oil.

Yes, it is like an internal burn.

Jill

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T00:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-171019>

FYI.........she got the blue light transgenerationally

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T00:57:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-171020>

That means she has EHS too then if she hears the wires going......another lack of DHA and UV light sign

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T00:57:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-171021>

Her dislike of fish is a big tell why she is the way she is

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T00:59:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daugher-with-epp-erythropoietic-protoporphyria.16175/post-171023>

"I'm wondering what the link is between the sun and porphyrin" Porphyrin rings all absorb all frequencies of UV light.........this is why porphyrin forms the ring of chlorophyll in plants and the ring in hemoglobin in RBC's in animals.

# Thread: Intuitive mindset vs Science
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-07-05T20:57:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170931>

Hello.
That's something that's been on my mind for a long time.. how much do you really need to know?
How far from the truth would you be if you just tried to think and act like an early man, at the same time being very sceptic about synthetic changes to the environment?
Knowing what to do and doing it is what matters most.
Obviously if you need a phd in both physics and biology to be healthy, only 1 person in a million will survive.
Now the science is awesome, and is clearly interesting as a hobby.
What both sides seem to have is open minded scepticism.
The scientist obviously will know the why better (but why can always go deeper), willl know how to fine tune things better depending on the situiation.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T21:09:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170932>

Human Intuitive Mindset vs. Science PSA: Intuitive is a thought process missing connectivity of consciousness to wisdom only.........the middle man is not needed in this exchange of the intuitiveness is strong. When it is not strong one defaults to dogma. The intuitive FEEL they are correct, they just cannot KNOW they are.

Man evolved among animals with more power in their cranium but not necessarily with the wisdom to use that intelligence optimally. Lady evolution gave females a deep playbook of intuition and femininity. Smart males have seen and keenly observed females who use this combination of gifts properly, are enabled to mystify the mind of most men I’ve known, including the one I see in the mirror.

### prAna303 — 2015-07-05T22:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170944>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=170931)
>
> Hello.
> That's something that's been on my mind for a long time.. how much do you really need to know?
> How far from the truth would you be if you just tried to think and act like an early man, at the same time being very sceptic about synthetic changes to the environment?
> Knowing what to do and doing it is what matters most.
> Obviously if you need a phd in both physics and biology to be healthy, only 1 person in a million will survive.
> Now the science is awesome, and is clearly interesting as a hobby.
> What both sides seem to have is open minded scepticism.
> The scientist obviously will know the why better (but why can always go deeper), willl know how to fine tune things better depending on the situiation.

I strongly belive that most people would have some kind of issue with going with the so called gut feeling, why?

If one always live inside then being outside becomes a scaring thing... How would i react, what if i can not survive, the smallest thing becomes big problems.

When you always dress in tons of garments provided by modern creation we also shut down every kind of normal signaling. I use clothes to protect myself from technology... Glasses to filter bad light, clothes to not allow my sensors to react to fake light, list goes on. Normal way around would be to protect myself from environment. This also makes me wonder, we know that light from nature is good but what if we overdose one single system? The cells need light from all kind of seasons, high sun in summer and dead cold in winter.

Last night i had a discussion with some top performing athletes, i mean the ones that make gold in olympics. Some of them have always had thoughts why they always meet me in the nature on the brink to naked, summer and winter. They dress up to protect themselves from sun and cold because they have been learned that, with their time out in nature would harm them... What this will end in is going to be funny to witness, just told my ideas that rhymes with Jacks science.

The data that makes us make turns in life is so very disturbed today, why? With a global net, known as internet we forget that the world is not global in time, even if we can use it 24/7 does not mean that we are made for it. One thing that works in, lets say another country right now can never suit me in a dead cold world. I have always gone out in the elements and looked if the data sync with the world around me.

Now most must collect ideas and sync them with a real environment. Carbs there does not mean carbs here even if some make it a trend.

Science to me is a passion but not anything that makes my life decisions, it can guide me and make me wonder deeper.

Yesterday i laid on a rocky beach, watching a clear blue sky make clouds, water turning and going from still to vivid and back again, that made me think about why water sticks to the earth instead of just flowing around everywhere.

Yesterday i watched birds fly and started to think that they do not, they perhaps surf on magnetic fields, bouncing around with switches in turns within thousands of seconds. Is this why they can follow a beachline with a great focus? Is the fish they see another magnetic field they just sense, not seeing and therefor can attack with good luck. Is this why they do not crash into things that often, they bounce of that magetic field?

I think we all become a new breed of men and women of science if we look around and see things in the natural light. Do it resonate with your senses? Eye sight is just one of many...

Science for me is the art of living. Every single one of us must become our own Einsteins, our own Arvo Pärts, our own Sylvie Guillems to create our own world that rhymes with the bigger one. Society today do not do this.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T22:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170946>

Science for me is the art of living. Every single one of us must become our own Einsteins, our own Arvo Pärts, our own Sylvie Guillems to create our own world that rhymes with the bigger one. Society today do not do this.

^^^^awesome

### prAna303 — 2015-07-05T22:19:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170949>

Just some things i saw from the local waterholes yesterday.

\- Everyone has a smartphone, surprised?

\- At a given point of time, everything became a parody.

\- A group of old "friends", guys. Only talked about their companies, technology and money.

\- Girls everywhere.

\- A society where i feel i do not belong but i stayed there, why?

Even if i would love to mingle with dancers and creators of classical music i slowly came to a thought. I can make this weird and wonderful new world by myself... With a glass of rum, a fine cigar, alone in this crowd, in one corner just watching and seeing it playing like a performance, things happen. In four hours i got chatted up with girls five times, often in groups of two to five. Given five numbers to which i told them that i do not use phones anymore but thanked them, only resulting in more connection...

This made me think about those japanese people that do not have physical contact anymore, why would they want to turn this down? The movie Lost in translation playes in my mind...

What is human missing most now? To be seen by other humans without a disturbing light... You can make any person feel very stressed with just the glimse, just the silent meaning of a look... If they feel bad, not your problem, most will react according to their own status... Of course, if you are messed up by yourself you only become a creep but then again, anyone that is natural will never be creepy.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T22:41:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170955>

Our future is as blank as a plane sheet if you don't have an eagle's eye for nature, that's why you should climb your knowledge to perch and obtain a vantage point to get a good perspective view at your future. Sometimes that vantage point won’t be high up, but might be _atomically atuned._

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-05T23:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intuitive-mindset-vs-science.16176/post-170972>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=170931)
>
> Hello.
> That's something that's been on my mind for a long time.. how much do you really need to know?
> How far from the truth would you be if you just tried to think and act like an early man, at the same time being very sceptic about synthetic changes to the environment?
> Knowing what to do and doing it is what matters most.
> Obviously if you need a phd in both physics and biology to be healthy, only 1 person in a million will survive.
> Now the science is awesome, and is clearly interesting as a hobby.
> What both sides seem to have is open minded scepticism.
> The scientist obviously will know the why better (but why can always go deeper), willl know how to fine tune things better depending on the situiation.

Post title: "Intuitive mindset vs Science"

This is a false dichotomy, Thomas. (The interesting difference is the difference between the method of science and that of the humanities - but that's a different question, and perhaps I'd best not start that hare now.)

Science can, and does, involve intuition. Or, at any rate, good scientists probably need it. Think of Kekule dreaming of snakes biting their tails. There are many examples of this kind of thing.

It seems to me that you are thinking of science as "knowledge" and I think that's a bit too broad and vague. (There are many different types of knowledge and not all of them have to do with science. Take, for example, a knowledge of the history of fashion.) I think the most helpful way to think of science is as a process of deriving general laws, and hence as being involved with _prediction_:

> Popper was profoundly impressed by the differences between the allegedly ‘scientific’ theories of Freud and Adler and the revolution effected by Einstein's theory of relativity in physics in the first two decades of this century. The main difference between them, as Popper saw it, was that while Einstein's theory was highly ‘risky’, in the sense that it was possible to deduce consequences from it which were, in the light of the then dominant Newtonian physics, highly improbable (e.g., that light is deflected towards solid bodies—confirmed by Eddington's experiments in 1919), and which would, if they turned out to be false, falsify the whole theory, nothing could, even _in principle_, falsify psychoanalytic theories.

[http://plato.stanford.edu/entries/popper/#SciKnoHisPre](http://plato.stanford.edu/entries/popper/#SciKnoHisPre)

Einstein could _predict_ how light would behave.

You say:

> How far from the truth would you be if you just tried to think and act like an early man, at the same time being very sceptic about synthetic changes to the environment?

I think the problem with this is that there is no particular reason to "act like an early man". And there are features of those societies we perhaps wouldn't want to emulate: they seem to have had fairly high levels of inter-personal violence for a start.

The original notion of the Paleo movement was not to "do as Grok would have done" but simply to pay attention to the conditions under which our species emerged and flourished. As Robb Wolf puts it, the evolutionary viewpoint is used as a heuristic not as something that delivers a ready-made answer. This is something the press really has trouble grasping.

I'll give you a concrete example. I think it's always useful to try to be concrete. So think of gravity. Life on this planet evolved under the force of gravity. It's a basic condition. This isn't a case of _doing_ anything an early man would have done. It's more a matter of noticing what the conditions of our species' life have been.

And notice that when you have weightless conditions - as on a spacecraft - there are consequences:

[http://science.nasa.gov/science-news/science-at-nasa/2001/ast01oct\_1/](http://science.nasa.gov/science-news/science-at-nasa/2001/ast01oct_1/)

Now that doesn't mean that it might not be interesting and potentially useful to play around with weightlessness on a temporary basis - as with a flotation tank - interesting podcast on that:

[http://notjustpaleo.com/kevin-johnson-float-tank-experiences/](http://notjustpaleo.com/kevin-johnson-float-tank-experiences/)

It's just that where there are conditions of life that bore upon us across all or most of our evolutionary past we have to pay attention to what happens when we change those conditions.

### Bill1 — 2015-07-17T17:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-rid-of-my-root-canal-for-heart-health-etc.16177/post-172181>

> [billy said:](/goto/post?id=172170)
>
> My Endodontist filled the root canal with gutta percha.
>
> "On May 12,2010 we completed a root canal on tooth #2 This was filled with **gutta percha**. I am happy to provide you with an MSDS for this product if you want one. There is no evidence this product would produce an increase cholesterol."

it's complicated. depends if you were tested for Lyme? it loves to hide in teeth/jaw. There's a few Dentists who know how to investigate your damaged tooth extraction for NICO **(Neuralgia-inducing cavitational osteonecrosis)**. If this is discovered then cavation surgery is indicated. It's removal of the damaged jaw bone & replaced with donor tissue. Google the best dentist in America: **Christopher Hussar, DO, DDS** \- his work is very impressive.

If you can located a doc who does **EVA Testing** - it's very accurate to see what's damaged, what you're allergic to and what interference fields there might be. Often injuries and scars create an electrical interference field. Neural Therapy is key to fixing this. C-section scars, surgery sites, dental work ect.... all create this.

.

### Penny — 2015-07-17T23:56:12+0300

might want to check the credibility of a report before you post it. So called Dr Barrett commits fraud under oath:

Quackwatch, [www.quackwatch.com](http://www.quackwatch.com/), (originally Lehigh Valley Committee Against Health Fraud, Inc.) is an AMA (American Medical Association) shill that took over from the Coordinating Conference on Health Information (CCHI) and the AMA's propaganda department called the Committee on Quackery when it had to disband ref: Lisa. It only promotes and defends Allopathic (drugs) medicine while attacking non-Allopathic.
Of Stephen Barrett of [www.quackwatch.com](http://www.quackwatch.com/) and [www.ncahf.com](http://www.ncahf.com/) (National Council Against Health Fraud--another shill for the AMA, American Medical Association):
biased and unworthy of credibility" in court of law, Jan 2007
Barrett also had said that he was a legal expert even though he had no formal legal training.
Barrett conceded that he was not a Medical Board Certified psychiatrist because he had failed the certification exam.
Barrett had filed similar defamation lawsuits against almost 40 people across the country within the past few years and had not won one single one at trial.
During the course of his examination, Barrett also had to concede his ties to the AMA, Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and Food & Drug Administration (FDA).
The MOST IMPORTANT thing to understand about Stephen Barrett is that... It is common knowledge that Stephen Barrett has been Officially Declared by the US Court System, in a PUBLISHED Appeals Court Decision (NCAHF v King Bio), to be 'Biased, and unworthy of credibility'...What that statement means, in layman's terms, simply, is that it is common knowledge that OFFICIALLY - NOTHING HE SAYS CAN BE LEGALLY RELIED UPON.
In a Canadian lawsuit (see below) Barrett admitted to the following:

The sole purpose of the activities of Barrett & Baratz are to discredit and cause damage and harm to health care practitioners, businesses that make alternative health therapies or products available, and advocates of non-allopathic therapies and health freedom.
Stephen Barrett testifies for money. He claims he's an "expert" in virtually everything. Those "expert witness" fees seem to be a significant part of Barrett's existence.
[www.quackwatch.org](http://www.quackwatch.org/), healthwather.net, the "quackbuster" operation is a conspiracy. It is a propaganda enterprise, one part crackpot, two parts evil. It's sole purpose is to discredit, and suppress, in an "anything goes" attack mode, what is wrongfully named "Alternative Medicine." It has declared war on reality. The conspirators are acting in the interests of, and are being paid, directly and indirectly, by the "conventional" medical-industrial complex.

They are running a monopoly and they will lie, cheat and steal to keep it that way...Every day, consumers are becoming more educated about the benefits of holistic and alternative methods. This is something that the medical establishment obviously fears and wants to crush with false propaganda. --Dr Eva Snead
**Barrett's Funding - TOP SECRET...**
Barrett was cornered in a Federal case in the State of Oregon not long ago, and asked about his income. He testified that over the past two years he made a TOTAL of $54,000.
How then does he afford to carry on fourteen (14) separate legal actions at one time?
If each legal action cost him $100,000, that would come to 1.4 million dollars ($1,400,000).
How do you squeeze $1.4 million out of a $54,000 total income?
Good question...
**Failed MD Stephen Barrett**
**Stephen Barrett - Professional Crackpot...**
The Internet needs health information it can trust. Stephen Barrett doesn't provide it...
Barrett is one of those people whose ambitions and opinions of himself far exceeds his abilities. Without ANY qualifications he has set himself up as an expert in just about everything having to do with health care - and more.
And this from a man who is a professional failure.
Records show that Barrett never achieved any success in the medical profession. His claim to being a "retired Psychiatrist" is laughable. He is, in fact, a "failed Psychiatrist," and a "failed MD."
The Psychiatric profession rejected Barrett years ago, for Barrett could NOT pass the examinations necessary to become "Board Certified." Which, is no doubt why Barrett was, throughout his career, relegated to lower level "part time" positions.
Barrett, we know, was forced to give up his medical license in Pennsylvania in 1993 when his "part-time" employment at the State Mental Hospital was terminated, and he had so few (nine) private patients during his last five years of practice, that he couldn't afford the Malpractice Insurance premiums Pennsylvania requires.
In a job market in the United States, where there is a "doctor shortage," Stephen Barrett, after his termination by the State mental Hospital, couldn't find employment. He was in his mid-50s at the time. He should have been at the top of his craft - yet, apparently, he couldn't find work.
It is obvious, that, after one humiliation after another, in 1993 Barrett simply gave up his medical aspirations, turned in his MD license, and retreated, in bitterness and frustration, to his basement.
It was in that basement, where Barrett took up "quackbusting" - which, in reality, means that Barrett attacks "cutting-edge" health professionals and paradigms - those that ARE achieving success in their segment of health care.
And there, in "quackbusting" is where Barrett finally found the attention and recognition he seems to crave - for, a while, that is, until three California Judges, in a PUBLISHED Appeals Court decision, took a HARD look at Barrett's activities, and declared him "biased, and unworthy of credibility."
Bitterness against successful health professionals is Barrett's hallmark. To him they're all "quacks." In this, his essays are repetitive and pedestrian.
Barrett, in his writings, says the same things, the same way, every time - change the victim and the subject, and still you yawn your way through his offerings. It's like he's filling out a form somebody gave him...
Take an overactive self importance, couple it with glaring failure and rejection in his chosen profession, add a cup of molten hatred for those that do succeed, pop it in the oven - and out comes Stephen Barrett - self-styled "expert in everything."
Barrett, we know, along with his website, is currently named, among other things, in a racketeering (RICO) case in Federal Court in Colorado.

[http://www.encognitive.com/node/1213](http://www.encognitive.com/node/1213)

.

This makes oxyhemoglobin polar magnetic, meaning it has a north and south pole. The O2 bound to it, is drawn to external magnetic fields while the oxygenated heme molecule itself repels them. This makes delivery of O2 easy from an energy standpoint because it is using magnetic flux power to complete the task. The O2 is drawn to tissues with high mitochondrial density because mitochondria have extensive electric currents that generate a magnetic fields and they draw paramagnetic substance to them. Moreover, mitochondria all have an ATPase with an Fo head that generates a magnetic field naturally because it rotates while protons and electrons are moving through it creating Lorentz magnetic forces. This is why oxygen is dropped off in tissues at a fundamental level.
Deoxyhemoglobin is more paramagnetic. The reason for this is its main goal is to be drawn back to the heart’s massive magnetic power to rebind with oxygen delivered from the lungs. This magnetic flux helps offset the pump pressures in the heart. This means deoxyhemoglobin will also be drawn to mitochondria that are making reduced O2. This also helps aid tissue deliver of O2. In neurosurgery, we use functional MRI and the paramagnetic signal of deoxyhemoglobin to delineate tissues well because it produces a stronger magnetic Resonance interaction in our scans. This is how I know I am correct. I look at people scans all day and see it in everyone. When it is present in everyone........you begin to realize what really is unfolding.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-07-06T03:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-visual-system-and-or-cerebellum-related-issue-hacks-plus-nmda-qs.16182/post-171047>

Hi all, long time no (meaningful) post :/

Wanted to inquire here about what is in the title of the thread. I wrote about these things in my journal, and suspect that these are a few of the root causes of my major neuro-sensory issues. I think these neuro-sensory issues are what then causes my supposed "psychological/psychiatric" deficits, such as anxiety.

I think that on a macroscopic level (tissue level - cerebellum, vestibular apparatus, etc) the problems then cause problems that are on a smaller scale, yet physiologically meaningful. For instance, these issues may be causing my Default Mode Network and/or Salience Network to function at a far less than optimal level.

Is anyone aware of any hacks, whether pharmacological, supplement based, dietary, physical/neurological (neurological pursuit drills and the like), that are cheap, which I can try for these issues?

I'm contemplating seeing some practitioners from a clinic which offers Interactive Metronome training, in addition to a more modern, updated form of Interactive Listening Therapy, but those are both moderately costly and time consuming. I definitely will pursue these paths, but I need something I can use now, even if it offers only acute relief.

Now, for the NMDA questions.

I also suspect that many of my issues are related to NMDA hypofunctioning and/or low acetylcholine. To make a long story short, I was once exposed to chemicals which simultaneously inhibit NMDA 'receptors' and diminish acetylcholine levels. Directly afterwards I started experiencing anxiety and brain fog, lack of new memory formation, etc. All things I never had problems with, and now have been experiencing for 4+ years.

Nicotine has choline relations -> nicotinic acetylcholine 'receptor' upregulation. Does it also increase choline in the neurotransmitter sense? ALCAR theoretically aids in both NMDA and choline related problems, but I am definitely hypothyroid, and I don't want ALCAR to mess with that further. Pregnenolone also causes NMDA 'receptor' upregulation and the like, but again, alters a variety of other hormones.

Any ideas on how to directly affect these processes in a safe manner?

Thanks for ANY input!

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-07-06T05:49:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-visual-system-and-or-cerebellum-related-issue-hacks-plus-nmda-qs.16182/post-171077>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/)
**"6.1. Inhalation of Hydrogen Gas**
Inhalation of hydrogen gas is a straightforward therapeutic method. Hydrogen gas can be inhaled by delivering hydrogen gas through a ventilator circuit, facemask or nasal cannula. Since inhaled hydrogen gas acts more rapidly, it may be suitable for defense against acute oxidative stress. "

**"6.7. Others**
Other reports had the following titles: Hydrogen therapy reduces apoptosis in neonatal hypoxia-ischemia rat model \[[92](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R92)\]; hydrogen gas reduced acute hyperglycemia-enhanced hemorrhagic transformation in a focal ischemia rat model \[[93](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R93)\]; hydrogen is neuroprotective and preserves cerebrovascular reactivity in asphyxiated newborn pigs \[[94](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R94)\]; beneficial effects of hydrogen gas in a rat model of traumatic brain injury via reducing oxidative stress\[[95](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R95)\]; beneficial effects of hydrogen gas against spinal cord ischemia-reperfusion injury in rabbits \[[96](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R96)\]; and **hydrogen protects vestibular hair cells from free radicals \[[97](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R97)\]."**

**"9.2. Hydrogen Bath**
H2 easily penetrates the skin and distributes throughout the body via blood flow. Thus, taking a warm water bath with dissolved H2 is a method of incorporating H2 into the body in daily life, especially in Japan. It takes only 10 minutes to distribute throughout the whole body, as judged by measuring hydrogen gas in expiration (unpublished results)."

"Actually, acarbose, which is an α-glucosidase inhibitor, markedly increased H2 production in volunteers. Thus, we propose that H2 produced by intestinal bacteria acts as a unique antioxidant and prevents cardiovascular events \[[139](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R139)\].

**10.4. Others**
Dietary turmeric induced H2 production from the intestinal bacteria \[[142](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R142)\], and lactulose was shown to be an indirect antioxidant ameliorating inflammatory bowel disease \[[143](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R143)\]."

Superconductivity record bolstered by magnetic data is now proven
Measurements show that hydrogen sulfide superconducts much closer to room temperature than other materials. Why should you care? H2S is a free radical. This means it has an unpaired electron. This means light can interact with this chemical at edges using the quantum Hall effect........what might this mean with respect to frequencies of light? Did you know that H2S is a signaling molecule in you right now? Hydrogen sulfide is produced in small amounts by some cells of the human body and has a number of biological signaling functions. Only two other such gases are currently known to act as signaling molecules in the human body: nitric oxide and carbon monoxide. Interesting huh? Hausman, Geoffrey M. Cooper, Robert E. (2000). "Signaling Molecules and Their Receptors". In NCBI bookshelf. The cell : a molecular approach (2nd ed.). Washington, D.C.: ASM Press. ISBN 087893300X.

# Thread: cytochrome 1 (read: basically another carb question)
