# Thread: Crohn's disease?  Do you know this man?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-25T03:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172915>

[http://content.time.com/time/covers/0,16641,19350610,00.html](http://content.time.com/time/covers/0,16641,19350610,00.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-25T03:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172916>

Dr. Emanuel Libman, suggested that toothpaste might be involved in the etiology of Crohn's disease, also known as regional ileitis. President Eisenhower had it and its incidence and prevalence is now explosive. Regional ileitis is an inflammation of the part of the small intestine where it connects with the large intestine called the colon. The area becomes scarred and an intestinal obstruction often develops. This may require emergency surgery to correct. No one paid much attention to Libman because it was assumed that the mammalian gastrointestinal tract does not absorb particles such as the aluminum and silicon found in toothpaste. But doctors at the University of London have found, in experiments on rats, that polystyrene particles are indeed absorbed into the veins of the intestinal tract and reach the liver. Polystyrene is a completely insoluble substance; if it can be absorbed by the intestine, there are probably few, if any, substances that cannot enter the venous or lymphatic circulations to some degree—including aluminum and silicon. Parenthetically, many pharmaceuticals—in fact, most of them—contain "insoluble" additives. If you are a chronic user of any medication, prescription or over-the-counter, you are a candidate for Crohn's disease. The doctors at the University of London who did the polystyrene experiments concluded: "Perhaps we should be more concerned about the fate of insoluble materials in toothpaste and pharmaceuticals which might be taken chronically." Another British group at St. Bartholomew's Hospital has added corroborating evidence by discovering aluminum, silicon, and titanium in the lesions of Crohn's disease.

Throw out all the toothbrushes and toothpaste in your bathroom. I realize that most of you are not willing to do this, so if you must brush, get three percent hydrogen peroxide from the drug store and mix it with baking soda. Make a thick solution with it, not a paste—and brush with that. If you insist upon brushing your teeth with store-bought toothpaste, don't use any that has fluoride in it.

And never drink water with F- in it.

### Jenny S — 2015-07-25T05:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172921>

So are you suggesting we shouldn't brush at all? I've been using powdered mix of green tea, bicarb & calcium.

### Joann — 2015-07-25T06:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172927>

dr Collins toothbrush is what I use. Lef toothpaste

### nicld — 2015-07-25T19:14:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172987>

I had a dentist who said it was not necessary to use toothpaste (I HATED mint toothpaste). Just the act of using the brush was enough.

### Lahelada — 2015-07-25T19:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-172988>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=172921)
>
> So are you suggesting we shouldn't brush at all? I've been using powdered mix of green tea, bicarb & calcium.

Green tea = mega fluoride unless steeped.

I just brush with only the toothbrush. Sometimes I add baking soda. toothpaste is long gone.
I am gonna be honest, I am not sure it's necessary to brush at all. Especially if you eat 1-2 a day like me, and eat only local seasonal food.

### Scompy — 2015-07-25T21:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crohns-disease-do-you-know-this-man.16602/post-173000>

I've been using baking soda and celtic salt for years now as toothpaste.

The problem I faced in the past was with my 6-year old. I got him using 'plackers' to get him into the habit of flossing off the plaque and also using a diluted salt/baking soda water on his toothbrush. Works great.

Regional ileitis is an inflammation of the part of the small intestine where it connects with the large intestine called the colon. The area becomes scarred and an intestinal obstruction often develops. This may require emergency surgery to correct. No one paid much attention to Libman because it was assumed that the mammalian gastrointestinal tract does not absorb particles such as the aluminum and silicon found in toothpaste. But doctors at the University of London have found, in experiments on rats, that polystyrene particles are indeed absorbed into the veins of the intestinal tract and reach the liver. Polystyrene is a completely insoluble substance; if it can be absorbed by the intestine, there are probably few, if any, substances that cannot enter the venous or lymphatic circulations to some degree—including aluminum and silicon. Parenthetically, many pharmaceuticals—in fact, most of them—contain "insoluble" additives. If you are a chronic user of any medication, prescription or over-the-counter, you are a candidate for Crohn's disease. The doctors at the University of London who did the polystyrene experiments concluded: "Perhaps we should be more concerned about the fate of insoluble materials in toothpaste and pharmaceuticals which might be taken chronically." Another British group at St. Bartholomew's Hospital has added corroborating evidence by discovering aluminum, silicon, and titanium in the lesions of Crohn's disease.
>

I realize that most of you are not willing to do this, so if you must brush, get three percent hydrogen peroxide from the drug store and mix it with baking soda. # Thread: Have a cold you want to get rid of?  Bio -hack a cold.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-25T03:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-172917>

Let me tell you about one of my bio hacks I did on my last cold in 2015 in the winter. I spent 15 minutes in AM sunlight naked. 7.5 minutes on your front and then 7.5 min on my back. Then put four ounces of 35 percent hydrogen peroxide (reagent grade) in a gallon of water. I then ran a cold humidifier in my bedroom all night with this mixture. My biohack had cold gone in the AM before I had to go to surgery.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-25T03:54:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-172918>

I did use a LED light on myself that night. It was in UV range. FYI.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-172996>

I am pretty sure modern lifestyle is the real cause of colds (or at least enormous lever) because it weakens our immune system like crazy.
Haven't caught a cold in many years. The last time? I woken up with an alarm clock(aka ruined sleep), and skipped breakfast, and was stressed in the morning. Now I know the only way to go(and what I do every morning) is to wake up when your brain tells you to, then take a looooooong breakfast very slowly half naked in the sun, then go meditate for 20+ minutes, then go on with daily activities.

### Sue-UK — 2015-07-26T13:45:55+0300

Surface chemistry of the skin, eye and gut ..... (Ubi 19). Surface of our individual planet? We have mass, gravity etc, were you recreating a healthy (winter) atmosphere? Is cells leaking UV the organism trying to repair their surface atmosphere as well as signalling? (I read that UV radiation in the night time atmosphere had been seen by satellite).

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-26T18:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-173074>

The atmosphere is also a bio plasma for sunlight. This is why 78% of it is filled with nitrogen. What does nitrogen do? It down powers sun light to make it usable for life below.

The dielectric constant describes the ability of a material to store energy, whereas, the loss-factor defines energy dissipation of a material. When microwaves are used on a living tissue it becomes dehydrated. Water has the highest dielectric constant in life at 78. When you microwave yourself or your food you are removing potential energy from your life equation.

Surface tension = surface free energy => lots of papers how how light and lasers affect wettability on surfaces as measured by work of cohesion/adhesion, contact angle, and the subsequent drop in interfacial tension. They're using it now for roll-to-roll processes and microfabrication. Check out Corona treatment. Been around since early '50s.

Guys like Robb Wolf have no clue how surface magnesium can possible help biochemical processes because he is ignorant of surface chemistry. Soon you will see that all surface chemistry begins with how light interacts or does not interact with the atomic lattice in your skin first.........that interaction determines what is possible or impossible in the cells below.

Biochemistry is 100% adaptable to the surface interaction of light above. When that light is fake light opposed to sunlight the results are a higher ubiquitin marking rate in cells below.

### Inger — 2015-07-26T18:31:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-173078>

I know magnesium on the skin works wonders.... if I get heart palps... they disappear in a heart beat when I use magnesium oil, pretty cool

### Penny — 2015-07-31T22:53:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-173642>

Magnesium oil is the only reason I can walk...  Thanks to Dr. K, of course I was plagued with achilles tendonitis and plantar fasciitis - had no idea it was dehydration... and blood sugar issues - I also rub Epsom salts on my body in the nice cold shower and my skin is so soft... hydration is key...

### Scompy — 2015-09-08T07:29:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-176718>

Hmm, curious now. With the Keto-diet, I'm often lacking salts. I'm very active, often playing competitive soccer matches. On the weekends, I often spend 2-3 hours doing pretty grueling yardwork and loose lots of salts. On those nights in particular if I don't eat a higher-salt dinner, I find that in the middle of the night, my legs will cramp. It's always my 2nd toe wanting to bend down. I've actually gotten up in the middle of the night to drink some salt-water if a cramp comes on, and this fixes it. Now I'm wondering if rubbing Mg on the legs could also help me (or make it worse) for a foot cramp. I'm actually thinking it's low sodium and/or potassium, since the salt-water usually works well for my calf/foot cramping.

I also just learned this potential solution from wikipedia pressure point for the calf : "Gentle [stretching](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stretching) and [massage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Massage), putting some pressure on the affected leg by walking or standing, or taking a warm bath or shower may help to end the cramp.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cramp#cite_note-nytimes-ray-8) If the cramp is in the calf muscle, pulling the big toe gently backwards will stretch the muscle and, in some cases, cause almost immediate relief. A relief trigger acupressure point is located on the upper lip, directly beneath the nose. Pressure with the finger tip, with massage pressure will cause a calf cramp to disappear after 45-70 seconds of finger pressure\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] (The quicker pressure is applied, the faster pain is relieved)."

### Joe Gavin — 2015-09-08T16:52:33+0300

A relief trigger acupressure point is located on the upper lip, directly beneath the nose. Pressure with the finger tip, with massage pressure will cause a calf cramp to disappear after 45-70 seconds of finger pressure\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] (The quicker pressure is applied, the faster pain is relieved)."

I learned about the importance of salt from this book when I went keto in February. I got the keto-flu, googled it and then bought it. salt made all the difference. and some electrolyte mix called Electro-Mix. [http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Living/dp/0983490708](http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Living/dp/0983490708)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-09-10T06:32:56+0300

### Scompy — 2015-09-10T07:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-176899>

I've personally tried this in the past and it gave me a worse ear-ache, though it did help the cold a bit I think. My ears have always been very sensitive where I can breath air through them with my sinuses, so doing the hydrogen peroxide seemed to cause the sinus to ear opening to be 'always open' as if I created a larger hole that couldn't be shut down. That sensation went away a few days later. So I think if I'm gonna biohack in the future, it's going to be with a humidifier with peroxide.

On the weekends, I often spend 2-3 hours doing pretty grueling yardwork and loose lots of salts. On those nights in particular if I don't eat a higher-salt dinner, I find that in the middle of the night, my legs will cramp. It's always my 2nd toe wanting to bend down. I've actually gotten up in the middle of the night to drink some salt-water if a cramp comes on, and this fixes it. Now I'm wondering if rubbing Mg on the legs could also help me (or make it worse) for a foot cramp. I'm actually thinking it's low sodium and/or potassium, since the salt-water usually works well for my calf/foot cramping.
>
> I also just learned this potential solution from wikipedia pressure point for the calf : "Gentle [stretching](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stretching) and [massage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Massage), putting some pressure on the affected leg by walking or standing, or taking a warm bath or shower may help to end the cramp.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cramp#cite_note-nytimes-ray-8) If the cramp is in the calf muscle, pulling the big toe gently backwards will stretch the muscle and, in some cases, cause almost immediate relief. A relief trigger acupressure point is located on the upper lip, directly beneath the nose. Pressure with the finger tip, with massage pressure will cause a calf cramp to disappear after 45-70 seconds of finger pressure\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] (The quicker pressure is applied, the faster pain is relieved)."

I would try the mag oil plus epsom salt in the shower/bath - then potassium - remember, as calcium effluxes, potassium takes its place and thus you may become low in potassium - bind it with bicarbonate and alkalize yourself Small amounts - use potassium with care...

### Dennis Clark — 2017-05-03T18:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-a-cold-you-want-to-get-rid-of-bio-hack-a-cold.16603/post-213304>

I tucked away this thread for a "rainy day" - i.e., for a time when I might get one of my rare colds (or some such infection). It finally happened this past Sunday. Monday I got a small cold humidifier at Walgreen's, filled it with straight 3% food-grade peroxide (couldn't find 35%), and ran it all night. An infection that, in the past, would inevitably sink into my chest and cause havoc for 1-2 weeks, was mostly gone by morning. Morning and afternoon sunshine are already part of my daily light regimes. Even without exactly mimicking Jack's biohack, just coming close, this was a remarkable strategy. Best ever, in fact. Thanks, JK!

### Woodstock — 2026-01-22T18:17:34+0200

My biohack had cold gone in the AM before I had to go to surgery.

I am having to improvise. I am somewhere that I didn’t have access to a humidifier and needed it when I woke up coughing violently.

I felt better after waking up at 3am and standing over a pot of boiling water that I added hydrogen peroxide and essential oils to. I spent at least two hours hunched over that pot and just kept adding more as it boiled down. I used a lot of extra blankets and slept about 8 hours which is rare for me.

Thank you for this hack. It’s not quite the same but when you need to make do, I am the problem solving gal and improvised.

Not over this yet but I am currently in a damp environment with very low EMFs. And probably hacking up all the stuff that built up in toxic Colorado for the 6 months I was home.

Any other hacks to speed this along, please chime in! I feel much better as soon as the sun pops up over the trees.

I am in a remote area on the big island. I am going to do some research as I hear there might be some geothermal lava hot pools somewhere. If anyone knows where….

Othwise google AI is not horrible for such things.

The dielectric constant describes the ability of a material to store energy, whereas, the loss-factor defines energy dissipation of a material. When microwaves are used on a living tissue it becomes dehydrated. Water has the highest dielectric constant in life at 78. When you microwave yourself or your food you are removing potential energy from your life equation.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-30T03:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-light-and-surface-chemistry-determine-health-or-illness.16626/post-173474>

You need to understand the history of luminescence: Several investigators reported luminescence phenomena during the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, but it was British scientist Sir George G. Stokes who first described fluorescence in 1852 and was responsible for coining the term in honor of the blue-white fluorescent mineral fluorite (fluorspar). Stokes also discovered the wavelength shift to longer values in emission spectra that bears his name. Fluorescence was first encountered in optical microscopy during the early part of the twentieth century by several notable scientists, including August Köhler and Carl Reichert, who initially reported that fluorescence was a nuisance in ultraviolet microscopy. The first fluorescence microscopes were developed between 1911 and 1913 by German physicists Otto Heimstädt and Heinrich Lehmann as a spin-off from the ultraviolet instrument. These microscopes were employed to observe autofluorescence in bacteria, animal, and plant tissues. Shortly thereafter, Stanislav Von Provazek launched a new era when he used fluorescence microscopy to study dye binding in fixed tissues and living cells. However, it wasn't until the early 1940s that Albert Coons developed a technique for labeling antibodies with fluorescent dyes, thus giving birth to the field of immunofluorescence. By the turn of the twenty-first century, the field of fluorescence microscopy was responsible for a revolution in cell biology, coupling the power of live cell imaging to highly specific multiple labeling of individual organelles and macromolecular complexes with synthetic and genetically encoded fluorescent probes.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-20T16:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-light-and-surface-chemistry-determine-health-or-illness.16626/post-179959>

How the eye uses a bistable pigment to dance between UV and blue light.

"Photoreaction of mouse OPN5 was examined spectroscopically by irradiating with UV light (357 nm): The spectrum of mouse OPN5 was shifted to longer wavelengths peaking at 470 nm (Fig. 1A, Light; Fig. 1B, curve 1). This blue-absorbing photoproduct was stable in the dark and reverted by the subsequent illumination with orange light (>520 nm) into the original UV-absorbing state (Fig. 1B, curve 2), suggesting reversible photoreactions between the two states. The spectral change induced by the UV light irradiation (Fig. 1B, curve 1) was almost symmetrical to that induced by the orange light (curve 2) with respect to the baseline. This result indicates the bistable nature of mouse OPN5. Repeated cycles of the alternate irradiations with the UV and orange light caused back-and-forth conversions between the UV- and blue-absorbing species (Fig. S2D), although it was accompanied by a gradual decrease in the absorbance."

[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0026388](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0026388)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-27T00:26:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-quantum-biology.16627/post-173136>

EZ water is basically: 2H2O <-> \[H3O\]+ + \[OH\]-

Melanin mediated splitting of water is: 2H2O <-> 2H2 + O2

You're getting charged ions in EZ water, and this happens best with Red to IR light input, next to a hydrophilic surface, like a protein, which is basically everywhere in the brain.

You're getting H2 gas and O2 gas when you split water. This basically says that melanin produces the terminal electron acceptor O2 from water.

However, the researchers think that the main product of the reaction is the H2 gas, which we've discussed many times to be the best free-radical scavenger you can possibly have.

Note: H2 gas is also produced in the large intestine by gut bacteria. Drinking H2 infused water is also a good thing.

H2 gas is the only thing known to be able to deal with the hydroxyl free radical. You'd expect highly oxidative tissues like neural tissue to be the ones that are most likely to produce the hydroxyl radical, so it makes sense to put melanin there to scavenge any free radicals.

Of course, if you read and begin to believe Roeland Van Wijk, in his biophoton book (page 208 and 209), scavenging these free radicals leads to more light release, anywhere in the range 230nm (UV) to 1200nm (near IR). Either way, you get more UV and visible light to give melanin more water-splitting energy, or more IR light to give water around proteins the ability to charge separate into EZ water. This is how photosynthesis and chlorophyll work with light nitrogen and porphyrins. Animals do it differently by using different proteins to be the electron donors in the quantum transaction.

Melanin is a ubiquitous biological pigment with unusual physicochemical properties. It is **paramagnetic**, like O2 and DHA. This makes its interaction with UV light very interesting.

One of the most unusual properties of melanin as a biomacromolecule is its persistent electron spin resonance (ESR) signal, a clear indication of free radical centers present in the material.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-27T00:36:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-quantum-biology.16627/post-173141>

Melanin being a natural antioxidant that can protect pigment cells from oxidative stress by sequestration of redox-active metal ions, quenching of electronically excited states of photosensitizing dye molecules and singlet oxygen, and by scavenging of reactive free radicals. Melanin comes from tyrosine and DOPA.
****Aromatic amino acids like tyrosine**** are relatively ****nonpolar and act like photon traps for UV light.**** To different degrees, all aromatic amino acids absorb ultraviolet light. Tyrosine and tryptophan absorb more than do phenylalanine; tryptophan is responsible for most of the absorbance of ultraviolet light (ca. 280 nm) by proteins. Tyrosine is the only one of the aromatic amino acids with an ionizable side chain. Tyrosine is one of three hydroxyl containing amino acids.

G. Prota, M. D'Ischia, A. Napolitano, The chemistry of melanins and related metabolites, in "The Pigmentary System", ed. JJ Nordlund et al., Oxford University Press, 1988.

Melanin is a very complex absorbing material. Melanins from natural sources fall into two general classes: **_eumelanin_**
A black-to-dark-brown insoluble material found in human black hair and in the retina of the eye. Eumelanin is a very unusual bio-molecule.
The characteristic emission of eumelanin does not in any way mirror the absorption or excitation line shapes. This means there is not a clear symmetry that exists between the absorption and emission of light from these proteins. This completely violates one of the most basic rules of electronic spectroscopy, the so-called “mirror image rule” of organic chromophores (Lakowics, 1999). Computationally methods failed to predict the properties of melanin despite that works in others macromolecules. I have a sense the reason why this is the case is because eumelanin is a true quantum computer itself capable or infinite interactions with the electromagnetic spectrum that only decides what it will do based upon the environment this interaction occurs in.
**_pheomelanin_**
A yellow-to-reddish-brown alkali-soluble material found in red hair and red feathers. A variety of low molecular weight pheomelanins are called "trichromes".

The amount of photons absorbed by a melanosome is pertinent to oxidative reactions catalyzed by melanosomes exposed to blue or ultraviolet light. Is the skin a giant solar panel for the brain and heart where our mitochondria are?

Hemoglobin’s peak light absorption peaks at 280 mm 420nm 540nm and 580nm and has sharp cutoff at 600 nm since green and yellow wavelengths of krypton ion lasers peak at 531 mm and 568 mm respectively they almost perfectly match the absorption peaks of hemoglobin which is why they coagulate blood so well. Yellow is a complementary color to blue. Blue light can causes clotting of blood too, while red light makes RBC's less sticky to coagulation. Absorption spectra of the skin, aortic wall and cornea show us some interesting connections when we look at them all plotted together. In the visible range of light (sunlight). The absorption spectra of the skin is 20-30 times that of the cornea. The absorption spectra of the aortic wall exhibits VERY SIMILAR peaks as hemoglobin. This data has been published for years. No one seems to link it. Parrish and Anderson from 1983, Keijzer et al. in 1989 and Eichler and Seiler in 1991 have all shown this. The **_peak absorption_** of human melanin pigment occurs around 335nm, and absorption is almost completely attenuated for wavelengths longer than 700nm.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-27T00:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-quantum-biology.16627/post-173142>

After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer (melanin) is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state. The last one, may also promote a reaction leading to the singlet ground state by either non radiative decay or by radiative triplet decay. The radiative decays are called fluorescence and phosphorescence, respectively. Life time for fluorescence are on the order of nanosecond time scales where phosphorescence may last up to milliseconds or even seconds. According to Foote (1968), two alternative reaction mechanisms exist for the decay of the excited triplet state which are called Type 1 or 2 reactions. They are characterized by either the generation of free radicals (Type 1) reactions, or the transfer of excitation energy to oxygen molecules directly in a Type 2 reaction.

During Type 1 reactions the triplet state next interacts with a target molecule or protein, other than oxygen. Stop and think about the skin where light first hits melanin. Where is this signal sent? To the blood plasma in the surface vessels below the skin and stored in the EZ of water. Where does the EZ of water matter most? The MINOS around a mitochondria to stop proton flow from the matrix to the exterior to allow chemiosmosis to occur naturally using QED rules. You do not need an ion pump on a membrane to do as is currently believed. Stop and think about mitochondria now at cytochrome 1 and what should happen seasonally for mitophagy. Type 1 reactions result in the release of free neutral or ionized radicals. We use these to signal. Further reaction with triplet oxygne may also lead to the formation of hydrogen dioxide or superoxide. Where have you heard that before?
In Type 2 reactions, the triplet state of the photosensitizer directly interacts with molecular triplet oxygen (3O2) which is then transferred to an excited singlet state of (1O2). During this reaction the electronic spins are flipped of the electrons in oxygen.

Both reactions are designed to occur simultaneously but when the environment around a mitochondria is altered by pseudohypoxia the Respiratory Quotient can be altered dramatically. The reason for this is because all electrons in ECT are designed to move to oxygen for reduction. Their flow is 100% dependent upon the electron density, size of the respiratory chain proteins, and the amount of oxygen present at the end of the respiratory chain. This makes pseudohypoxia within the mitochondria a very powerful environmental variable because it controls the pull of electrons in ECT. Pull in ECT is measured in voltage. So pseudohypoxia means that voltages in mitochodria are falling and that means the magnetic field developed in mitochondria have diminished.

Excited singlet oxygen is very reactive and toxic. It leads to cellular oxidation, mitochondrial swelling and necrosis. Weishaupt in 1968 identified singlet oxygen as the TOXIC agent in photoactivation of tumor cells.

Carotenoids promote the toxic singlet oxygen to triplet oxygen which is harmless................Does the skin and retina have carotenoids? Yep. Did you know the RPE in the retina has more melanin in it than any other place in the human body? Think the RPE melanin and blue light are linked in some way? Damn straight. Carotenoids and melanin are step down mechanisms in the eye for the UV part of light.

Now.......did you know blue light increases singlet oxygen and decreases triplet? This is why blue light chronically is "no bueno"

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-27T00:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-quantum-biology.16627/post-173147>

Although the accumulation of excess body fat is a complex process with many variables, one variable could be light effect on circadian rhythms. Studies have shown that prolonged artifical daily light exposure (beyond the normal daily exposure we experience) can increase fat mass through an increase in adipocyte or fat cell size.

Kooijman et al. (2015) conducted a study where three groups of rats were placed under three different daily light conditions to test the effects on fat mass. Rats were exposed to 12, 16, and 24 hours of light each day while caloric intake remained constant. Kooijman discovered that rats exposed to 16 and 24 hours of light had an increase in fat mass despite there being no change in lean mass. Kooijman explained that this was likely due to the lights effects on circadian rhythms.

Circadian rhythms have been known to regulate a wide variety of physiological as well as metabolic processes in the body. These processes range from digestion and hormone release, to the onset of sleep and eating patterns.

While natural light during normal hours of the day is benefical, we should aim to decrease our exposure to artifical light at night, which most of us now get via the use of computers, TVs, cell phones, and tablets.

Biochemistry is 100% adaptable to the surface interaction of light above. When that light is fake light opposed to sunlight the results are a higher ubiquitin marking rate in cells below. ### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-28T04:52:40+0300

Optical properties of the melanin are central to its function. Melanin is the human chlorophyll; Melanin (S-cholesterol/D3/porphyrins) is to the animal kingdom as chlorophyll is to plant kingdom. Both molecules have the amazing capacity to split the water molecule at room temperature. But the process in chlorophyll is irreversible and melanin makes the process in two ways, the dissociation and reforming of the water molecule with the consequential release of energy.

The human body has a photosynthesis system composed of Light/ Melanin/ Water, arranged in order of abundance in nature. Melanin is able to absorb the full electromagnetic spectrum, from radio to gamma rays, in contrast, chlorophyll just absorbs blue and red light (400 to 600 nm). Oppositely melanin has the intrinsic property to split and reform the water molecule, and the reaction happens as follows:

**2H2O ↔ 2H2 + O2 + 4e

This means that melanin is a special kind of electrophile. CO2 and DHA also are electrophiles in biochemistry.
**
Melanin has the astonishing capacity to split the water molecule at room temperature, for instance, in the laboratory, it is necessary to heat up water at 2000°C in order the reach the separation of hydrogen from the oxygen atoms. Moreover, melanin supports the reunification of water molecule giving off liquid water and four high energy electrons. Those electrons can be used to do physical work and perform optical signaling. The reaction is not symmetrical in time because, like any other chemical reaction, depends on the surrounding environment.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-28T15:13:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-quantum-biology.16627/post-173294>

**Beyond mitochondria, what would be the energy source of the cell?**

**Currently, cell biology is based on glucose as the main source of energy. Cellular bioenergetic pathways have become unnecessarily complex in their eagerness to explain that how the cell is able to generate and use energy from the oxidation of glucose, where mitochondria play an important role through oxidative phosphorylation. During a descriptive study about the three leading causes of blindness in the world, the ability of melanin to transform light energy into chemical energy through the dissociation of water molecule was unraveled. Initially, during 2 or 3 years; we tried to link together our findings with the widely accepted metabolic pathways already described in metabolic pathway databases, which have been developed to collect and organize the current knowledge on metabolism scattered across a multitude of scientific articles. However, firstly, the literature on metabolism is extensive but rarely conclusive evidence is available, and secondly, one would expect these databases to contain largely the same information, but the contrary is true. For the apparently well studied metabolic process Krebs cycle, which was described as early as 1937 and is found in nearly every biology and chemistry curriculum, there is a considerable disagreement between at least five databases. Of the nearly 7000 reactions contained jointly by these five databases, only 199 are described in the same way in all the five databases. Thus to try to integrate chemical energy from melanin with the supposedly well-known bioenergetic pathways is easier said than done; and the lack of consensus about metabolic network constitutes an insurmountable barrier. After years of unsuccessful results, we finally realized that the chemical energy released through the dissociation of water molecule by melanin represents over 90% of cell energy requirements. These findings reveal a new aspect of cell biology, as glucose and ATP have biological functions related mainly to biomass and not so much with energy. Our finding about the unexpected intrinsic property of melanin to transform photon energy into chemical energy through the dissociation of water molecule, a role performed supposedly only by chlorophyll in plants, seriously questions the sacrosanct role of glucose and thereby mitochondria as the primary source of energy and power for the cells.**

**[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25645910](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25645910)**

Plants utilize the green pigment chlorophyll to absorb energy from sunlight to breakdown carbon dioxide into sugars. This is well known, and taught in probably every elementary school across the world. What is not as well known, is that the dark colored pigment called melanin found in the skin of humans and other mammals can absorb various types of electromagnetic radiation. In fact, melanin appears capable of absorbing a much broader spectrum of such radiation than chlorophyll. This was proven when scientists noticed various fungi growing in radioactive environments actually seemed to be thriving. Testing of these fungi proved the melanin producing their dark color was absorbing gamma radiation and converting it to energy. Mexican researcher Dr. Arturo Solís Herrera. an ophthalmologist, has been performing research on disorders of the human eye. Most of you know the eye contains the perfect black box radiator for light. He started discovering the importance of the pigment Melanin in the RPE in regards to eye conditions. This was when I got interested in what he was doing but not for his commercial interests. He found for some reason, unknown to him, melanin a bio-genie amine seemed to protect the tissues of the eye. After extracting and applying melanin therapeutically to the eyes of patients, they saw results their claims were impressive clinically.

As time went on it was discovered that melanin was actually collecting energy from electromagnetic radiation of the sun, and using it to split water atoms into hydrogen, oxygen, and four additional electrons. This process can also act in reverse because it is quantized. Such a reaction is not exothermic (releasing energy) but endothermic storing energy. Energy storage is a function of all the cell membranes in eukaryotes. The mitochondria matrix is filled with a colloid of hydrogen. This reaction creates a proton (H+) and sent to mitochondria where it can be recombined with oxygen to produce energy, Animals seem to use hydrogen as their version of a fuel cell. The cells can then use this energy to supplement the sugars the body provides them from the TCA cycle. Sugars are polymers of CO2, H2O, and sunlight. In this process, melanin acts like a catalyst which promotes the chemical reaction, but is not consumed by it. **It is akin to an enzyme of photosynthesis.**

Melanin appears to act as a "super chlorophyll" due to its quantized advantages over regular chlorophyll. Melanin and eumelanin are to the animal kingdom what chlorophyll is to the plant kingdom to make energy in a cell. This augments ATP and generates most of the energy that life needs to run. Melanin has hundreds of reaction centers compared to the single reaction center in chlorophyll, the ability to absorb energy from a far broader portion of the electromagnetic spectrum, and capability to function for years outside of human tissue. Chlorophyll is believed to become totally inactive after only twenty seconds.

The matrix is filled with increased levels of hydrogen in the mitochondria. It improves their function, and lower the production of free radicals while allowing calcium to flow in and out to create vibrations on the inner mitochondrial membrane. This links to Dr. Dough Wallace's research. This science could be very significant, because over time free radical production produces DNA deletions in the mitochondria that cannot be effectively repaired. This is what drives % heteroplasmy in aging. Unlike nuclear DNA, mitochondrial DNA is located in loops because of its bacterial lineage. Once these loops of DNA are damaged the efficiency of the mitochondria is reduced, more free radicals are produced, and a cascade of damage begins. Mitochondrial damage limits energy production in a cell.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-20T04:52:05+0200

[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0000457](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0000457)

Science is messy and therefore people with medical issues life is disheveled. People hammer out the details, usually in a disconnected fashion. Then finally someone synthesizes disparate information in a more palatable form, then you see it on Good Morning America and everybody is like, "we knew it all along." Consider the 1978 Nobel Prize as a great example of what ails us. It was a great piece of science. We use chemiosmosis and proton gradients to make charges and capacitor. But ATP is not what Mitchell said it was or what modern biology or medicine think it is. From the 1978 press release: "Cells of green plants as well as certain bacteria and algae can capture energy by means of chlorophyll directly from sunlight - the ultimate source of energy for all life on Earth - and utilize it, through photosynthesis, to convert carbon dioxide and water into organic compounds. Other cells, including those of all animals and many bacteria, are entirely dependent for their existence on organic compounds which they take up as nutrients from their environment. Through a process called cell respiration, these compounds are oxidized by atmospheric oxygen to carbon dioxide and water.

During both photosynthesis and respiration, energy is conserved in a compound called adenosine triphosphate, abbreviated as ATP. When ATP is split into adenosine diphosphate (ADP) and inorganic phosphate (Pi), a relatively large amount of energy is liberated, which can be utilized, in the presence of specific enzymes, to drive various energy-requiring processes. Thus, ATP may be regarded as the universal 'energy currency' of living cells." So what is my quantum beef? No one did the energy budget of a cell based upon Mitchell's ideas. The one major problem with the press release........no added up all the ATP made and the energy costs in a cell. If they did they would have noticed something shocking. Cellular energy cost exceeded ATP supply by a 500 fold margin back in the 1970's using that era's technology. Today in the 2010's, biophysicists have repeated those study was repeated and the deficit is now 3000 fold. Every day we go further from Peter Mitchell's Nobel Prize we are further from the truth because of that one wrong turn in biologic history. The next question should have been where does the energy come from then? Answer: It comes from sunlight and the interaction of light on our surfaces with cell water.

We have a photosynthetic path that uses melanin and porphyrins and hemoglobin in blood. No one will ask that question because we gave a dude a Noble prize without ever checking his energy math. To this day........no doctor knows this information because they cannot fathom Ling's work. Moreover, dissociation of the water molecule constitutes the very first reaction of photosynthesis in plants, and was unsuspected, even unthinkable in human beings, until we found it in human retina in 1990s. Plants and human beings have the same very first reaction as the origin of life. The impact in the field of molecular biology is huge; therefore the role of the water and glucose must be redefined, glucose is just a source of biomass, instead water is the real source of energy of the eukaryotic cell, and neuron cell is not an exception. The main source of energy of the CNS is the CSF and therefore the ventricles and subarachnoid space. Blood vessels are merely source of biomass. This is why heteroplasmy rates rise as we age. Research along these 23 years thought us that medical modulation of human photosynthesis has extraordinary therapeutic results in CNS´s diseases tied to mitochondrial dysfunction.

Melanin is a human chlorophyll; Melanin is to animal kingdom as chlorophyll is to plant kingdom. Both molecules have the amazing capacity to split the water molecule at room temperature. Secondly, melanin has the intrinsic property to split and reform the water molecule using **hydrogen peroxide**, and the reaction happens as follows:

2H2O ↔ 2H2 + O2 + 4e

Melanin has the astonishing capacity to split the water molecule at room temperature, for instance, in the laboratory, it is necessary to heat up water at 2000°C in order the reach the separation of hydrogen from the oxygen atoms. Melanin is a biogenic amine like dopamine, melatonin, serotonin, and histamine. Moreover, melanin supports the reunification of water molecule giving off liquid water and four high energy electrons. The reaction is not symmetrical in time or optically because, as any other chemical reaction, depends of the photochemistry of the micro-environment of cells.

Human photosynthesis and the eukaryotic cell breaks the ground of the cellular metabolism, the energy released symmetrically in all directions by melanin is a real source of photonic energy of the eukaryotic cell, and the neuron cell is not an exception. All melanocytes are from neuroectoderm. **There is a HUGE misconception in photosynthesis that the creation of glucose as is the main source of energy**. This bad idea comes from the observation that in plants glucose is the main product of energy absorption photosynthesis. The traditional diagram of photosynthesis must changes as follows:

6CO2 + 6H2O → C6H12O6 + 6O2 + Energy

I have to smile at Jack's response. Because I get it.... FINALLY. I am working towards resolving my estrogen dominance/ hormone issues, and it's been very tough for me to realize (I mean to really "get it" and not just "in theory" or "intellectually" but to have it truly resonate within) that... **it's not your diet.** Hormone issues aren't fixed with anything if your environment is bad.

Not to say there's not a place for discussing supplements for dealing with estrogen dump as a result of CT. But focusing on the right thing(s) so you won't have the hormone issues to begin with... that is where it's at. Get control of your circadian biology, regain leptin sensitivity, use CT to improve/ enhance all of your efforts, and realize we all live in an altered field that has a negative impact on our cell's signalling ability. Figure out what you personally need to do to overcome this.

I listened to a podcast this morning where Ameer Rosic interviewed some Dr. about hormone optimization...and that's when I knew I had changed. I listened to the guy talk about removing certain things from your diet as a starting point, then how to add other things in, and it was all tied to food's effect on hormones. I actually turned it off, and spent the rest of the drive in to work thinking about all the people who will follow this advice, and how an awful lot of them will still have problems. Yet, I'm not sure if I feel worse for the people it works for or the ones that it doesn't. If it doesn't work, at least those people still have a chance of discovering the truth. The other people will tell all their friends about the new diet they're on to fix their hormones...

### Joce_ — 2015-07-29T04:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-metabolism.16628/post-173372>

Thanks Jack for responding. I guess I'm not dumping enough estrogen. I'll try to continue with the DIM until my body is optimal. For newbies, supplements to provide the body of nutrients that have been depleted seems like a good way to reverse the effects.

### Shijin13 — 2015-07-29T16:43:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-173408>

Doing an ASI, plus a 6 day hormone and melatonin ASI. Also doing a Urine Hormone analysis, and getting labs drawn either tomorrow or Friday. My cycle has been crazy for the past 3 months.

I'm sure HCG and an altered circadian cycle are to blame... but I need to see what's going on. Running full labs ... even though I ran them in April... things are off... so I need a bench mark where I'm at I'm adding FSH and LH to look to see if what's going on may be related to entering perimenopause/menopause - (my mom hit menopause when I was 12 so she was 42-43 time frame which is where I'm at so this is a possibility)

Switched from Armour to cytomel about 8 wks ago...

All other Hormones are the same... 100mg progesterone troche, 1.1ml sublingual progesterone 50mg/ml and 1.2ml topical 200mg/ml progesterone gel

Supplements have been reduced to the following: Ubiquinol, 20g D-ribose, 4mg 5MTHF, 30Kmcg Molybedenum, 15mg Lithium orotate, 1500mg acytel L-carnitine, 200mg pregnenolone, and cinnamon bark extract.

Labs in December 2014 & my Labs in April 2015

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-02T01:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-173752>

so Last night Jack CALLED ME OUT on FB in the Quantum health group.

> "[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi) Gretchen: Be open and discerning using your intuition and realize that your intuition grows as you strengthen your mind. The mind is a function of the electrons in your senses. The way to strengthen your mind is giving it things it is made up of DHA and seafood. You have gotten that message a long time. Maybe this is the day that message hits home deeper. Every time you look at your phone or this keyboard with its blue light it destroys your DHA in your brain. This limits your ability to receive messages from anyone during a talk, a meeting, sex, or any connection................disconnect to reconnect via your senses."

So Jack - your earlier comment on this thread (FB QHG) about connecting.... has entanglement. Connecting was the message at church tonight.

Connection doesn't occur when we stand alone. It occurs when we build intimacy with the people around us. With the people we love and care about. With the people we work with. With our neighbors. By being in the moment.

I am guilty of not always being in the MOMENT, when I should be. Stress from job instability. Stress from Chaos and Havoc being kids and fighting (I never experienced that as an only child so this is foreign to me and I'm learning how to help them interact w/o the fighting). Stress from trying to be a working mom, a wife, a mother, and a woman. Stress from trying to fix all the Fucked up shit I did to myself in my career choices, in the people who I've let into my lives, when I shouldn't have. Stress from kicking them out. Stress from moving. Stress from going back to school. Keeping up with the maintenance on the house. Hell, trying to keep up with Jack and understand where he's taking me on this ride.

And last night John and I focused on connecting through dancing, kissing and loving.... I focused on being in the Moment for that small fraction of my day. and it was AMAZING.

This afternoon, instead of working on the house maintenance we stopped. We went to a carnival and invited a neighbor and their kids just to connect. When we got home, we continued on this theme and played clue with the kids.

Connect and stay in the moment you're in. Not 15 minutes from now. not 6 years ago. Not on the shit people have done, or haven't done. Just the moment.

Sometimes I need to be told stuff a thousand different ways for it to sink in to my think skull. Other times, it clicks on the first go round.

### nonchalant — 2015-08-02T03:48:12+0300

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-07T18:23:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174194>

So, I'm trying to get back into the swing of things, eating BAB in prep for the seasonal fall transition which is always a PITA and usually means migraine hell. but one thing that I've been challenged with is morning hunger for any breakfast, let alone the BAB.

I do not want food at all in the AM. Especially after I get up and do my morning CT, ground and am sunlight. I'm not hungry until 930-10 am at the earliest. I'm regularly up btwn 545-6am and I'm in the tank w/in 5min of waking.

I know that BAB timing is critical for circadian syncing... but For 2years now, I've had no desire to eat when I get up. before the LR - I used to be ravenous.... yet with AM CT, grounding, sunlight and 2mg of nicotine I have no desire for food. Its like my ideal eating window is from 10am - 7pm. and only 2 meals.. ( I really need to move the 7pm window back but sometimes that's a challenge but I'm working on it)

the interesting thing is If I eat w/in an hour I find I'm snacking on stuff all day....If I wait until I'm hungry and eat then I have no desire for food for the rest of the day. Now If I know I'm going into the office... I will force myself to eat... but then once in the office - the only food I want is chocolate and coffee - damn CA+ Efflux. Which is the suck. I get outside as much as possible, and try to hit the oysters every chance I get.

Intuition tells me that the CT, Grounding, and AM sunlight are driving the desire not to eat - getting my energy from the earth and the sun. Wondering if anyone else is experiencing similar things.

IIRC @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) has had similar experiences....

### fitness@home — 2015-08-07T19:19:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174200>

Another one who is not hungry in the early morning. I get up at 4 am and can easily wait until my 11 am lunch which equals a 17 hour fasting period (dinner at 6 pm the night before).

I can do BAB if it is weighted heavier on fats, with moderate protein (which defeats the purpose)

I get outside as much as possible, and try to hit the oysters every chance I get.
>

>
> IIRC @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) has had similar experiences....

I agree on the morning light, grounding and hunger. I usually wake an hour or two before sunrise and slam a bottle of water (yes, good water!). I'll either go back to bed for some lucid dreaming or hit the recommended reading list. I'm chalking my sleep pattern to the Biphasic Theory.

After a shower I hit the beach for a sunrise pic and standing in the water then off to work--this has been my turning point--I HAVE TO DO THIS!!  Usually around 745-8 at work I do BAB of 3-4 eggs and some sort of meat. Added potatoes because it's summer and I'm Irish but transitioning off that now with light changing.

Will usually eat 85% dark chocolate or nuts during the day along with a ton more water. I use nicotine like a mofo. Completely Wi-fi office. Fun stuff!! NOT!!

Quit coffee five days ago with no ill effects thanks to a post in the FB group. Was doing the coconut oil thing in it. I liked it, gave me energy. Just don't feel I need it now. Mini-biohack. Straight water ( and adult beverages in somewhat of moderation at appropriate times).

After work I like to hit the beach again and go swimming if schedule allows.

Weekends I like to ride the bicycle around in the sun. I find I rarely have a desire to eat.

Dinner is usually early-ish and seafood-ish.

Sleep by 10. Even my 15 year old son knows not to fuck with me after 10 now. Slowly getting him on the quantum train.

Light Water Magnetism and DHA baby! Only way to live for me now. I word vomit on anyone who'll listen.

That and sharing how I've addressed my limiting factors. Honesty with myself has been the biggest change. People are both horrified and impressed when I tell them what a pussy I had been in my life for the longest time. Gotta confront your past and let it go. I'm learning to temper my brutal honesty, but it's still honest. That's the other only way I'm living now. All the shit on the blogs, FB group and Forums is just that if you don't face yourself.

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-07T19:28:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174202>

So I'm not the only one Dawn... that's good to know... I've found I can't do the heavy fats in the AM any longer either... interesting thing... I just got the first set of my most recent rounds of labs back...

things are changing...

So, I think I'm just going to continue with my AM CT, Grounding, morning sun, and 2mg nicotine. and eat when hunger hits in the am. I am also tracking to keep accountable for what I put into my mouth...

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-07T19:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174203>

> [Joe Gavin said:](/goto/post?id=174201)
>
> I agree on the morning light, grounding and hunger. I usually wake an hour or two before sunrise and slam a bottle of water (yes, good water!). I'll either go back to bed for some lucid dreaming or hit the recommended reading list. I'm chalking my sleep pattern to the Biphasic Theory.
>
> After a shower I hit the beach for a sunrise pic and standing in the water then off to work--this has been my turning point--I HAVE TO DO THIS!!  Usually around 745-8 at work I do BAB of 3-4 eggs and some sort of meat. Added potatoes because it's summer and I'm Irish but transitioning off that now with light changing.
>
> Will usually eat 85% dark chocolate or nuts during the day along with a ton more water. I use nicotine like a mofo. Completely Wi-fi office. Fun stuff!! NOT!!
>
> Quit coffee five days ago with no ill effects thanks to a post in the FB group. Was doing the coconut oil thing in it. I liked it, gave me energy. Just don't feel I need it now. Mini-biohack. Straight water ( and adult beverages in somewhat of moderation at appropriate times).
>
> After work I like to hit the beach again and go swimming if schedule allows.
>
> Weekends I like to ride the bicycle around in the sun. I find I rarely have a desire to eat.
>
> Dinner is usually early-ish and seafood-ish.
>
> Sleep by 10. Even my 15 year old son knows not to fuck with me after 10 now. Slowly getting him on the quantum train.
>
> Light Water Magnetism and DHA baby! Only way to live for me now. I word vomit on anyone who'll listen.
>
> That and sharing how I've addressed my limiting factors. Honesty with myself has been the biggest change. People are both horrified and impressed when I tell them what a pussy I had been in my life for the longest time. Gotta confront your past and let it go. I'm learning to temper my brutal honesty, but it's still honest. That's the other only way I'm living now. All the shit on the blogs, FB group and Forums is just that if you don't face yourself.

Joe that's awesome... Love it. I've found even with the kids that on the days we wake them up and make them spend 30mins out side, their clocks immediately sync up the next day!

### Inger — 2015-08-08T13:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174238>

Well, I just eat in teh mornings anyways if hungry or not..lol I am like that, I can always eat

I now make me a big nice salad with ACV, and top it with lots of raw seafood... that is my breakfast out in the morning sun  I eat the same every day..lol
but it is so good I never get tired of it!

### Sue-UK — 2015-08-11T13:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174407>

I'm jealous of the talk of morning sun  our summer is so bad I yesterday resorted to nude rain bathing . I just decided that the plants are still growing, the fruit is still ripening ..... If there is enough natural light for photosynthesis, there should be enough environmental information up for grabs for me ..... and wouldn't a.m. rainwater contain photons....? then there is the effects of a.m. rain splashing on the skin surface ..... Then that has me thinking .....I often have a cool (not cold) shower late in the evening in summer to get my skin surfaces cooler before sleep ..... Evening rain ......?

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=173930)
>
> my whole life I've had de ja vous experiences. only recently have I come to realize its tied to DHA and the tensgerity structure w/in my cells/mitochondria that allows me to pick up the oscillations/frequency/vibrations of what's coming down the pike... I've used this in my "Day" job for years... to see what needed to happen next. As I read Jack's blogs... I know where he's going next... its crazy how often that happens...

Have you read "Physics in Mind"? In it he describes how the brain might act as a quantum supercomputer. (I must relisten to the quantum brain webinar too .... ) Perhaps you are supercomputing at work, and the ability is expanding as you gain more information from the blogs. I've had flashes of supercomputing, but it isn't consistent ( which is probably a reflection I should examine ) Lyall Watson in the book Supernature probably describes my less than optimal attempts (I do sometimes know where Jack's going next). People on a moving train are at the same moment in time .... Three people sitting in the same carriage, one has his window blind partly drawn so sees less than the one who has a full view out of the window. The third looks out at a different angle. Someone sat in a carriage nearer the front sees the change of view out of the window before the others. At that moment they all have different information. The passenger sat illegally on the roof has a much wider perspective, but isn't forseeing the future (the line/blog ahead), its more like a better view of the present, using the senses more fully. I think maybe I need to spend more time on construction/maintenance of my quantum brain, than telling myself to get up on the \*\*\*\*\*\*\* roof

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-11T18:14:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174419>

I'll add that book to my reading list...

Sadly the sun is beginning to depart us. mornings now, the sunrise is later, and in the evenings the sunset is earlier. I'm beginning to already miss its brilliance at 6am... Today I was up at 530 (no alarm clock) and the sun was not out, and it was grey and dreary after the hour of sunrise... I broke up the 660nm UFO lamp to wake us all up... I foresee that becoming a regular event on a daily occurrence.

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-11T18:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174421>

I foresee that becoming a regular event on a daily occurrence.

definitely bumming about the light...still went to the beach in the rain however for some grounding and a crappy picture, at least according to my cousin!

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-11T18:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174425>

so earlier today on FB @[endless](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/160/) posted this thread
[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...urself with stagnant energy and expect growth](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/search/?query=%20You%20cannot%20surround%20yourself%20with%20stagnant%20energy%20and%20expect%20growth)

Its pretty epic. I have a comment that I cannot share on FB, but I will share here.

I have an emotional vampire in my life. It is my MIL. The woman hates me and up until this year I've done everything to be the "good" daughter in law, for the sake of John and the kids. Finally this year I laid it down to my John as MIL was pushing us to go on vacation with her, which makes me fucking miserable. I end up walking on eggshells the entire time. my parenting choices are always questioned. I told John that I wouldn't spend my precious vacation with my family being miserable and that if we wanted a family vacation it would be the 4 of us, other wise I was going to skip the family vacation and do my own thing and he'd have to explain to the kids why mommy won't go on vacation with grandma.

Last year on vacation I was ridiculed at the dinner table infont of my children by my MIL. I got up and in the middle of diner I said, "I QUIT" I can't fucking do anything right, and I'm done trying. I'm done. I am not part of "this" family. and I walked out. as I was leaving John started yelling at his mom for being a bully and a bitch to me and that she had no right to do that to his wife and the mother of his children.

I spent the remainder of our vacation miserable, b/c she was around. This year I made a determination that I will not waste my time, effort or energy dealing with this woman. I do not engage her at all when she's visiting the kids. I will not go to her house. I WILL NOT SUBJECT MYSELF TO HER DEGRADATION. SHE HAS NOTHING TO OFFER ME, and I WANT NOTHING FROM HER.

The interesting thing, is as I watch my kids grow up, I see her deliberately placing guilt on my kids, for not wanting to spend time with her. Havoc realizes that she doesn't genuinely want to spend time with her and chooses not to engage her. He's actually ask John when can he meet his dad ( long story there for another time). When MIL found out that we were going to NOLA she got pissed b/c she thought we were going to NOLA to have the kids meet John's dad. She cornered me one afternoon at a family event and told me how hurt she was, that we didnt' tell her and I needed to communicate with her more, so we could spend more time together. I told her, there was no reason for either of us to spend time together, as it wasn't good for either of us, and that it was her son's responsibility to communicate with her, not mine. I don't expect John to communicate with my parents, that's my job. She got mad and told me that there's a communication problem, and I told her then she needs to fix it with HER SON, and that the reason there's a communication problem is b/c she doesn't respect our decisions as a family.

After Havoc was born, she decided that she needed a role in making decisions in our family and she and I got in an all out arguement with the kids in the room, and I told her POINT BLANK, " YOU ARE NOT PART OF THE DECISION MAKING OF THIS FAMILY. THAT IS ME AND JOHN. YOU DON'T GET A SAY. YOU DON'T HAVE TO LIKE HOW WE DO THING, BUT YOU HAVE TO RESPECT IT, AND IF YOU DON'T GET OUT."

Out of love for John, I backed down on this 5 years ago. I never should have done that. I understand this vampire is in my life, but I do not engage it. John always comments how he hates how his mom always leverages the "Catholic" guilt against him and that as her son, he has a duty to be a part of her life. He dreads talking to his mom. Every weekend he puts off talking to her, he sends her calls to voice mail, he hates how she ignores him when he tells her not to buy stuff for the kids, or gives us stuff, that she thinks we need. She gives him crap every weekend about not going to a Catholic Church. His guilt over being her only child weighs upon him, and I wish I could help him eliminate that. He hates how that he's the only person in her life that she can rely upon, and how she expects that he'll drop everything for her.

Jack is 100% right in that we must excise those who hold us back from the A. Its easy to drop friends b/c they're not "family". I've always said in my career, that I'll do what it takes to get things done, if my superiors aren't happy, oh well, they don't have the power to hurt me like family does. Family will pull all the tricks in the book keeping you from achieving who you are. Granted there are some who truly love and support you for who you are, but there are those that don't. I watched my mom come from a similar guilt ridden family and it took me telling her at 27 in a therapist office that if she wanted to have a good relationship with me, she needed to cut the baggage she had with her mom, and that until that happened we'd never have the mother - daughter relationship she wanted with me.

people who love you, respect you, and support you will cheer you on, let you fail, and let y ou get back up on your own, to achieve greatness. They will get out of the way, when they're holding you back. They don't stand in your way, or try to impede progress as you become the most amazing version of who you can be. Anyone who tries in my life, is not given the time or day. Fuck them and the high and might horse they road in on.

@[Joe Gavin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/15415/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T19:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174427>

Never rearrange your life for people in order to meet mediocrity. If they couldn’t see your worth at the moment you met then they never will. Let their thoughts be filled with their regrets and your life filled with thankfulness because of this revelation.

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-11T19:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174429>

> Never rearrange your life for people in order to meet mediocrity. If they couldn’t see your worth at the moment you met then they never will. Let their thoughts be filled with their regrets and your life filled with thankfulness because of this revelation.

Hell yeah Jack. Hell yeah... Sometimes we're so focused on acceptance that we forget to to live our lives by our standards, instead of others...

I've always said in my career, that I'll do what it takes to get things done, if my superiors aren't happy, oh well, they don't have the power to hurt me like family does. Family will pull all the tricks in the book keeping you from achieving who you are. Granted there are some who truly love and support you for who you are, but there are those that don't. I watched my mom come from a similar guilt ridden family and it took me telling her at 27 in a therapist office that if she wanted to have a good relationship with me, she needed to cut the baggage she had with her mom, and that until that happened we'd never have the mother - daughter relationship she wanted with me.
>
> people who love you, respect you, and support you will cheer you on, let you fail, and let y ou get back up on your own, to achieve greatness. They will get out of the way, when they're holding you back. They don't stand in your way, or try to impede progress as you become the most amazing version of who you can be. Anyone who tries in my life, is not given the time or day. Fuck them and the high and might horse they road in on.
>
> @[Joe Gavin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/15415/)

GOOD FOR YOU!!!!!! It is hard. My MIL used to HATE me for "ruining" her son's life by getting pregnant to young.....uhm excuse me, he DID take part in the whole conception!!!!! We kept our distance a bit and built our family around us. Eventually she saw the light and I was the best DIL or at least compared to my SIL who is not bad.

I have no relationship with my father and oh well.

Then there is my mother. She thrives on conflict (hence why she does foster care) and I just cannot handle the drama so I distance my self from her too. Oh, I still love her and talk to her once in a while and she only live 20 minutes away. Sometimes I feel a bit guilty about it but I need to protect my emotional health and not worry about hers. I am just so thankful that the DH and I are on the same page with pretty much everything. In your case, it is great that John does speak up for you. Him being an only child, that's a tough one.

We kept our distance a bit and built our family around us. Eventually she saw the light and I was the best DIL or at least compared to my SIL who is not bad.
>
> I have no relationship with my father and oh well.
>
> Then there is my mother. She thrives on conflict (hence why she does foster care) and I just cannot handle the drama so I distance my self from her too. Oh, I still love her and talk to her once in a while and she only live 20 minutes away. Sometimes I feel a bit guilty about it but I need to protect my emotional health and not worry about hers. I am just so thankful that the DH and I are on the same page with pretty much everything. In your case, it is great that John does speak up for you. Him being an only child, that's a tough one.

My parents are awesome and drive me nuts at the same time, as the result of being an only. Its only been in the past 15years that my mom and I can say we actually like each other. My Dad on the other hand still thinks of me as his little girl, and sometimes wants me to do things differently. When this happens I remind him its my life to mess up. I've gotta do things, and sometimes that means I FAIL. Every FAILURE has brought me to where I am today.

The one great thing is this entire Journey of health has strenghtened my relationship with John. We're actively trying to help each other build up our weakness, and continue to cultivate our strengths...

### caroline — 2015-08-12T00:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174460>

All that turmoil and heartache impacts your health in so many viscious ways....and the kiddos are affected too. Not good for them to see you abused in so many ways.

I put up with abuse from my mother for years and years and then my daughters took over her role I guess. JK made me realise that I don't deserve that shit ...and that was a huge watershed moment for me.

Now, a year later, I have a very loving, kind, smart and may I just say - very sexy partner who treats me like gold. Tony has three adult sons who also treat me with love and respect and all his friends as well. I have never been part of that before.

Jack always tells us ....we have to be good enuf for ourselves first ......

You are a very special woman Gretchen ..... lets rock the world!

### caroline — 2015-08-12T01:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174462>

one more thought that John might relate too.....

We had a close friend - a Catholic priest ....I am Catholic too so know about all the guilt!

I finally decided to have the conversation with him about my abusive mother ...

His word were simple and direct -

Your parents don't automatically deserve your respect ....they have to earn it. How liberating is that???

My Dad on the other hand still thinks of me as his little girl, and sometimes wants me to do things differently. When this happens I remind him its my life to mess up. I've gotta do things, and sometimes that means I FAIL. Every FAILURE has brought me to where I am today.

Letting a child fail is a hard concept to grasp. My hubby had a hard time understanding that when our daughter was a senior in high school. They fought all the time since he thought she needed to learn life lessons. My thought was if we have not taught her enough stuff by now, you yelling at her will not get her to learn. We need to allow her to fail and be there to help pick up the pieces if she needs it.

Now we have a very successful, strong, independent beautiful young woman who was able to move all the way across the country with her boyfriend to have a life of their own. I don't think we did too bad for being very young parents and I having came from a divorces dysfunctional family.

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-12T22:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174511>

The last several weeks during work I've been hitting a mid-day slump, especially when I'm in my EMF wifi Ikea library office from hell. While my labs are starting to show some AWESOME Improvements, I'm still struggling with dehydration at the cellular level. so instead of shoving as much chocolate in my mouth due to CA+ efflux... today I took my ass outside ate crab salad for lunch and laid down on the ground and watched the clouds and threw the ball to Trooper. I also popped 2mg of nicotine to see how that would impact the chocolate/coffee urges.

I fell better... just from being outside. adding in the nicotine and the desire for both chocolate and coffee are gone.

now to figure out to get the Water I'm drinking into my cells....

### Shijin13 — 2015-08-14T20:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-blog-my-journal-yourpaleohealthcare-com-journey-to-optimal.16630/post-174688>

Lust (or a mind fuck)

I see him from across the room as I
enter the pub, that dark handsome fellow
brooding at the bar. Oh, he exudes sexuality.
I want him; I must have him tonight. I desire
to see him looking at my naked body
with approval in his eyes. To feel him tease
me and taste my skin with his fingers, tongue
and lips. to ravish me with all abandon.
I can feel my tongue caress a wet path from
his lips and ears straight down his chest
chiseled in my mind. His proof of wanting
me stand rigid hard with his desire. To feel
his tongue delve and taste my sweet nectar bringing
me to an ecstatic height of pleasure. His tentative
entry lured deeper by my moistness, his pace
quickens as I cum again getting wetter, he slides
right in for his final plunge cuming with in me.
OH, I float down only instances after laying eyes on him.
He'll never know what an incredible mind fuck he was!
Uh, lust.

written in april of '99 at the Jet. \]
